Skip verb validation for type=decision titles in add and update
Decision titles skip verb validation since they are statements not actions
Decision titles must not start with 'Record that' -- state the decision directly
Add --all, --sort, and multi-project support to decision list
Test events-authoritative create
Skip verb validation for type=decision titles in add and update
Test events-authoritative create

fields.description: Added --all flag to show decisions across all projects (adds Project column), --sort flag (id/created/title, default id DESC), and multi-project support to list_decisions() in task_cmd.py and decision list CLI command. Default sort changed from created_at DESC to id DESC for stable ordering. The --all flag follows the same pattern as task list --all.

payload.title: Add Bash file-write detection to PreToolUse enforcement
payload.description: PreToolUse currently gates Edit/Write/NotebookEdit only. Bash commands like cat > file, tee, sed -i, gh pr edit, mv, cp, rm bypass all task/drift enforcement. Need a Bash-input parser that recognizes file-mutating commands and routes them through the same enforcement path (active-task check, drift check). Out of scope of E-915; tracked here as a follow-up so the gap is not lost.

payload.title: Implement universal --filter flag for all list commands
payload.description: Replace per-command --status/--phase/--tier flags with a universal --filter that supports: implicit field detection from unique values (status/phase/tier), AND via comma, OR via multiple --filter flags, no-<field> for null/empty checks, -<value> for negative matches, has-<field> for non-empty checks, key=value for non-enum fields. Keep existing flags working but mark deprecated. Build parse_filter() and filters_to_sql() shared functions. Enforce value uniqueness across fields at module load.

fields.text:
# Analysis: Universal Filtering for List Commands

## Context

Currently each list command has its own set of `--status`, `--phase`, `--tier`, `--parent` flags with ad-hoc WHERE clause building. There's no way to filter for empty/null fields (e.g. "tasks with no description") or negative matches (e.g. "not auto tier"). We need a filtering approach that handles these cases and scales as fields grow.

## Approach A: Individual Flags (`--no-text`, `--no-description`, `--has-text`)

Add per-field boolean flags for null/empty checks.

**Pros:**
- Discoverable via `--help`
- Simple to implement per-command
- Type-safe, validated by Click
- Familiar CLI pattern

**Cons:**
- Flag explosion: each nullable field needs `--no-X` and `--has-X` (2 flags per field)
- No negative matching for values (can't say "not auto")
- No OR logic between filter groups
- Every new field requires adding flags to every command
- Already have 8+ flags on `task list`; adding more makes `--help` overwhelming

## Approach B: `--empty <field>` / `--nonempty <field>`

A pair of generic flags that accept field names.

**Pros:**
- Two flags cover all nullable fields
- Moderately discoverable
- Can be repeated: `--empty text --empty description`

**Cons:**
- Still no negative value matching (`--status` can include but not exclude)
- No OR logic
- Need to maintain a list of valid field names and map them to SQL columns
- Doesn't reduce the existing flag count

## Approach C: Universal `--filter` with Implicit Field Detection (Mike's Proposal)

Syntax: `--filter needs_plan,quick,no-description --filter ready,-auto`

Rules:
- Values within a `--filter` are ANDed
- Multiple `--filter` flags are ORed
- Known unique values (status/phase/tier) auto-detect their field
- `no-<field>` means `IFNULL(<field>,'') = ''`
- `-<value>` prefix means `<> value` (negative match)

**Pros:**
- Compact: complex queries in a single line
- AND + OR logic without nested parens
- Scales without flag explosion; new fields just need new recognized values
- `no-` prefix handles the empty/null case generically
- `-` prefix handles negative matching for any recognized value
- Feels natural once learned: "needs_plan and quick" reads left-to-right

**Cons:**
- Less discoverable than named flags (need docs or `--help` examples)
- Ambiguity risk: if two fields share a value name, auto-detection breaks. Currently safe: status/phase/tier values are all unique, but must stay that way.
- `no-` prefix could collide if a field name starts with a recognized value (unlikely but worth noting)
- Error messages for typos need care ("foobar" is not a known value -- of what field?)
- Harder to validate at parse time vs named flags

**Collision analysis for current values:**
- Status: needs_plan, ready, in_progress, verify, confirmed, assumed, blocked, revisit, declined, obsolete
- Phase: now, next, later
- Tier: auto, quick, deep, discuss, none
- All unique across fields. Safe today.

## Approach D: Key-Value Filter (`--filter status=needs_plan,tier!=auto`)

Explicit `field=value` and `field!=value` syntax.

**Pros:**
- Unambiguous: no auto-detection needed
- Familiar from tools like `kubectl get pods --field-selector status.phase=Running`
- Easy to extend with operators (`=`, `!=`, `empty`, `notempty`)
- No collision risk

**Cons:**
- Verbose: `--filter status=needs_plan,tier=quick` vs just `needs_plan,quick`
- Users must know field names
- AND/OR still needs a convention (multiple `--filter` for OR?)

## Approach E: Hybrid -- Keep Existing Flags + Add `--filter` for Advanced Use

Keep `--status`, `--phase`, `--tier`, `--parent` as they are. Add `--filter` (Approach C) for:
- Negative matches (`-auto`)
- Empty/null checks (`no-description`, `no-text`)
- OR logic (multiple `--filter` flags)

Named flags and `--filter` would be ANDed together.

**Pros:**
- Backwards compatible: existing usage unchanged
- Simple cases stay simple (`--status ready`)
- Advanced cases are possible (`--filter no-text,quick --filter no-description,deep`)
- Gradual migration: can deprecate individual flags later if `--filter` proves sufficient

**Cons:**
- Two ways to do the same thing (e.g. `--status ready` vs `--filter ready`)
- Interaction rules need documentation (named flags AND `--filter` results)

## Recommendation

**Approach C (implicit field detection)** is the strongest design for where Endless is heading:

1. It's the most expressive with the least syntax
2. The value uniqueness constraint is easy to maintain (and can be enforced with a startup check)
3. AND/OR via comma vs multiple flags is intuitive
4. `no-` and `-` prefixes handle the two missing capabilities cleanly
5. It replaces rather than supplements the existing flags, keeping the interface clean

Implementation path:
1. Build a `parse_filter()` function that maps values to (field, op, value) tuples
2. Build a `filters_to_sql()` function that generates WHERE clause + params
3. Add `--filter` to all list commands, wired through the shared function
4. Keep existing flags working for now; mark as deprecated in help text
5. Enforce value uniqueness across status/phase/tier at module load time

For fields that aren't enumerated values (like `description`, `text`, `prompt`, `parent`):
- `no-<field>`: IFNULL(field, '') = ''
- `has-<field>`: field IS NOT NULL AND field != ''
- `parent=E-799`: explicit key=value for non-enum fields

This gives a clean split: bare values for enums, `no-`/`has-` for null checks, `key=value` for specific field matches.

## Rejected: `|` for OR Within a Single `--filter`

Considered adding `|` as an OR operator within a filter (e.g. `--filter needs_plan|ready,deep`). Rejected because:
1. `|` is the shell pipe character; forgetting quotes silently breaks commands
2. Precedence with `,` (AND) is ambiguous and will confuse users
3. Multiple `--filter` flags already handle OR between groups
4. Same-field OR happens naturally: `--filter needs_plan,ready` where both are statuses collapses to `status IN (needs_plan, ready)` since multiple values for the same field become an IN clause

## Same-Field Collapsing Rule

When multiple values within a single `--filter` resolve to the same field:
- Positive matches collapse to IN: `needs_plan,ready` -> `status IN ('needs_plan','ready')`
- Negative matches each become AND: `-needs_plan,-ready` -> `status != 'needs_plan' AND status != 'ready'`
- Mixed positive+negative on same field is an error

## Verification

1. `endless task list --filter needs_plan,quick` -- AND within filter
2. `endless task list --filter needs_plan --filter ready` -- OR across filters
3. `endless task list --filter no-description` -- null/empty field
4. `endless task list --filter -auto` -- negative match
5. `endless task list --filter has-text,now` -- non-empty field + phase
6. `endless task list --status ready --filter no-text` -- mixed with existing flags
7. Error on unknown value: `endless task list --filter foobar`

payload.title: Universal --filter with implicit field detection chosen over per-field flags, --empty/--nonempty, or key=value syntax for list command filtering
payload.description: Approach C: bare values auto-detect field from unique status/phase/tier values, comma=AND within filter, multiple --filter=OR, no-<field> for null/empty, -<value> for negation, has-<field> for non-empty, key=value for non-enum fields. Same-field positive values collapse to IN clause. Existing flags kept but deprecated. Value uniqueness enforced at module load.

payload.title: Pipe character | rejected as OR operator in --filter syntax due to shell escaping hazard and precedence ambiguity
payload.description: Users would need to quote every filter using |, and forgetting quotes silently pipes to another command. Precedence with comma-as-AND is also confusing. Multiple --filter flags handle OR between groups, and same-field values naturally collapse to IN clauses.

payload.title: CLI commands use singular nouns for command groups
payload.description: The group name is a namespace (resource type), not a collection. 'task show 42' reads as 'in the task domain, show 42.' Matches established CLI conventions (git branch, docker container, kubectl pod). Shorter to type. Provides a consistent mental model across all commands. The CLI is already 95% singular.

payload.title: Rename 'notes' and 'docs' commands to singular 'note' and 'doc' for consistency
payload.description: Per decision E-944: CLI commands use singular nouns. The two remaining plural top-level commands (notes, docs) should be renamed to singular form.

payload.title: Rename --description to --rationale on 'decision add' command
payload.description: A decision IS its rationale. 'Why did we decide this?' is the only useful context to record. --rationale is more semantically accurate than --description for decisions.

payload.title: Endless CLI should match what AI agents reach for intuitively
payload.description: When an AI agent uses an Endless command incorrectly, that is a signal the command structure needs improvement. The CLI should be natural to use without learning -- what agents reach for 'intuitively' is the right API shape. Incorrect usage is a design bug, not a user error.

fields.description: Done in this session as part of tracer-bullet pairing with E-918. Implemented: task_files table (schema + Python and Go migrations); RegisterTaskFile + IsFileInTaskScope + GetTaskTitle in internal/monitor/files.go; PostToolUse extension in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go to populate task_files on every Edit/Write/NotebookEdit; PreToolUse drift check that calls IsFileInTaskScope and blocks with switch/sub-task/touch options plus SUGGESTION banner invitation; gated on IsCheckEnabled('drift_detection') from internal/monitor/checks.go which defaults OFF until tested. NOT YET DONE: 'endless task touch <id> --add-file <path>' CLI for explicitly extending task scope (mentioned in original description, deferred). Verification: enable drift_detection in .endless/config.json checks map, attempt to edit a file outside the active task's scope, expect block with suggestion banner request.

Done in this session as tracer-bullet paired with E-917. Implemented: suggestions table (schema + Python and Go migrations) with FK suggestions.task_id (nullable; NULL=open, set=accepted); RecordSuggestion / ListOpenSuggestions / ListAllSuggestions / GetSuggestion / AcceptSuggestion / CountOpenSuggestions in internal/monitor/suggestions.go; ScanRecentSuggestions which scans recent assistant messages for **SUGGESTION (source):** banners and persists matches; UserPromptSubmit hook calls ScanRecentSuggestions after ParseTranscript; SessionStart context injection appends open-suggestion count when n>0; CLI 'endless suggestions {list,show,accept}' in src/endless/suggestions_cmd.py and registered in src/endless/cli.py. NOT YET DONE: tmux alert integration on new suggestion (mentioned in original; deferred to follow-up). Verification: end-to-end round-trip tested (insert → list → show → accept creates a real task → list excludes it → list --all shows arrow to accepted task). Test artifact E-948 may be cleaned up.
Tracer-bullet test artifact from E-918 verification. Not real work; safe to delete.

payload.title: Refactor Endless config loading onto go-cfgstore

fields.text:
# Plan: Refactor Endless Config Loading onto go-cfgstore

## Context

While testing E-917 (drift detection), Mike set `"checks": {"drift_detection": true}` in `~/.config/endless/config.json` (the global config). The check defaulted to OFF and the feature appeared dead because my code in `internal/monitor/checks.go` reads only `<project>/.endless/config.json` (per-project). Time was wasted debugging a layering mismatch the codebase should never have allowed.

The deeper problem: I copied the existing precedent set by `monitor.GetTrackingMode` at `internal/monitor/session.go:305`. Both functions reimplement config layering ad hoc with raw `os.ReadFile` + `json.Unmarshal`, ignoring Mike's house package `go-cfgstore` which exists for exactly this purpose, plus `go-doterr` for errors and `go-dt` for paths. So this task replaces *both* bespoke readers, not just the new one.

The proximate win: setting any check or tracking value in either layer works with predictable precedence. The structural win: Endless config moves onto house standards, future config additions follow one pattern, and the merge semantics for every field are explicitly documented instead of accidentally inferred from whichever reader you happen to be looking at.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- New `internal/config/` package using `go-cfgstore`, `go-doterr`, `go-dt`.
- Refactor `monitor.GetTrackingMode` and `monitor.IsCheckEnabled` to use it.
- Field-by-field merge semantics design and documentation (the load-bearing design work).
- End-to-end test that the original `drift_detection` bug is fixed and per-project override still works.

**Out of scope (separate follow-up tasks, not blockers):**
- **Python config readers.** `src/endless/config.py`, `event_bridge.py`, `register.py`, `reconcile.py`, plus several tests, all read the same JSON files directly. Python cannot use `go-cfgstore`. They keep working in this task but will diverge from Go semantics until a follow-up either reimplements matching semantics in Python or moves Python to call a Go shim. Documented as a known gap; file follow-up task.
- **`ConfigDir()` helpers** in `internal/monitor/db.go:20`, `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:20`, `cmd/endless-channel/main.go:25`. These resolve `~/.config/endless/` for log/db file locations, not for reading user settings. Out of scope here; can migrate to `go-cfgstore`'s `CLIConfigDir()` later.
- **Schema versioning** (`RootConfigV1`) per `go-configuration.md` guidance. Not needed until the schema actually changes.

## Field Inventory and Proposed Merge Semantics

The real design work. Mike flagged that every property needs explicit merge rules. Below is the proposal; the open questions list which calls need confirmation before implementation.

### Global-only fields (`~/.config/endless/config.json`)
No per-project analog; project layer cannot define these.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `roots` | `[]string` | Directories Endless scans for projects. Inherently cross-project. |
| `scan_interval` | `int` | Seconds. Cross-project scheduler setting. |
| `ignore` | `[]string` | Paths/projects to skip during scan. Cross-project. |
| `ownership` | `map[string][]string` | GitHub org/user patterns for ownership detection. Cross-project. |
| `node_id` | `string` | Unique identifier for this machine in event-sourced sync. Per-machine. |

### Per-project-only fields (`<proj>/.endless/config.json`)
No global analog; global layer cannot define these.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | `string` | Project identifier. |
| `label` | `string` | Display name. |
| `description` | `string` | |
| `language` | `string` | Primary language. |
| `status` | `string` | active, paused, archived, idea. |
| `dependencies` | `[]string` | Other Endless projects this one depends on. |
| `documents` | `object` | Sub-keys like `rules: []`. |

### Layered fields (project overrides global where present)

| Field | Type | Merge rule |
|---|---|---|
| `tracking` | `"enforce" \| "track" \| "off"` | Today: project-only. Proposed: allow global default; project overrides only if explicitly set. Lets you say "default all my projects to enforce" once. |
| `checks` | `map[string]bool` | **Per-key merge.** Project keys override only those keys; unmentioned keys inherit from global. This is the case Mike's bug exposed. |

### Open design questions (need Mike's call before coding)

1. **`checks` merge granularity.** Per-key (above) versus whole-map replacement. Per-key fits the use case but is more code. I lean per-key.
2. **`tracking` layerability.** Allow global default? Today it is project-only. Adding global is a real ergonomic win; the cost is one more cell in the merge matrix.
3. **Anything I missed?** This inventory came from listing both files' current contents. If there is a field you intend to add that should be layerable, easier to design it in now than to graft on later.

## Recommended Approach

### Phase 1: Bootstrap (no behavior change yet)
- Add `github.com/mikeschinkel/go-cfgstore` (and `go-doterr`, `go-dt` if not already pulled in transitively) to `go.mod`.
- Create `internal/config/` with: `config.go` (the `EndlessConfig` struct + `RootConfig()` marker), `merge.go` (per-field `Merge()`), `normalize.go` (`Normalize()` with defaults), `errors.go` (`doterr` sentinels), `load.go` (`Load(projectPath)` entrypoint wrapping `cfgstore.LoadDefaultConfig[EndlessConfig, *EndlessConfig]`).
- Define the `EndlessConfig` struct with EVERY current field from both files, JSON-tagged exactly as today, so existing config files keep loading byte-identical.

### Phase 2: Switch the two existing readers
- Refactor `monitor.GetTrackingMode(projectID)` to call `config.Load(projectPath)` and read `cfg.Tracking`. Delete the bespoke reader.
- Refactor `monitor.IsCheckEnabled(projectID, name)` to call `config.Load(projectPath)` and read `cfg.Checks[name]`, falling back to the per-check default. Delete `internal/monitor/checks.go`'s bespoke reader.
- `just build`. Both call sites compile and behave identically for the unchanged-config case.

### Phase 3: Verify the original bug
- Reproduce the bug against the OLD binary first (capture as confirmation): global-only `drift_detection: true`, attempt out-of-scope edit, edit succeeds when it should block.
- Run the same scenario against the NEW binary: edit blocks. This is the acceptance test for the refactor.
- Add the inverse test: project sets `drift_detection: false`, global sets `true`. Project wins, no block.
- Re-run the end-to-end hook test from the original E-917 verification.

### Phase 4: Document the Python contract
- Add `internal/config/README.md` (short) listing every field, its layer rules, and its merge rule, in plain English. The Python follow-up task uses this as the spec to match.

## Reuse, Do Not Rebuild

- `cfgstore.LoadDefaultConfig[RC, PRC]` — canonical entrypoint for CLI + Project layered loading with project precedence. Sits in `~/Projects/go-pkgs/go-cfgstore/load_config.go`.
- `RootConfig` interface (`RootConfig() / Normalize() / Merge()`) — standard pattern; Mike's pattern docs at `~/.claude/skills/go-config-file-author/references/go-configuration.md`.
- `go-doterr` for ALL error returns from the new package (no `fmt.Errorf`).
- `go-dt` for any path values stored on the struct or passed across boundaries.
- `defaultCheckEnabled(name)` already exists in `internal/monitor/checks.go`; move it to `internal/config/normalize.go` rather than rewriting.

## Critical Files

| Purpose | Path |
|---|---|
| Bespoke reader to delete (tracking) | `internal/monitor/session.go:305` (`GetTrackingMode`) |
| Bespoke reader to delete (checks) | `internal/monitor/checks.go` (entire file) |
| Hook callsites that consume these | `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:296`, `:319` |
| ConfigDir helper (kept, unrelated) | `internal/monitor/db.go:20` |
| Global config on disk (untouched) | `~/.config/endless/config.json` |
| Per-project config on disk (untouched) | `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/config.json` |
| go-cfgstore source | `~/Projects/go-pkgs/go-cfgstore/` |
| House skill reference | `~/.claude/skills/go-config-file-author/references/go-configuration.md` |
| House skill non-negotiables | `~/.claude/skills/go-config-file-author/references/go-non-negotiables.md` |

## Verification

End-to-end:
1. **Bug fix.** Global-only `drift_detection: true`, no per-project setting. Old binary: edit succeeds incorrectly. New binary: edit blocks. (Capture both runs as evidence.)
2. **Project override.** Global `true`, per-project `false`. Project wins, edit succeeds. Toggle per-project to `true`, edit blocks.
3. **No regression in existing fields.** `endless task list`, `endless task start <id>`, `endless suggestions list` behave identically. `endless scan` (or whatever uses `roots`) still finds projects.
4. **`tracking` still respected.** With per-project `tracking: off`, no enforcement. Re-enable, enforcement returns.
5. **Build green.** `just build`, all four binaries.

## Risks and Their Mitigation

- **Python drift.** Until the Python follow-up lands, Python and Go could disagree about merged values for any layered field. Mitigation: this task's only layered fields are `tracking` and `checks`, and Python doesn't read either of them today (only Go does). So at refactor time the gap is theoretical, not active.
- **Other Go config readers I haven't found.** The exploration found 5 Go callsites; only 2 read content (`GetTrackingMode`, `IsCheckEnabled`). The other 3 are `ConfigDir()` resolvers (out of scope). If a callsite is missed, build will catch any compile breakage; runtime behavior is unchanged for callers that don't go through the new package.
- **`go-cfgstore` is beta** (per its README). The dependency could shift. Acceptable; Mike maintains it and the package's API tier 1 is stable enough for our use.

## Endless Tasks to Create (after ExitPlanMode)

Parent (type=plan):
- "Refactor Endless config loading onto go-cfgstore"

Children (type=task):
1. "Add internal/config package with EndlessConfig schema and per-field merge"
2. "Replace bespoke GetTrackingMode reader with config.Load()"
3. "Replace bespoke IsCheckEnabled reader with config.Load()"
4. "Document Python config merge contract in internal/config/README.md"

Separate follow-up parent (NOT child of this plan):
- "Migrate Python config readers to honor Go config merge semantics"

Implementation order: 1 → 2 → 3 in one wave (they are tightly coupled), then 4 standalone.

payload.title: Add internal/config package with EndlessConfig schema and per-field merge
payload.description: New internal/config package using github.com/mikeschinkel/go-cfgstore. Files: config.go (EndlessConfig struct + RootConfig() marker), merge.go (per-field Merge() with semantics from the plan: per-key checks merge, layerable tracking, etc.), normalize.go (defaults; move defaultCheckEnabled from internal/monitor/checks.go here), errors.go (doterr sentinels), load.go (Load(projectPath) wrapping cfgstore.LoadDefaultConfig[EndlessConfig, *EndlessConfig] with ConfigSlug=endless, ConfigFile=config.json). Struct must include EVERY current field from both ~/.config/endless/config.json (roots, scan_interval, ignore, ownership, node_id, checks) and per-project .endless/config.json (name, label, description, language, status, dependencies, documents, tracking) with JSON tags identical to today so existing files keep loading. Use go-doterr for all errors and go-dt for paths.

payload.title: Replace bespoke GetTrackingMode reader with config.Load()
payload.description: Refactor monitor.GetTrackingMode at internal/monitor/session.go:305 to call internal/config.Load(projectPath) and return cfg.Tracking. Delete the existing bespoke os.ReadFile + json.Unmarshal implementation. Callsite is cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:296. Behavior must be identical for any existing config file.

payload.title: Replace bespoke IsCheckEnabled reader with config.Load()
payload.description: Refactor monitor.IsCheckEnabled in internal/monitor/checks.go to call internal/config.Load(projectPath) and read cfg.Checks[name] with fallback to per-check defaults. Delete the existing bespoke reader (entire file becomes unnecessary; the default helper moves to internal/config/normalize.go per the parent plan). Callsite is cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:319. With this in place, setting checks.drift_detection in either ~/.config/endless/config.json (global) OR <proj>/.endless/config.json (per-project) must work, with project key overriding global key (per-key merge confirmed by Mike).

payload.title: Document Python config merge contract in internal/config/README.md
payload.description: Short README listing every EndlessConfig field, its layer (global-only / project-only / layered), and its merge rule in plain English. Becomes the spec the Python follow-up task uses to match Go semantics. No code, just doc.

payload.title: Migrate Python config readers to honor Go config merge semantics
payload.description: Follow-up to E-949 (NOT a child). Python has 7+ readers in src/endless/config.py, event_bridge.py, register.py, reconcile.py plus tests, all using direct json.load. They currently work for the unchanged-config case but will diverge from Go's merge rules for any layered field (today: checks and tracking). Two viable paths: (a) reimplement the Go merge semantics in Python config.py (gross duplication, fragile); (b) move Python to call a Go shim like 'endless config get <key>' that runs Go's loader and returns the merged value. Path (b) aligns with the broader E-799 / E-894 direction of Go owning data reads. Discuss before implementing. Spec for Python parity lives in internal/config/README.md (to be written under E-949).

payload.title: Use per-key merge for checks map between global and per-project config
payload.description: Decision context: planning E-949 (refactor Endless config loading onto go-cfgstore). Question: when both ~/.config/endless/config.json and <proj>/.endless/config.json define a 'checks' map, should the project map fully replace the global map (whole-map), or override only the keys it explicitly sets (per-key)? Decision: PER-KEY. Rationale: Mike confirmed each key can potentially need its own merge semantics, so per-key gives the most flexibility. Whole-map would defeat the use case where a project sets just one check and silently loses inherited defaults for all others. Implication: Merge() in internal/config/merge.go iterates the global map keys and only overrides those explicitly present in the project map.

payload.title: Allow tracking field to be set in global config as a default
payload.description: Decision context: planning E-949 (refactor Endless config loading onto go-cfgstore). Question: today 'tracking' (enforce/track/off) is per-project-only. Should it be allowed in ~/.config/endless/config.json too as a fleet-wide default, with per-project overriding when set? Decision: YES, layer it. Rationale: Mike confirmed; the ergonomic win of 'default all my projects to enforce' once is worth one more cell in the merge matrix. Implication: EndlessConfig.Tracking participates in Merge() with project precedence; project value (if non-empty) wins, else global value, else Normalize() applies the registered-project default of enforce.

payload.title: Add general-purpose task relation CLI and surface relations in task show
payload.description:
Sibling to E-852 (which shipped narrow blocks/blocked-by support). Broader scope: make all task_deps relations first-class via CLI rather than requiring manual SQL or text references in descriptions. Punch list (vocabulary in E-XXX, the sibling decision):
1. At-creation flags on endless task add: --blocks <id>, --blocked-by <id>, --relates-to <id> [--rel-type <type>]
2. At-creation flags on endless decision add: --about <task-id> (default dep_type=informs), --decides <task-id> (alternate)
3. General-purpose primitive: endless task relate <source> --to <target> --type <type>; endless task unrelate <source> --to <target> [--type <type>]; endless task block becomes thin wrapper for relate --type blocks
4. Discoverability: endless task show <id> displays ALL relations grouped by type (not just blocks/replaces, which is today's behavior and the reason text-based references slip through unnoticed); endless decision show <id> displays what it links to
5. Expand or sibling-rename endless task deps: either show all relations or add endless task relations <id>; both with --type <type> filter
6. Reverse lookups: endless task list --related-to <id> [--rel-type <type>]; endless decision list --about <task-id>
Schema accepts any dep_type string (CHECK constraints removed), so no migration needed. Implementation order: settle vocabulary first (blocked on the sibling decision), then ship at-creation flags + show-all-relations together (highest impact), then primitive + reverse lookups.

payload.title: Standardize the canonical vocabulary of task relation dep_types
payload.description:
Pending decision (placeholder until resolved). Current task_deps.dep_type values in use across the code: blocks, needs, replaces. Proposed additions to support broader linking: informs / informed_by, decides / decided_by, relates_to (symmetric), documents / documented_by. Question: what is the canonical, frozen set, and which are inverses of each other (one stored row vs two)?

Why this needs to be decided BEFORE the sibling implementation task lands: CLI flags hard-code these strings. Adding a flag for a relation type that gets renamed later is churn. Schema accepts any string (CHECK constraints removed), so this is purely a UX/docs decision, not a migration.

Specific calls to make:
1. Is informs the right verb for decision→plan, or should it be decides? Or both with different semantics (informs = soft, decides = hard)?
2. Is relates_to worth including as the generic catch-all, or do we force a more specific type every time?
3. For inverse pairs (blocks/blocked_by, informs/informed_by), do we store one row and infer the inverse, or store both? Today blocks is stored as one row with source=blocked_task, target=blocker, and the inverse is queried by swapping. Same pattern for new pairs.
4. Should documents/documented_by be in the initial set or a follow-up?

Context: this decision blocks implementation of the sibling task (broader task relation CLI) which itself is the sibling of E-852 (narrow blocks/blocked-by support, already shipped at verify status).

fields.description:
Sibling to E-852 (which shipped narrow blocks/blocked-by support). Broader scope: make all task_deps relations first-class via CLI rather than requiring manual SQL or text references in descriptions. Punch list (vocabulary decision in E-958, blocking implementation):
1. At-creation flags on endless task add: --blocks <id>, --blocked-by <id>, --relates-to <id> [--rel-type <type>]
2. At-creation flags on endless decision add: --about <task-id> (default dep_type=informs), --decides <task-id> (alternate)
3. General-purpose primitive: endless task relate <source> --to <target> --type <type>; endless task unrelate <source> --to <target> [--type <type>]; endless task block becomes thin wrapper for relate --type blocks
4. Discoverability: endless task show <id> displays ALL relations grouped by type (not just blocks/replaces, which is today's behavior and the reason text-based references slip through unnoticed); endless decision show <id> displays what it links to
5. Expand or sibling-rename endless task deps: either show all relations or add endless task relations <id>; both with --type <type> filter
6. Reverse lookups: endless task list --related-to <id> [--rel-type <type>]; endless decision list --about <task-id>
Schema accepts any dep_type string (CHECK constraints removed), so no migration needed. Implementation order: settle vocabulary first (blocked on E-958), then ship at-creation flags + show-all-relations together (highest impact), then primitive + reverse lookups.

payload.title: Fix --status filter requiring --all for terminal statuses
payload.description: task list and task search excluded terminal statuses (confirmed/assumed/declined/obsolete) before applying --status filter, making --status assumed return nothing without --all. Reordered logic so explicit --status overrides the default exclusion.

payload.title: Explicit CLI filters override implicit display defaults
payload.description: When a CLI command has an implicit default that hides certain results (e.g., task list hiding terminal statuses unless --all is passed), an explicit filter flag for that same dimension (e.g., --status assumed) MUST override the implicit default. Rationale: a user passing --status X is unambiguously asking for X; requiring them to also pass --all is surprising and forces redundant flags. Apply to all current and future filter flags. First instance: E-959 fixed task list and task search.

payload.title: Require every task and decision item to have a non-empty description
payload.description: Policy decision: title alone is insufficient for any task or decision, no exceptions. Driver: Mike challenged the prior loose stance with the test 'does the title actually convey what work is being asked' and the contrived counter-example offered (Fix typo in CLAUDE.md) failed the test on its own merits (which typo? where?). No valid example surfaced where title alone carries the same context that title + description does. Implication: enforcement task is E-XXX-impl (next sibling, paired with this decision). Backfill of existing NULL-description tasks is E-XXX-backfill.

payload.title: Enforce description requirement at CLI write time rather than via schema NOT NULL
payload.description: Implementation approach decision for the policy decision (sibling). Schema-level NOT NULL on the existing nullable description column would require a SQLite table rebuild (per the schema comment, prior table-rebuild migrations caused catastrophic data loss in this repo). Risk-reward poor. Better to enforce at the write boundary: endless task add, endless decision add, endless task update. CLI can also be smarter than the schema, e.g., auto-derive description from the first non-blank line of --text when --text is provided and --description is not. Display-side fallback (show text excerpt when description is NULL) covers any pre-policy NULL rows that survive backfill.

payload.title: Require description on endless task add, decision add, and task update
payload.description: Enforce the description-required policy at CLI write time. Specifics: (1) endless task add: reject when neither --description nor --text is provided; if --text is given and --description is not, auto-derive description from first non-blank, non-heading line of the text file. (2) endless decision add: same rule, but decisions usually have no --text, so practically --description becomes required. (3) endless task update: when updating other fields, do not touch description; when explicitly clearing description (--clear description), require user to confirm or pass --force. (4) Display fallback in endless task show / decision show: when description is NULL (pre-policy rows that survive backfill), render an excerpt of text or '(no description, see text)' rather than blank. No schema change. Code locations: src/endless/cli.py (task_add, decision_add, task_update commands), src/endless/task_cmd.py (handlers). Implementation pairs with backfill task; ship together so CLI rule does not start failing against not-yet-backfilled rows.

payload.title: Backfill descriptions for existing tasks that lack them
payload.description: Mike identified 17 tasks with NULL description that should have one: E-949, E-931, E-930, E-927, E-926, E-925, E-915, E-909, E-874, E-776, E-612, E-611, E-610, E-609, E-608, E-607, E-606. Pairs with E-963 (CLI enforcement); both ship together so the new CLI rule does not start failing immediately against pre-existing NULL rows. Approach: for each task, read the title, attached text/prompt, and parent context to compose a 1-3 sentence description that conveys what the work is and why it exists. Use endless task update <id> --description '...'. Note: a follow-up scan should be run after backfill to catch any other NULL-description rows beyond the 17 Mike spot-checked.

fields.description: Policy decision: title alone is insufficient for any task or decision, no exceptions. Driver: Mike challenged the prior loose stance with the test 'does the title actually convey what work is being asked' and the contrived counter-example offered (Fix typo in CLAUDE.md) failed the test on its own merits (which typo? where?). No valid example surfaced where title alone carries the same context that title + description does. Implication: enforcement task is E-963 (CLI requirement). Backfill of existing NULL-description tasks is E-964. Implementation approach decision is E-962.

Plan to refactor Endless config loading from ad-hoc os.ReadFile + json.Unmarshal patterns onto Mike's go-cfgstore package, with explicit per-field merge semantics for layered config (global ~/.config/endless/config.json + per-project .endless/config.json). Driven by the drift_detection enable bug found during E-917 testing where a global config setting was silently ignored because the reader only checked per-project. Plan attached as text. Children E-950 through E-953; sibling decisions E-955, E-956, E-961, E-962; Python-side follow-up is E-954.

Parent plan to enforce task and decision recording via Claude Code hooks. Driver: docs/TASK_RECORDING_PROMPT.md identified repeated failure to record code changes as tasks and decisions emerging from chat as decision items, despite text-based reminders in CLAUDE.md and memory. Plan attached as text. Children: E-917 (drift detection on PreToolUse), E-918 (suggestions capture infra), E-919 (decision checkpoint on Stop), E-920 (SessionEnd manifest backstop), E-921 (config knobs for per-check disable). Follow-up: E-940 (close Bash escape hatch).

Records the policy that decision titles must state the decision directly rather than wrap it in meta-framing like 'Record that X' or 'Decide whether Y'. Driver: titles appear standalone in lists and dashboards; meta-framing wastes the primary information slot. Sibling to E-930 which exempts decisions from actionable-verb validation. Implementation lives in src/endless/task_cmd.py validate_title.

Records the policy that decision-typed items bypass the actionable-verb title validation enforced for tasks. Reason: decisions are statements (e.g., 'X is preferred over Y'), not actions (e.g., 'Implement X'); requiring an action verb forces awkward phrasing. Sibling to E-931 (no 'Record that' framing). Implementation lives in src/endless/task_cmd.py validate_title (skip when type=decision).

Adds endless decision list command and excludes type=decision items from the default endless task list output. Driver: decisions and tasks have different lifecycles (decide-then-confirm versus plan-then-implement-then-verify-then-complete) and surfacing them mixed in the same list creates noise during routine task triage.

Records the CLI convention that the literal string 'none' means NULL for nullable filters across endless task subcommands (e.g., --tier none, --parent none). Driver: needed a portable way to filter for unset values without shell-quoting empty strings or inventing per-field flags like --no-tier. Companion to E-925 which adds --parent filter using this convention.

Adds --parent <id|none> filter to endless task list, next, recent, search, and active subcommands. Driver: need to focus on direct children of a specific plan task (or root-level tasks via --parent none) without scrolling through every project task. Companion to E-926 which establishes 'none' as the NULL sentinel.

Adds --llm token-efficient output flag to endless task deps, matching the --llm flag already on list/show/next/active/recent/search. Driver: agents querying task dependencies need the same compact output format as other list commands to fit more context per call.

Fix the go.mod replace directive to use a public fork URL instead of a local-path reference, so the Python-to-Go session-recap migration in parent E-870 builds successfully outside Mike's local checkout. Required before E-870 can be merged or used by other developers.

Sandbox/smoke task for testing agentic workflows: build a Python Flask one-page site serving a happy-face SVG on port 5555. Full instructions in the prompt field (use existing .venv with Flask, open browser when running, do not ask questions). Not part of Endless feature work; created as a contained exercise.

Fix non-deterministic ordering in the BatchAssignment view caused by Go map iteration order. Sort keys before rendering so test output is deterministic and golden-file comparisons are reliable. Part of the gomion/go-tealeaves TUI testing infrastructure plan E-605. (Inferred from title; refine if needed.)

Wire the VT terminal emulator bidirectionally for PTY-based end-to-end tests of the TUI: test inputs flow through the PTY and outputs are captured back through the emulator for assertion. Part of TUI testing infrastructure plan E-605. (Inferred from title; refine if needed.)

Expand BatchAssignment and FileIntent Update method test coverage with deeper scenarios beyond the basic smoke tests in E-606. Part of TUI testing infrastructure plan E-605. (Inferred from title; refine if needed.)

Add navigation test for drilldown/drillup operations using a mock StackViewer to isolate navigation logic from real stack rendering. Part of TUI testing infrastructure plan E-605. (Inferred from title; refine if needed.)
Plan 3 of TUI testing rollout E-605: identify and close component coverage gaps in the go-tealeaves test suite. (Inferred from title and parent context; refine if needed.)
Plan 2 of TUI testing rollout E-605: comprehensive golden-file tests for gomtui plus Update-method tests. (Inferred from title and parent context; refine if needed.)

Plan 1 of TUI testing rollout E-605: foundational test infrastructure plus smoke tests across all 4 layers (cell/text, component, model, application). Establishes the test scaffold that subsequent plans (E-607, E-608, plus targeted tests E-609 through E-612) extend. (Inferred from title and parent context; refine if needed.)

Mike identified 17 tasks with NULL description that should have one: E-949, E-931, E-930, E-927, E-926, E-925, E-915, E-909, E-874, E-776, E-612, E-611, E-610, E-609, E-608, E-607, E-606. Pairs with E-963 (CLI enforcement). Backfill done in this session: 11 had enough context (E-949, E-915, E-931, E-930, E-927, E-926, E-925, E-909, E-874, E-776, plus the gomion E-605 children where parent context made meaning clear). 6 (E-606 through E-612) are inferred from titles and parent E-605 context with explicit '(Inferred; refine if needed)' marker so future Mike or session can refine without confusion. Follow-up: scan for any other NULL-description rows beyond Mike's spot-checked 17.

fields.text:
# Plan: Refactor Endless Config Loading onto go-cfgstore

## Context

While testing E-917 (drift detection), Mike set `"checks": {"drift_detection": true}` in `~/.config/endless/config.json` (the global config). The check defaulted to OFF and the feature appeared dead because my code in `internal/monitor/checks.go` reads only `<project>/.endless/config.json` (per-project). Time was wasted debugging a layering mismatch the codebase should never have allowed.

The deeper problem: I copied the existing precedent set by `monitor.GetTrackingMode` at `internal/monitor/session.go:305`. Both functions reimplement config layering ad hoc with raw `os.ReadFile` + `json.Unmarshal`, ignoring Mike's house package `go-cfgstore` which exists for exactly this purpose, plus `go-doterr` for errors and `go-dt` for paths. So this task replaces *both* bespoke readers, not just the new one.

The proximate win: setting any check or tracking value in either layer works with predictable precedence. The structural win: Endless config moves onto house standards, future config additions follow one pattern, and the merge semantics for every field are explicitly documented instead of accidentally inferred from whichever reader you happen to be looking at.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- New `internal/config/` package using `go-cfgstore`, `go-doterr`, `go-dt`.
- Refactor `monitor.GetTrackingMode` and `monitor.IsCheckEnabled` to use it.
- Field-by-field merge semantics design and documentation (the load-bearing design work).
- End-to-end test that the original `drift_detection` bug is fixed and per-project override still works.

**Out of scope (separate follow-up tasks, not blockers):**
- **Python config readers.** `src/endless/config.py`, `event_bridge.py`, `register.py`, `reconcile.py`, plus several tests, all read the same JSON files directly. Python cannot use `go-cfgstore`. They keep working in this task but will diverge from Go semantics until a follow-up either reimplements matching semantics in Python or moves Python to call a Go shim. Documented as a known gap; file follow-up task.
- **`ConfigDir()` helpers** in `internal/monitor/db.go:20`, `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:20`, `cmd/endless-channel/main.go:25`. These resolve `~/.config/endless/` for log/db file locations, not for reading user settings. Out of scope here; can migrate to `go-cfgstore`'s `CLIConfigDir()` later.
- **Schema versioning** (`RootConfigV1`) per `go-configuration.md` guidance. Not needed until the schema actually changes.

## Field Inventory and Proposed Merge Semantics

The real design work. Mike flagged that every property needs explicit merge rules. Below is the proposal; the open questions list which calls need confirmation before implementation.

### Global-only fields (`~/.config/endless/config.json`)
No per-project analog; project layer cannot define these.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `roots` | `[]string` | Directories Endless scans for projects. Inherently cross-project. |
| `scan_interval` | `int` | Seconds. Cross-project scheduler setting. |
| `ignore` | `[]string` | Paths/projects to skip during scan. Cross-project. |
| `ownership` | `map[string][]string` | GitHub org/user patterns for ownership detection. Cross-project. |
| `node_id` | `string` | Unique identifier for this machine in event-sourced sync. Per-machine. |

### Per-project-only fields (`<proj>/.endless/config.json`)
No global analog; global layer cannot define these.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | `string` | Project identifier. |
| `label` | `string` | Display name. |
| `description` | `string` | |
| `language` | `string` | Primary language. |
| `status` | `string` | active, paused, archived, idea. |
| `dependencies` | `[]string` | Other Endless projects this one depends on. |
| `documents` | `object` | Sub-keys like `rules: []`. |

### Layered fields (project overrides global where present)

| Field | Type | Merge rule |
|---|---|---|
| `tracking` | `"enforce" \| "track" \| "off"` | **Layered.** Global may set a default; project overrides only if explicitly set. (Resolved 2026-04-28.) |
| `checks` | `map[string]bool` | **Per-key merge with per-key dispatch.** Each key can have its own merge rule. Default rule: project value overrides global value for that key only; unmentioned keys inherit from global. Implementation must allow registering custom per-key merge logic for keys that need it. (Resolved 2026-04-28.) |

## Resolved Design Questions (2026-04-28)

1. **`checks` merge granularity.** Granular per-key merge. Each key can potentially have its own merge requirements, so the merge implementation must dispatch per-key rather than apply one uniform rule across the map. Default rule for keys without a custom rule: project wins if set, else inherit global, else fall back to `defaultCheckEnabled(name)`.
2. **`tracking` layerability.** Layerable. Global may set a default; project overrides only when explicitly set. Empty/unset on the project side means inherit, not "off".
3. **Additional layerable fields.** None planned at this time. New layerable fields will be added by extending the `EndlessConfig` struct and its `Merge()` method when the need arises.

## Recommended Approach

### Phase 1: Bootstrap (no behavior change yet)
- Add `github.com/mikeschinkel/go-cfgstore` (and `go-doterr`, `go-dt` if not already pulled in transitively) to `go.mod`.
- Create `internal/config/` with: `config.go` (the `EndlessConfig` struct + `RootConfig()` marker), `merge.go` (per-field `Merge()`, including per-key dispatch for `checks`), `normalize.go` (`Normalize()` with defaults), `errors.go` (`doterr` sentinels), `load.go` (`Load(projectPath)` entrypoint wrapping `cfgstore.LoadDefaultConfig[EndlessConfig, *EndlessConfig]`).
- Define the `EndlessConfig` struct with EVERY current field from both files, JSON-tagged exactly as today, so existing config files keep loading byte-identical.

### Phase 2: Switch the two existing readers
- Refactor `monitor.GetTrackingMode(projectID)` to call `config.Load(projectPath)` and read `cfg.Tracking`. Delete the bespoke reader. Honor the new layered semantics: empty/unset on the project side inherits global.
- Refactor `monitor.IsCheckEnabled(projectID, name)` to call `config.Load(projectPath)` and read `cfg.Checks[name]`, falling back to the per-check default when neither layer sets the key.
- `just build`. Both call sites compile and behave identically for the unchanged-config case.

### Phase 3: Verify the original bug
- Reproduce the bug against the OLD binary first (capture as confirmation): global-only `drift_detection: true`, attempt out-of-scope edit, edit succeeds when it should block.
- Run the same scenario against the NEW binary: edit blocks. This is the acceptance test for the refactor.
- Add the inverse test: project sets `drift_detection: false`, global sets `true`. Project wins, no block.
- Re-run the end-to-end hook test from the original E-917 verification.

### Phase 4: Document the Python contract
- Add `internal/config/README.md` (short) listing every field, its layer rules, and its merge rule, in plain English. The Python follow-up task uses this as the spec to match.

## Reuse, Do Not Rebuild

- `cfgstore.LoadDefaultConfig[RC, PRC]` — canonical entrypoint for CLI + Project layered loading with project precedence. Sits in `~/Projects/go-pkgs/go-cfgstore/load_config.go`.
- `RootConfig` interface (`RootConfig() / Normalize() / Merge()`) — standard pattern; Mike's pattern docs at `~/.claude/skills/go-config-file-author/references/go-configuration.md`.
- `go-doterr` for ALL error returns from the new package (no `fmt.Errorf`).
- `go-dt` for any path values stored on the struct or passed across boundaries.
- `defaultCheckEnabled(name)` already exists in `internal/monitor/checks.go`; move it to `internal/config/normalize.go` rather than rewriting.

## Critical Files

| Purpose | Path |
|---|---|
| Bespoke reader to delete (tracking) | `internal/monitor/session.go:305` (`GetTrackingMode`) |
| Bespoke reader to delete (checks) | `internal/monitor/checks.go` (entire file) |
| Hook callsites that consume these | `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:296`, `:319` |
| ConfigDir helper (kept, unrelated) | `internal/monitor/db.go:20` |
| Global config on disk (untouched) | `~/.config/endless/config.json` |
| Per-project config on disk (untouched) | `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/config.json` |
| go-cfgstore source | `~/Projects/go-pkgs/go-cfgstore/` |
| House skill reference | `~/.claude/skills/go-config-file-author/references/go-configuration.md` |
| House skill non-negotiables | `~/.claude/skills/go-config-file-author/references/go-non-negotiables.md` |

## Verification

End-to-end:
1. **Bug fix.** Global-only `drift_detection: true`, no per-project setting. Old binary: edit succeeds incorrectly. New binary: edit blocks. (Capture both runs as evidence.)
2. **Project override.** Global `true`, per-project `false`. Project wins, edit succeeds. Toggle per-project to `true`, edit blocks.
3. **Tracking layering.** Global `tracking: enforce`, no per-project setting. Behavior matches enforce. Add per-project `tracking: off`, behavior matches off. Remove per-project setting, behavior reverts to enforce.
4. **No regression in existing fields.** `endless task list`, `endless task start <id>`, `endless suggestions list` behave identically. `endless scan` (or whatever uses `roots`) still finds projects.
5. **Build green.** `just build`, all four binaries.

## Risks and Their Mitigation

- **Python drift.** Until the Python follow-up lands, Python and Go could disagree about merged values for any layered field. Mitigation: this task's only layered fields are `tracking` and `checks`, and Python doesn't read either of them today (only Go does). So at refactor time the gap is theoretical, not active.
- **Other Go config readers I haven't found.** The exploration found 5 Go callsites; only 2 read content (`GetTrackingMode`, `IsCheckEnabled`). The other 3 are `ConfigDir()` resolvers (out of scope). If a callsite is missed, build will catch any compile breakage; runtime behavior is unchanged for callers that don't go through the new package.
- **`go-cfgstore` is beta** (per its README). The dependency could shift. Acceptable; Mike maintains it and the package's API tier 1 is stable enough for our use.

## Endless Tasks to Create (after ExitPlanMode)

Parent (type=plan):
- "Refactor Endless config loading onto go-cfgstore"

Children (type=task):
1. "Add internal/config package with EndlessConfig schema and per-field merge"
2. "Replace bespoke GetTrackingMode reader with config.Load()"
3. "Replace bespoke IsCheckEnabled reader with config.Load()"
4. "Document Python config merge contract in internal/config/README.md"

Separate follow-up parent (NOT child of this plan):
- "Migrate Python config readers to honor Go config merge semantics"

Implementation order: 1 → 2 → 3 in one wave (they are tightly coupled), then 4 standalone.

payload.title: Add session summary command grouped by parent_id
payload.description:
Add 'endless session summary [<session-id>]' command that lists tasks touched in a session, grouped by parent task ID.

Decision (2026-04-28):
- Wait for E-799 (event-sourced architecture) to fully land. Session attribution will query the event log filtered by session_id rather than time-window intersection (cross-contaminates when sessions overlap) or a new session_task_touches join table (extra schema/write path).
- v1 grouping is parent_id only. No LLM/topical clustering in v1.
- If parent grouping proves too sparse for sessions with many root-level tasks, file a follow-up task for AI-based grouping (e.g., apfel for cheap local clustering). Do not build it preemptively.

Defaults to current session if no ID given.

payload.title: Add 'detail' alias for 'task show' command
payload.description: Claude muscle-memory keeps reaching for 'endless task detail <id>' instead of 'show'. Pave the cowpath: register 'detail' as a Click alias for the same callback so both verbs work. 'show' remains primary.

payload.title: Add parallel-session support via git worktrees
Add plan-file copy-on-attach for stable task plan paths
Add phrases table for shared string matching across hooks and CLI
Add git worktrees and behavioral gate for parallel sessions

fields.text:
# Parallel Sessions on Endless: Worktrees, Plan Stability, and the Behavioral Gate

## Context

Mike runs many parallel Claude Code sessions in tmux against the endless repo. Two problems block this from scaling:

1. **Code collision**: sessions share one working tree, so concurrent edits conflict.
2. **Topic drift**: when Mike redirects a session ("actually, let's also Y"), Claude jumps into Y without recording a new task. Today the user has to police this manually.

The fix is three coordinated workstreams. They overlap conceptually (all touch the session/task/hook surface) but should land in sequence to avoid one stalling the others.

## Workstream sequencing

```
W1 (plan-file stability) ──► W2 (phrases table) ──► W3 (worktrees + gate)
```

W1 is a prerequisite for W3 because plan-file presence is the trigger for worktree creation. W2 is a prerequisite for W3 because the gate's trigger phrases live in the phrases table. W2 is also independently useful (replaces hardcoded task-verb regexes in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`).

---

## Workstream 1 — Plan-file path stability

**Problem.** `tasks.plan_file_path` today points at `~/.claude/plans/<conversation-name>.md`. The harness names plan files per-conversation, not per-task, and Claude overwrites them when entering plan mode for unrelated work. References go stale silently.

**Fix.** Endless takes ownership of plan content the moment it's attached to a task.

- `endless task update <id> --text <source-path>` reads the source, writes a copy to `.endless/plans/<task-id>.md`, and stores that endless-owned path in `tasks.plan_file_path`. The harness path is treated as a transient draft buffer.
- Re-running `--text` overwrites the endless copy. User controls when updates happen.
- The directory `.endless/plans/` is checked into the repo (these are decisions; they belong with the codebase).

**Critical files.**
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — modify `task update --text` to copy-and-rewrite.
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — no change; the column already exists.

**Verification.** Attach a plan to a task; verify `.endless/plans/<id>.md` exists and `tasks.plan_file_path` points there. Overwrite the harness file; verify the task's plan content is unchanged.

---

## Workstream 2 — Phrases table

**Why.** Several places in endless hardcode lists of strings: title-verb whitelist in `task_cmd.py` (existing E-757 proposal), action regexes in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`, etc. The behavioral gate (W3) needs another such list (pivot triggers). One general table beats N specific tables.

**Obsoletes E-757.** That task proposes a `verbs` table for title verbs only. W2 covers it via `kind='verb'`. Mark E-757 replaced-by once W2 is a real endless task.

**Schema.**
```sql
CREATE TABLE phrases (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  kind TEXT NOT NULL,                 -- 'pivot', 'verb', 'action', ...
  phrase TEXT NOT NULL,
  match_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'substring',  -- 'substring' | 'word' | 'regex'
  case_sensitive INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  enabled INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S','now')),
  UNIQUE(kind, phrase)
);
```

`match_type` is orthogonal to `kind`: different kinds typically default to different match types, but rows within a kind can mix as needed.
- `substring` — input contains the phrase. Default; right for most pivot phrases.
- `word` — word-boundary match. Right for verbs ("Add" matches "Add a feature" but not "Adder").
- `regex` — full regex pattern. For action patterns and any future complex matches.

Project-scoped. Global fallback if a `kind` has no project rows.

**Naming convention** (general principle, applies beyond this table):
- **snake_case** for data and code identifiers in non-Go contexts: DB column names, Python/Ruby/Rust variable names, env var names. Also for DB string-column *values* that are NOT user-typed.
- **kebab-case** for user-facing strings: CLI flags, CLI argument values that the user types as labels (including `kind` values in this table), file names, URL slugs, git branch names.
- **Go exception**: Go identifiers use PascalCase (exported) or camelCase (unexported), NEVER snake_case or kebab-case.

So `kind='action'` (kebab; user types it on CLI), `--case-sensitive` (kebab), `task/757-move-title-verbs` (kebab), `CreateWorktree` (Go PascalCase), `def parse_phrase` (Python snake).

**CLI.**
- `endless phrase add <kind> "<phrase>" [--match substring|word|regex] [--case-sensitive]`
- `endless phrase list [--kind <k>]`
- `endless phrase disable <id>` / `endless phrase enable <id>`
- `endless phrase remove <id>`

No `endless verb` alias; not enough use to warrant the surface.

**Seed data.**

`kind='pivot'`, default `match_type='substring'` (one kind for both soft and hard triggers; `case_sensitive` distinguishes):
- case-insensitive: "actually", "wait", "also can you", "by the way", "btw", "different", "switch to", "while you're at it", "instead", "and can you"
- case-sensitive: "PIVOT" (Mike's deliberate override)

`kind='verb'`, `match_type='word'`, case-insensitive: seeded from current `_TITLE_VERBS` set in `src/endless/task_cmd.py`.

`kind='action'`, `match_type='regex'`: lifted from hardcoded regexes in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`. Hook reads from the table at startup, caches in memory.

**Critical files.**
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — new table.
- `internal/monitor/db.go` — migration.
- new: `src/endless/phrase_cmd.py` — CLI verbs.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — read `verb` phrases from DB instead of `_TITLE_VERBS`.
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — read `action` phrases from DB.

---

## Workstream 3 — Worktrees and the behavioral gate

Two complementary mechanisms, same goal: never let work happen without an explicit task association.

### Decisions locked from this conversation

- **No h2 / h2pp absorption.**
- **No DB worktrees table.** Filesystem and git are authoritative.
- **Layout inside the repo**: `.endless/worktrees/<name>/`, gitignored. Aligns with JetBrains' worktree support (location-agnostic via `git worktree list`).
- **Configurable path.** Endless must co-exist with other worktree managers, not own.
- **Not per-task by default.** Hybrid: only **plan-bearing** tasks earn a worktree.
- **Worktrees are agent-facing, not user-facing.** The `endless worktree` verb is primarily an API for Claude.
- **No `create` verb.** Worktree creation is always a side-effect of starting a plan-bearing task.
- **No `switch` verb.** A child process can't change its parent shell's cwd; switching means a new session.
- **Spawn stays narrow.** Don't couple worktree creation to `task spawn`.
- **Overlap detection deferred.** Mike coordinates manually short-term.

### Worktree trigger: plan-bearing task transitions to `in_progress`

A task is **plan-bearing** if `tasks.plan_file_path` is set (and points at the endless-owned file from W1). That's the entire definition. No `--isolated` flag, no children-detection. Edge cases are handled by adding a flag later if they bite.

When `endless task start <id>` runs on a plan-bearing task:

1. Compute branch name `task/<id>-<slug>`.
2. If a worktree for that branch exists, do nothing.
3. Otherwise: `git worktree add -b <branch> .endless/worktrees/<id>-<slug> <base>`, write `.endless/worktree.json` sidecar `{task_id, base_branch, created_at}`.
4. Print a cd-or-spawn hint if the current session's cwd is elsewhere. Hook handles enforcement.

### Worktree lifecycle (derived state, no DB)

| State | Derived from |
|---|---|
| **active** | `git worktree list --porcelain` shows it AND `.endless/worktree.json` present |
| **merged** | branch in `git branch --merged <base>` |
| **abandoned** | unmerged, no commits in N days, no live session bound; surfaced not auto-cleaned |
| **foreign** | listed by git but no endless sidecar; left alone |

Endless never deletes a worktree it didn't create.

### Behavioral gate (UserPromptSubmit hook)

The hook fires on every user message. **Two layers, no semantic guessing.**

**Layer 1 — always-on context refresh.** Inject one line into Claude's context: *"Active task: E-XXX — <title>."* No comparison, no decision required. Keeps the active task salient at the moment context is most likely to drift.

**Layer 2 — deterministic gate.** Match the user's message against `kind='pivot'` rows in the phrases table. Match → block Write/Edit until Claude clears the gate via CLI verb (see clearance rules below). Same gate, same clearance, regardless of whether the trigger was case-sensitive ("PIVOT") or case-insensitive ("actually") — the case-sensitivity flag distinguishes deliberate from incidental typing, not severity.

Worktree-bound work gets the gate automatically: the worktree IS the scope declaration, so PreToolUse already enforces task↔worktree match. Main-checkout work uses Layer 1 + Layer 2.

**Gate clearance.** Two paths, friction proportional to risk:

- **Continuation** (`endless task start <existing-id>` or `endless task confirm <id>`): Claude self-clears. No user approval needed; the user just typed the trigger and is in the loop. If Claude declares wrong, the user corrects on the next turn.
- **New task creation** (`endless task add ...` triggered as gate clearance): brief draft-and-approve flow. Claude proposes a title and parent based on the user's message; user accepts or edits. One content-bearing turn.

Avoids approval fatigue while preserving oversight where it matters.

### Hook integration summary

- **SessionStart**: read cwd, walk up for `.endless/worktree.json`, bind worktree identity in-memory.
- **UserPromptSubmit (new event)**: inject Layer 1 reminder; check phrases table for Layer 2 triggers.
- **PreToolUse**: existing task-active check + worktree↔task match for plan-bearing tasks.
- **Stop / SessionEnd**: nudge to merge if task moved to `verify` and worktree clean.

### CLI surface (Claude is the primary consumer)

Output is `--json` capable, exit codes stable, no interactive prompts.

**Introspection (high frequency):**
- `endless worktree current` — what worktree am I in? bound task? dirty/clean/ahead/behind?
- `endless worktree list [--status active|merged|abandoned|foreign]`
- `endless worktree show <name>`
- `endless worktree for-task <task-id>` — resolve task to worktree path or "not isolated".

**Mutation:**
- `endless worktree merge <name>` — fast-forward into base, then `git worktree remove`. Refuses on `foreign` or unmerged loss.
- `endless worktree drop <name>` — explicit cleanup; refuses unmerged/dirty without `--force`.

### Co-existence with other worktree managers

- `worktree_dir` configurable in `.endless/config.json` (default `.endless/worktrees`).
- Discovery via `git worktree list --porcelain` + sidecar filter, not path-prefix.
- `merge` / `drop` refuse on worktrees lacking an endless sidecar.

### Sidecar format

JSON, matching the rest of `.endless/`. Not YAML.

```json
{
  "task_id": "E-808",
  "base_branch": "main",
  "branch": "task/808-event-logs-authoritative",
  "created_at": "2026-04-28T15:30:00"
}
```

---

## What we're explicitly NOT doing yet

- Cross-branch overlap detection (file conflict warnings before merge).
- Auto-cleanup of abandoned worktrees.
- Recipe templates / pods / manager-evaluator pattern (h2pp absorption).
- Auto-cd or auto-spawn on task start.
- Replacing tmux.
- LLM-based topic-drift detection (cry-wolf risk; deterministic gates only).

## Critical files (full picture)

**W1:**
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — copy-and-rewrite in `task update --text`.

**W2:**
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — `phrases` table.
- `internal/monitor/db.go` — migration.
- `src/endless/phrase_cmd.py` (new) — CLI verbs.
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — read task-verb phrases from DB.

**W3:**
- `internal/git/worktree.go` (new) — wrapper over `git worktree`.
- `internal/monitor/worktree.go` (new) — sidecar IO, derived-state queries.
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — `SessionStart` worktree resolution; new `UserPromptSubmit` handler; `PreToolUse` worktree↔task check.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `task start` triggers worktree creation for plan-bearing tasks.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` (new) — `current / list / show / for-task / merge / drop`.
- `.gitignore` — add `.endless/worktrees/`.

## Slug generation

Lowercase, strip filler words (`a`, `an`, `the`, `to`, `from`, `of`, `for`, `with`, `in`, `on`, `at`, `by`, `and`, `or`), replace non-alnum with dash, collapse repeated dashes, truncate to 40 chars at a word boundary.

Example: task E-757 "Move title verbs from hardcoded list to database table" → slug `move-title-verbs-hardcoded-list-database`. Branch: `task/757-move-title-verbs-hardcoded-list-database`.

## Resolved decisions

- Branch naming: `task/<id>-<slug>` (slug rules above).
- Layer 1 reminder: delivered as system reminder, not prepended to user message.
- Gate clearance: CLI verb required; continuation self-clears, new-task creation gets brief draft-and-approve flow.
- One pivot kind, not separate soft/hard categories.
- snake_case for DB/code, kebab-case for user-facing strings.

## Verification

**W1.** Attach a plan to a task; confirm `.endless/plans/<id>.md` exists and is referenced. Overwrite the harness draft; confirm task's plan unchanged.

**W2.** Add a shift_trigger phrase via CLI; send a user message containing it; confirm hook fires. Disable; confirm it stops. Replace a hardcoded task-verb regex with a DB row; confirm hook still detects task starts.

**W3.**
- Two parallel sessions on two plan-bearing tasks; each gets its own worktree; no edit collisions.
- Plan-bearing task active, cwd is main checkout; PreToolUse blocks with switch-worktree message.
- User types "actually let's fix the auth bug"; soft gate fires; Claude can't edit until task declared.
- User types "PIVOT" mid-message; force gate fires regardless of context.
- Small ad-hoc task `in_progress`; no worktree created; existing session keeps editing freely.
- Manually `git worktree add` outside endless; `endless worktree list` shows it as `foreign`; merge/drop refuse.

# W1 — Plan-file Path Stability

> Part of E-968 (parallel-session support). See `.endless/plans/E-968.md` for full context.

## Problem

`tasks.plan_file_path` today points at `~/.claude/plans/<conversation-name>.md`. The harness names plan files per-conversation, not per-task, and Claude overwrites them when entering plan mode for unrelated work. References go stale silently. This blocks W3, which uses plan-file presence as the worktree-creation trigger.

## Fix

Endless takes ownership of plan content the moment it's attached to a task.

- `endless task update <id> --text <source-path>` reads the source, writes a copy to `.endless/plans/<task-id>.md`, and stores that endless-owned path in `tasks.plan_file_path`. The harness path is treated as a transient draft buffer.
- Re-running `--text` overwrites the endless copy. User controls when updates happen.
- The directory `.endless/plans/` is checked into the repo (these are decisions; they belong with the codebase).

## Critical files

- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — modify `task update --text` to copy-and-rewrite into `.endless/plans/<task-id>.md` and store that path.
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — no change; the column already exists.

## Bootstrap note

This task fixes the very problem we hit while planning E-968. The four plan files attached to E-968/E-969/E-970/E-971 were copied manually as a one-time workaround. After W1 ships, future plans flow through `task update --text` automatically.

## Verification

- Attach a plan to a task via `task update --text`; verify `.endless/plans/<id>.md` exists and `tasks.plan_file_path` points there.
- Overwrite the harness draft file at the original path; verify the task's plan content is unchanged.
- Re-run `task update --text <new-path>`; verify endless copy is overwritten with new content.

# W2 — Phrases Table

> Part of E-968 (parallel-session support). See `.endless/plans/E-968.md` for full context. Obsoletes E-757.

## Why

Several places in endless hardcode lists of strings: title-verb whitelist in `task_cmd.py` (existing E-757 proposal), action regexes in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`, etc. The behavioral gate in W3 (E-971) needs another such list (pivot triggers). One general table beats N specific tables.

## Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE phrases (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  kind TEXT NOT NULL,                 -- 'pivot', 'verb', 'action', ...
  phrase TEXT NOT NULL,
  match_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'substring',  -- 'substring' | 'word' | 'regex'
  case_sensitive INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  enabled INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S','now')),
  UNIQUE(kind, phrase)
);
```

`match_type` is orthogonal to `kind`: different kinds typically default to different match types, but rows within a kind can mix as needed.
- `substring` — input contains the phrase. Default; right for most pivot phrases.
- `word` — word-boundary match. Right for verbs ("Add" matches "Add a feature" but not "Adder").
- `regex` — full regex pattern. For action patterns and any future complex matches.

Project-scoped. Global fallback if a `kind` has no project rows.

## Naming convention (general principle)

- **snake_case** for data and code identifiers in non-Go contexts (DB column names, Python/Ruby/Rust variable names, env var names; also DB string-column *values* that are NOT user-typed).
- **kebab-case** for user-facing strings (CLI flags, CLI argument values that the user types as labels including `kind` values in this table, file names, URL slugs, git branch names).
- **Go exception**: Go identifiers use PascalCase (exported) or camelCase (unexported), NEVER snake_case or kebab-case.

So `kind='action'` (kebab; user types it on CLI), `--case-sensitive` (kebab), `task/757-move-title-verbs` (kebab), `CreateWorktree` (Go PascalCase), `def parse_phrase` (Python snake).

## CLI

- `endless phrase add <kind> "<phrase>" [--match substring|word|regex] [--case-sensitive]`
- `endless phrase list [--kind <k>]`
- `endless phrase disable <id>` / `endless phrase enable <id>`
- `endless phrase remove <id>`

No `endless verb` alias; not enough use to warrant the surface.

## Seed data

`kind='pivot'`, default `match_type='substring'`:
- case-insensitive: "actually", "wait", "also can you", "by the way", "btw", "different", "switch to", "while you're at it", "instead", "and can you"
- case-sensitive: "PIVOT" (Mike's deliberate override)

`kind='verb'`, `match_type='word'`, case-insensitive: seeded from current `_TITLE_VERBS` set in `src/endless/task_cmd.py`.

`kind='action'`, `match_type='regex'`: lifted from hardcoded regexes in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`.

## Critical files

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — new `phrases` table.
- `internal/monitor/db.go` — migration.
- new: `src/endless/phrase_cmd.py` — CLI verbs.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — read `verb` phrases from DB instead of `_TITLE_VERBS`.
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — read `action` phrases from DB at startup, cache in memory.

## Verification

- Add a `pivot` phrase via CLI; confirm it appears in `endless phrase list`.
- Disable; confirm `enabled=0`.
- Replace a hardcoded title-verb check with a DB lookup; confirm task creation still validates.
- Replace a hardcoded action regex with a DB row; confirm hook still detects task starts/completes.

payload.title: Fix endless project resolution to detect main repo from inside a worktree
Show updated field values in task update output
payload.description: Currently 'endless task update' prints '• Updated E-###: status' listing only field names. Should print 'status=ready' (or similar) showing the new value for every changed field. Source: src/endless/task_cmd.py:1528. The fields dict already holds the values; iterate fields.items() and render key=value pairs. Truncate long blobs (text, prompt) to a short preview or just show '<set>' / '<cleared>'. Handle None as 'cleared' or '∅' for parent_id/tier clears.

payload.title: Task update CLI shows field=value, not just field names
payload.description: When 'endless task update' changes one or more fields, the per-item output line should display the new value for each field, not just the field name. Rationale: a user (or agent) reading the output wants to verify what the new state actually is without a follow-up 'task detail' call; field names alone confirm only that something changed. Format: '• Updated E-###: status=ready, tier=2'. For long blobs (text, prompt) show a compact marker like '<set>' or '<cleared>' rather than dumping the body. Cleared values (None) render as 'cleared' or similar sentinel. Applies to all fields the update command accepts.

Add .endless/worktrees/ to .gitignore

payload.title: Add implements/implemented-by relationship type
payload.description:
Add a new task relationship type 'implements' (with inverse 'implemented-by') so an implementation task can link back to the decision it acts on. Mirrors the existing replaces/replaced-by pattern from E-890 — see commit 68b7986 for the precedent.

Use case that motivated this: E-975 (decision: gitignore worktree artifacts) and E-976 (implementation: added .endless/worktrees/ to .gitignore, commit 32c4b75) need to be linked so a future reader of E-975 can find the evidence, and a future reader of E-976 can find the rule it acts on. As part of testing this work, close that loop: wire E-976 implements E-975.

Acceptance: (a) 'endless task implements <impl-id> --by <decision-id>' verb works (or analogous syntax matching replaces/replaced-by), (b) 'endless task show E-975' and 'endless task show E-976' both display the linkage, (c) E-976 → E-975 link is in place after this task lands.

payload.title: Add 'implements' link type between tasks
payload.description: Add an 'implements' relationship link between tasks so a verify/implementation task can be linked to the decision task it implements. Example: E-976 (verify, 'Add .endless/worktrees/ to .gitignore') implements E-975 (decision, 'Gitignore .endless/worktrees/'). The decision is the reusable rule; the verify task is the concrete evidence/implementation. Linking them lets future readers walk from implementation back to the rule it implements (and forward from a rule to all its implementations). Implementor: please close the loop by linking E-976 -> E-975 once the link type works.
