Test event-bridge fix
Add bridge verification test
Sync stray events from worktree .endless/events to main event log
payload.description:
E-972 fixed event_bridge.emit_event so events now correctly land in main's .endless/events/. But before the fix, events written from inside worktrees went to <worktree>/.endless/events/events-*.jsonl. Those stray events are not yet reflected in main's authoritative event log.

Scope: identify worktrees with stray event files (compare timestamps/sizes against main), determine which events are duplicates of main vs. unique writes that need to be replayed/merged, and either replay them into main's log or document them as accepted loss.

For the e-967 worktree specifically: events file is 150651 bytes (main is 153226 bytes after fix). The worktree may have unique events between when it was created and when E-972's fix landed.

fields.text:
Decision: standardize task relation vocabulary so the general-purpose linking CLI (E-957) can land without churn.

## Decided (2026-04-28)

**Verb:** `link` is the canonical command for creating typed relationships between tasks.

**Syntax:** `endless task link <source> --to <target> --as <type>` (and inverse `endless task unlink ...`).

**`block` is a shortcut, not a separate verb.** `endless task block <id> --by <other>` remains as a thin wrapper that calls `link --as blocks` under the hood. Same applies to any other inverse-paired shortcuts we add (e.g., `replace`, `implements`).

**Initial canonical dep_types:**
- `blocks` / `blocked_by` (already in use)
- `needs` (already in use)
- `replaces` / `replaced_by` (already in use)
- `implements` / `implemented_by` (E-977/E-978 — link impl task to its decision)
- `informs` / `informed_by` (decision → task it informs, soft)
- `relates_to` (symmetric catch-all)

**Inverse storage:** one row, query by swapping source/target — same pattern as today's `blocks`. No duplicate rows.

**Deferred:**
- `decides` / `decided_by`: not adopted. `informs` is sufficient for soft "this decision affects this task"; `implements` covers the hard "this task acts on this decision".
- `documents` / `documented_by`: deferred to follow-up; not needed for current use cases.

## Why this matters

CLI flags hard-code these strings. Locking the vocabulary now prevents renaming churn once `task add --blocks <id>`, `task add --implements <id>`, etc. are wired up.

Schema is already permissive (CHECK constraints removed), so this is purely a UX/docs decision — no migration needed.

## Unblocks

- E-957 (general-purpose relation CLI)
- E-977/E-978 (`implements` link type)

payload.title: Add --decision flag to task add/update for inline rationale capture
payload.description: Add a --decision <text> flag to 'endless task add' and 'endless task update' that automatically creates a paired decision-type task and links it via 'informs' (or 'decides', per E-958 vocab). Motivation: task<->decision pairs have become painfully common in this codebase (see E-957, E-958, E-975/E-976, E-977/E-978). Today, capturing rationale requires three steps: (1) make the change, (2) 'task add --type decision' with the rationale, (3) link them. Compressing that into one CLI invocation removes the friction that causes rationale to get lost in commit messages or chat instead of the task DB. Depends on E-957 (general-purpose link CLI) and E-958 (vocabulary). Acceptance: 'endless task update E-X --status verify --decision "smoke tested locally"' creates a decision task, links it to E-X, and records the rationale in one shot.

payload.title: Capture rationale inline via --decision flag rather than separate task+link workflow
payload.description:
Decision (2026-04-28): rather than continuing to require a 3-step ritual to capture rationale (make change, create decision task, link them), 'endless task add' and 'endless task update' should support a --decision <text> flag that performs all three steps atomically.

Why: task<->decision pairs have become painfully common in Endless development. Friction in capturing rationale at the moment-of-decision causes it to leak into commit messages, chat, or be lost entirely. Compressing capture to one CLI call removes the excuse.

Alternatives considered:
- Hook-based capture (E-582): captures AskUserQuestion Q&A automatically, but only fires during interactive AI sessions and only for that specific tool. Doesn't cover the common case of 'I just decided to do X while typing a CLI command'.
- Status quo (manual 3-step): high friction, gets skipped under deadline pressure.
- Free-text comment field on task: doesn't get queryable as a decision, doesn't show up in 'task list --type decision'.

Implements via E-980. Depends on vocabulary settled in E-958 (specifically: which dep_type does --decision use? Likely 'informs' or 'decides_for' — defer to E-958).

payload.title: Add 'verify' and 'reconcile' to title verbs allowlist
payload.description: Two verbs hit during E-968 work: 'verify' (legitimate action verb for verification tasks) and 'reconcile' (legitimate action verb for sync/cleanup work). Both blocked by validate_title in src/endless/task_cmd.py:99. Add them to _TITLE_VERBS so future tasks with these verbs aren't blocked. Will be obsoleted by E-970 (phrases table) but worth fixing now to stop wasting verbs as friction.

fields.title: Reconcile stray events from worktree .endless/events into main event log

payload.title: Add 'prune' to title verbs allowlist
payload.description: Hit during E-969 follow-up filing. 'Prune' is a legitimate action verb. Will be obsoleted by E-970 (phrases table).

payload.title: Prune unattached plan snapshots by age and size cap
payload.description:
Once E-969 ships eager plan snapshots to .endless/plans/snapshots/, this task adds the prune mechanism.

Policy:
- Snapshots whose content was attached to a task end up copied to .endless/plans/<task-id>.md, so the snapshot itself is redundant after attach (provenance lives at the task's file).
- Keep for N days (default 30, configurable): unattached snapshots.
- Hard cap (default 100MB, configurable): oldest-first eviction.

CLI: 'endless plan snapshots prune [--days N] [--max-size MB] [--dry-run]'. Idempotent. Surface count and bytes freed. Triggered opportunistically (on 'endless plan snapshots' invocations) or via explicit prune call. No background daemon.

payload.title: Fix go.mod replace directives to resolve from worktrees
payload.description:
go.mod has 'replace github.com/mikeschinkel/go-cfgstore => ../go-pkgs/go-cfgstore' (and similar for go-doterr, go-dt, go-cliutil, go-logutil). The relative '../' resolves correctly from main repo (~/Projects/endless → ~/Projects/go-pkgs/) but not from inside a worktree (~/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/<id> → ~/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/go-pkgs/ which doesn't exist).

Reproducer: cd .endless/worktrees/<id> && go build ./cmd/endless-hook

Options:
1. Symlink as worktree-bootstrap step: create .endless/worktrees/go-pkgs -> ~/Projects/go-pkgs (would let all sibling worktrees share via the parent dir).
2. Update go.mod replace paths to use a more portable scheme.
3. Document a manual symlink convention until E-971 automates worktree setup.

Likely (1) baked into E-971's worktree-creation hook. Until then, manual symlink works.

fields.text: # Test plan content

fields.description: Parent task for the parallel-session-on-endless effort. Goal: let multiple Claude Code sessions work on the same repo concurrently without code collisions or topic drift between sessions. Approach: per-session git worktrees so sessions are filesystem-isolated; main becomes a sacred clean integration target; behavioral gate (PIVOT trigger + UserPromptSubmit hook) catches topic shifts before edits leak between scopes. Decomposes into W1 (E-969) plan-file stability, W2 (E-970) phrases table, W3 (E-971) worktrees + gate, plus supporting bug fixes E-972 (project resolution from worktree) and infra (E-975/E-976 gitignore). Full design in .endless/plans/E-968.md.

Workstream 1 of E-968. Captures plan content at every plan-file Write so within-session overwrites and never-attached plans are recoverable. Two pieces shipped (commit f1e953d): (1) PostToolUse hook in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go writes a content-addressed snapshot to .endless/plans/snapshots/<ts>-<sha8>.{md,json} on every Write to ~/.claude/plans/*; (2) endless task update --text additionally writes a stable per-task copy to .endless/plans/<task-id>.md (DB stays canonical; file is a predictable export). New CLI: 'endless plan-snapshots {list,show}' with --session/--today/--json filters. Pruning deferred to E-984. Plan: .endless/plans/E-969.md.

Workstream 2 of E-968. Replaces several hardcoded string lists in endless with a single 'phrases' table keyed by 'kind' (single-word: pivot, verb, action) and discriminated by 'match_type' (substring | word | regex). Project-scoped with global fallback. Obsoletes E-757 (which proposed a verbs-only table). New CLI: 'endless phrase {add,list,disable,enable,remove}' with --match and --case-sensitive flags. Seeds: pivot triggers (actually, wait, by the way, ..., plus PIVOT case-sensitive); verbs from current _TITLE_VERBS in src/endless/task_cmd.py; actions from hardcoded regexes in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go. Naming convention: kebab-case for kind values (user-typed at CLI), snake_case for column names. Plan: .endless/plans/E-970.md.

Workstream 3 of E-968. The headline feature: per-session git worktrees so concurrent sessions don't collide, plus a behavioral gate to catch topic drift. Two flavors: per-session worktrees (auto-created on session start, removed on session end) and dedicated task worktrees (for plan-bearing tasks; persist across sessions). Rust-style ownership: at most one owner at a time, transferable via .endless/worktree.lock (atomic O_EXCL claim, stale via kill -0, released on SessionEnd). Main = sacred clean integration target; 'endless worktree land' refuses dirty main. Behavioral gate: UserPromptSubmit hook injects active-task system reminder (Layer 1) plus deterministic phrase match against E-970's pivot kind (Layer 2); PIVOT word as deliberate user override; gate clearance via task add or task start (CLI verb required, not chat ack). Blocked by E-969 (plan-bearing detection), E-970 (phrase storage), E-972 (project resolution). Plan: .endless/plans/E-971.md.

Bug: when endless CLI commands run from inside a git worktree (e.g. .endless/worktrees/<id>/), events were appended to the worktree's .endless/events/ instead of main's, because event_bridge.emit_event defaulted project_root to Path.cwd(). Result: bifurcated event logs with the same project's writes split across multiple .endless/events/ directories. Discovered when another session reported strange behavior from inside the e-967 worktree; confirmed by comparing event-log file sizes between main and worktree. Fix (commit 351ec45): emit_event now looks up the project's registered path by name from the projects table and uses that, falling back to cwd only if the project isn't registered. Worktree's events file at the time of the fix had 150651 bytes of strays — reconciliation tracked separately as E-979.

Decision: .endless/worktrees/ is gitignored. Rationale: worktree directories are local git state (each contains a .git pointer file plus a full checkout), not project content. They're created and managed by 'git worktree add', vary per developer/machine, and shouldn't be tracked. The decision is intentionally narrow to 'worktrees/'; other items under .endless/ (config.json, plans/, events/, data.sql) remain tracked because they ARE project content. Implemented by E-976 (commit 32c4b75). Establishes a precedent for any future worktree-adjacent local state files (e.g., E-971's .endless/worktree.lock, which lives inside a worktree and is therefore already covered by this gitignore).

Add '.endless/worktrees/' to .gitignore. Implements E-975 (decision). Single-line addition to the existing .gitignore file. Committed as 32c4b75. Verification: 'git status' from main no longer shows .endless/worktrees/ as untracked when worktrees exist; existing worktree at .endless/worktrees/e-967 is now ignored as expected.

payload.title: Add pluggable post-worktree-create hook for project-specific bootstrap
payload.description:
E-985 surfaced a Go-specific worktree friction (go.mod replace directives use ../go-pkgs/... which doesn't resolve from inside .endless/worktrees/<id>/). The fix for Go is one specific solution, but every language/project will have its own worktree-bootstrap concerns: Node needs npm install or symlink to node_modules; Python may need venv recreation; Rust may need target/ cleanup; etc. Endless cannot bake all of these in.

Make worktree-bootstrap pluggable via a project-owned hook. Proposed mechanism: when 'endless worktree' creates a new worktree (per E-971's design), it looks for and runs '<project-root>/.endless/hooks/post-worktree-create.sh' (or similar) inside the freshly-created worktree directory. The script is project-owned, version-controlled, and free to do anything: symlink, install deps, run language-specific tooling.

Endless ships no default hook. Each project (or its AI assistant) writes the hook appropriate to its stack. For endless-on-endless, the hook would symlink ~/Projects/go-pkgs into the worktree's parent dir to satisfy the go.mod replace paths.

Acceptance: (a) hook discovered at a documented predictable path, (b) executed with worktree path as argument and cwd, (c) hook failure is surfaced but non-fatal (worktree still created), (d) E-985's manual symlink replaceable by a documented hook script.

Linked to E-985 (the Go-specific symptom that motivated this generalization).

payload.title: Resolve tension between event log auto-appends and clean-main precondition
payload.description:
Architectural conflict surfaced during E-969 work:

(1) E-808 design (commit c9425fb): event log files at .endless/events/events-*.jsonl are committed and authoritative; every endless CLI mutation appends to them.

(2) E-971 design (parent E-968): main is a sacred clean integration target; 'endless worktree land' refuses dirty main.

These two designs conflict. Every endless CLI invocation (run from anywhere, since E-972's fix routes events to main's project root regardless of cwd) leaves main with an uncommitted event log append. Main is therefore continuously dirty, which would block 'land' on every invocation.

Manifestations seen during E-968 work: needed to commit '.endless/events/events-a7f3-000001.jsonl' as a separate housekeeping commit just to keep main landable.

Options to consider (not exhaustive, expected to widen during research):

A. Auto-commit events log on every append. Each mutation produces a tiny commit. Pros: main stays formally clean. Cons: explosive commit-log noise, performance cost, churns git index.

B. Gitignore the events log, sync via separate mechanism. Pros: main truly clean. Cons: events log loses git as durable store; need alternative replication/sync.

C. Define 'landable cleanliness' more narrowly than 'git status clean'. Land permits an uncommitted .endless/events/ append but refuses other dirt. Pros: pragmatic. Cons: special case in the land logic; risk of expanding the carve-out over time.

D. Move events log out of the working tree (e.g., into .git/endless/ or ~/.config/endless/events/<project>/). Pros: not in working tree, can't dirty it. Cons: events no longer git-versioned; sync across machines becomes manual.

E. Background daemon batches event commits every N seconds or M events. Pros: cleanliness eventually-consistent. Cons: new daemon process, race with land, eventual-consistency surprises.

This needs discussion and probably a research session before implementation. Tagged --type research.

payload.title: Fix newlines in session list summary corrupting table layout
payload.description: endless session list renders raw summaries which can contain literal newlines (e.g. recap text starting with '**Verify (uncommitted, in work...'), breaking the column layout in the terminal. Collapse all whitespace (newlines, tabs, runs of spaces) to single spaces before truncation in src/endless/session_cmd.py around line 352.

fields.text:
# E-957: General-purpose `task link`/`unlink` CLI

## Context

Endless task↔decision pairs and other cross-task relationships have become painfully common (E-957, E-958, E-975/E-976, E-977/E-978). Today only `block`/`unblock` and `replace` create typed relations, and the display layer surfaces only those two dep_types — every other linkage gets lost in description text, unlinkable and unqueryable.

E-958 (now confirmed) locks the vocabulary: `link` is the canonical CLI verb (the agent-instinctive choice; see `~/.claude/projects/-Users-mikeschinkel-Projects-endless/memory/feedback_cli_naming_agent_friction.md`). `block` and `replace` survive as thin shortcut wrappers. Six initial dep_types are frozen: `blocks/blocked_by`, `needs` (kept only as a transition synonym → migrated away), `replaces/replaced_by`, `implements/implemented_by`, `informs/informed_by`, `relates_to`.

This plan implements E-957's full punch list. Outcome: any task or decision can be linked to any other with a typed relationship; `task show` displays all relations grouped; `task add` and `decision add` accept at-creation flags; reverse lookup via `task list --related-to`; legacy `block`/`replace` continue to work.

## Decisions resolved up front

1. **Storage direction:** active voice. A row stores `source=actor, target=acted_upon, dep_type=<active_verb>`. So a row with `dep_type='blocks'` reads "source blocks target" — source is the blocker, target is the blocked task. Existing `needs` rows (stored as `source=blocked, target=blocker, dep_type='needs'`) get their source/target swapped AND the dep_type renamed to `'blocks'`. **This also fixes a latent bug in `replaces` rows** — `replace_task` (task_cmd.py:2128-2133) currently stores `source=old, target=new, dep_type='replaces'`, which reads literally as "old replaces new" but the comment and behavior treat it as "old replaced_by new". The label is active but the layout is passive — they disagree. Migration swaps source/target for existing `replaces` rows so the active label matches the layout going forward.

   *Why this over passive storage:* the CLI verb (`task block`, `task replace`), the stored dep_type (`'blocks'`, `'replaces'`), and the row layout all align. Active voice is what agents and humans reach for naturally; reading raw SQL says the same thing the verb implies. Worth the one-time migration cost.

2. **Canonical stored dep_types** (5 stored, all active voice; 4 inverse views computed): `blocks`, `implements`, `informs`, `replaces`, `relates_to` are stored. Their inverses `blocked_by`, `implemented_by`, `informed_by`, `replaced_by` are the same row queried with source/target swapped. `relates_to` is symmetric (no inverse name).
3. **`decision add --about` vs `--decides`:** two flags, two semantics. `--about <task-id>` → `dep_type=informs` (soft: decision provides context). `--decides <task-id>` → `dep_type=implements` with source=task, target=decision (hard: task implements this decision; equivalent to `implements` from the task side).
4. **Legacy CHECK constraint** in `db.py:147-148` (`CHECK (dep_type IN ('blocks', 'needs'))`) must be dropped from any DB that has it. SQLite has no `DROP CHECK`, so the migration uses table-rebuild (CREATE new, INSERT … SELECT, DROP, RENAME) inside a transaction. Migration is idempotent (detects CHECK in `sqlite_master.sql`).
5. **`add_item` returns the new id.** Cleaner than threading through the event payload. Only the CLI layer at cli.py:557 currently ignores the return.
6. **`unlink` without `--as`:** when omitted, query all dep_types between the pair. Zero → "no relation" error. One → remove it. Multiple → error listing them and require `--as`. Never silent delete.
7. **Display order in `task show`:** fixed, action-priority descending, only render groups with rows: `blocked_by`, `blocks`, `implements`, `implemented_by`, `informs`, `informed_by`, `replaces`, `replaced_by`, `relates_to`.

## Critical files

- `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/src/endless/task_cmd.py` — relation CRUD and display logic (lines 2119-2302, plus task_next at 715-728, detail_item at 1543-1714, add_item at 1124-1170, show_plan at 551-703)
- `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/src/endless/cli.py` — CLI registrations (block/replace/unblock/deps at 696-731, task add at 528-557, task update at 560-589, task list at 362-394, decision add at 777-792)
- `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/src/endless/db.py` — schema migration (lines 73-163, particularly 132-153 for task_deps init)
- `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/internal/schema/schema.sql` — canonical schema (line 108: `DEFAULT 'blocks'` → `DEFAULT 'blocked_by'`); also `//go:embed`-ed by `/internal/schema/schema.go` so any change ripples to Go consumers
- `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/tests/test_relations.py` — new

## Phase 1 — Schema, vocabulary constant, migration

Files: `db.py`, `schema.sql`, `task_cmd.py`

1. `schema.sql:108` — `DEFAULT 'blocks'` is already correct for active-voice storage; **no change needed**. (The default expressed the original intent; data drift introduced `needs`.)
2. `db.py:138-153` — drop the `CHECK` clauses on `dep_type`, `source_type`, `target_type` (the source/target_type CHECKs go too — they're stale once cross-project relations expand; `schema.sql` doesn't have them, so converge). `DEFAULT 'blocks'` stays.
3. `db.py` — add `_migrate_task_deps_v5(conn)` invoked from `_migrate` after `_migrate_v4`. Idempotent. Steps wrapped in `BEGIN`/`COMMIT` with `PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF` around the rebuild:
   - **Drop CHECK** if present: read `sql` from `sqlite_master` for `task_deps`. If contains `CHECK (dep_type`: rebuild table (CREATE `task_deps_new` matching `schema.sql`, `INSERT INTO task_deps_new SELECT * FROM task_deps`, `DROP TABLE task_deps`, `ALTER TABLE task_deps_new RENAME TO task_deps`).
   - **Migrate `needs`/`blocks` rows to active-voice `'blocks'` with swap:**
     ```sql
     UPDATE task_deps
     SET    source_id = target_id,
            target_id = source_id,
            dep_type  = 'blocks'
     WHERE  dep_type IN ('needs', 'blocks');
     ```
     SQL semantics evaluate all RHS expressions against the original row before any column is written, so source/target swap atomically.
   - **Fix latent `replaces` direction bug** (swap only, no rename — label is already correct):
     ```sql
     UPDATE task_deps
     SET    source_id = target_id,
            target_id = source_id
     WHERE  dep_type = 'replaces';
     ```
   - **Collision safety:** UNIQUE constraint could theoretically fire if two tasks were both linked in opposite directions (e.g. A blocks B AND B blocks A as separate rows). Migration runs in a transaction; if `IntegrityError` raised, abort with a clear error pointing the user to the backup at `~/.endless/backups/`.
4. New constant in `task_cmd.py` near top:
   ```python
   # display_name -> (stored_dep_type, swap_source_target)
   CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES = {
       "blocks":          ("blocks", False),         # source blocks target
       "blocked_by":      ("blocks", True),          # inverse view
       "implements":      ("implements", False),     # source implements target
       "implemented_by":  ("implements", True),
       "informs":         ("informs", False),        # source informs target
       "informed_by":     ("informs", True),
       "replaces":        ("replaces", False),       # source replaces target
       "replaced_by":     ("replaces", True),
       "relates_to":      ("relates_to", False),     # symmetric
   }
   ```

**Verification:** `sqlite3 ~/.endless/endless.db ".schema task_deps"` shows no CHECK. `SELECT DISTINCT dep_type FROM task_deps` returns subset of {blocks, implements, informs, replaces, relates_to}. Spot-check a known blocked task: row should have `source_id=blocker, target_id=blocked, dep_type='blocks'`. New migration test (Phase 7) passes.

## Phase 2 — Core `link_tasks` / `unlink_tasks` primitives

Files: `task_cmd.py`, `cli.py`

1. `task_cmd.py` — add `link_tasks(source_id, target_id, dep_type)`:
   - Validate `dep_type in CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES`; resolve to (stored, swap); if swap, swap source/target.
   - Insert. Reuse the UNIQUE-error message pattern from `add_dep`.
2. `task_cmd.py` — add `unlink_tasks(source_id, target_id, dep_type=None)`:
   - If `dep_type`: normalize via swap map; delete with strict `dep_type =` filter.
   - If not: query all rows between the pair (both directions), apply Issue 5 logic.
3. `task_cmd.py` — `add_dep` and `remove_dep` become thin shims that call `link_tasks(..., 'blocked_by')` / `unlink_tasks(..., 'blocked_by')`. The display name `'blocked_by'` resolves via the swap map to stored `'blocks'` with source/target swapped — so existing callers preserve their semantics. Delete the shims after Phase 3 completes.
4. `cli.py` — register `task_link` and `task_unlink` after line 731:
   ```python
   @task_cmd.command("link")
   @click.argument("source_id", type=TASK_ID)
   @click.option("--to", "target_id", type=TASK_ID, required=True)
   @click.option("--as", "dep_type",
                 type=click.Choice(list(CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES)),
                 required=True)
   ```
   `task_unlink` mirrors but `--as` is optional.

**Verification:** `endless task link E-1 --to E-2 --as blocks`; `endless task link E-1 --to E-2 --as relates_to`; `endless task unlink E-1 --to E-2 --as blocks`; `endless task unlink E-1 --to E-2` (errors when ambiguous).

## Phase 3 — Refactor `block`/`replace`/`unblock` into wrappers

Files: `cli.py`, `task_cmd.py`

1. `cli.py:700-703` — `task_block` body becomes `link_tasks(item_id, blocker_id, 'blocked_by')`.
2. `cli.py:720-723` — `task_unblock` body becomes `unlink_tasks(item_id, blocker_id, 'blocked_by')`.
3. `cli.py:710-713` — `task_replace` keeps its compound shape (relation + status change).
4. `task_cmd.py:2119` — `replace_task(old_id, new_id)` calls `link_tasks(old_id, new_id, 'replaced_by')` instead of inline insert. (The display name `'replaced_by'` resolves to stored `'replaces'` with swap=True, so the row ends up `source=new_id, target=old_id, dep_type='replaces'` — reads correctly as "new replaces old".) Obsolete-status update stays. Preserve the specialized "already replaced by" error message; map `link_tasks`'s generic UNIQUE error here.
5. After verifying coverage, delete the `add_dep`/`remove_dep` shims.

**Verification:** existing shell history (`endless task block E-X --by E-Y`, `endless task replace E-X --by E-Y`) continues to work. `endless task deps` output unchanged for fixtures from before the migration.

## Phase 4 — Display: `task show` and `task deps`

Files: `task_cmd.py`

1. Replace `get_deps_for_display` (line 2266) with `get_all_relations(item_id) -> dict[str, list[row]]` keyed by display-name in the fixed Decision-7 order. One SQL: `SELECT td.dep_type, td.source_id, td.target_id, ... FROM task_deps td ... WHERE source_id = ? OR target_id = ?`. Bucket rows in Python via `(stored_dep_type, is_source)` → display-name lookup (inverse of CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES).
2. Rewrite `show_deps` (line 2198) to iterate the dict in fixed order. Render only non-empty groups.
3. Update `detail_item` at line 1656 to use `get_all_relations`.
4. Add a `relations` command in `cli.py` that mirrors `deps`. Update `deps` help text to note `relations` is the new name; keep `deps` working forever.
5. Update `task next` query at `task_cmd.py:721-727`. Today reads "exclude tasks where source is the current task and target is unconfirmed" (using passive `needs` storage). With active `blocks` storage, semantics flip to "exclude tasks where they appear as the *target* of a `blocks` edge from an unconfirmed source":
   ```sql
   AND t.id NOT IN (
       SELECT td.target_id FROM task_deps td
       WHERE td.target_type = 'task' AND td.dep_type = 'blocks'
         AND td.source_id IN (
             SELECT t2.id FROM tasks t2 WHERE t2.status != 'confirmed'
         )
   )
   ```

**Verification:** `endless task show E-957` and `endless task deps E-957` show grouped output with new dep_types. `endless task relations E-957` shows the same. `endless task next` still ranks correctly with respect to blockers.

## Phase 5 — At-creation flags on `task add` and `decision add`

Files: `task_cmd.py`, `cli.py`

1. `task_cmd.py:1170` — `add_item` returns `item_id`.
2. `cli.py:528-557` — add to `task_add`:
   - `--blocks` (multiple, TASK_ID) → `link_tasks(new_id, t, 'blocks')` for each
   - `--blocked-by` (multiple, TASK_ID) → `link_tasks(new_id, t, 'blocked_by')`
   - `--relates-to` (multiple, TASK_ID) → `link_tasks(new_id, t, 'relates_to')`
   - `--implements` (multiple, TASK_ID) → `link_tasks(new_id, t, 'implements')`
3. `cli.py:777-792` — add to `decision_add`:
   - `--about` (multiple, TASK_ID) → `link_tasks(decision_id, t, 'informs')` — stores "decision informs task" (active stored, no swap)
   - `--decides` (multiple, TASK_ID) → `link_tasks(t, decision_id, 'implements')` — stores "task implements decision" (active stored, no swap; note source/target order at the call site reflects who is acting)

**Verification:** `endless task add "T1" && endless task add "T2" --blocked-by E-<T1> && endless task show E-<T2>` shows `blocked_by` group. `endless decision add "D1" --about E-<T1> --decides E-<T2>` creates two relations correctly.

## Phase 6 — Reverse lookup on `task list`

Files: `cli.py:362-394` (`task_list`), `task_cmd.py:551-703` (`show_plan`)

1. Add CLI options `--related-to <id>` and `--rel-type <display_name>` to `task_list`.
2. In `show_plan`, when `related_to` provided: build a candidate-id set with a single SQL — `SELECT source_id, target_id, dep_type FROM task_deps WHERE source_id = ? OR target_id = ?` — and filter out the input id. If `rel_type` provided, normalize via `CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES` and filter rows by stored dep_type plus correct direction.
3. Intersect this id-set with the WHERE clause's existing parameterized query.

**Verification:** `endless task list --related-to E-957`; `endless task list --related-to E-957 --rel-type blocks`; `endless task list --related-to E-957 --rel-type implemented_by`.

## Phase 7 — Tests

New `/Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/tests/test_relations.py`. (Confirm test infra by reading existing `tests/conftest.py` and an existing `test_*.py` file.)

Cases:
- `test_link_unlink_roundtrip` per dep_type (parametrized)
- `test_inverse_pair_stored_once_queried_both_ways`
- `test_at_creation_flags_task_add` — covers `--blocks`, `--blocked-by`, `--relates-to`, `--implements`
- `test_at_creation_flags_decision_add` — covers `--about`, `--decides`
- `test_task_list_related_to`
- `test_task_show_renders_all_groups_in_fixed_order`
- `test_unique_constraint_friendly_error`
- `test_self_link_rejected`
- `test_unlink_ambiguous_requires_as`
- `test_migration_strips_check_swaps_and_renames` — seed a CHECK-constrained `task_deps` with `'needs'` rows (passive layout: source=blocked, target=blocker) and `'replaces'` rows (passive layout: source=old, target=new), run `_migrate`, assert: CHECK gone, `needs` rows now `dep_type='blocks'` with source=blocker/target=blocked, `replaces` rows still `dep_type='replaces'` but with source=new/target=old
- `test_legacy_block_command_still_works` — golden behavior for `task_block` shim
- `test_replace_command_still_sets_obsolete` — same for `replace_task`

**Run:** `just test` (per project CLAUDE.md).

## Risk callouts

- **CLI muscle memory** — `block`, `replace`, `unblock`, `deps` all keep working as shortcuts. No breaking change.
- **Existing scripts parsing `task deps` output** — output gains new sections (informs, implements, etc.). Grep the repo for any script piping `endless task deps`; update callers in this PR.
- **JSON / LLM exporters** — `detail_item` LLM output (lines 1599-1636) and JSON output (1566-1597) reference dep_types. Confirm both gain the new groups; don't break existing automation reading those formats.
- **`add_item` return type** — public-ish helper changes from `None` to `int`. Audit all callers (`import_json`, bulk-import path, any test fixture); the `_ = add_item(...)` style is fine but a positional-positional return-type assertion in tests would break.
- **Schema embed** — `internal/schema/schema.sql` is `//go:embed`-ed by `/internal/schema/schema.go`. Go-side consumers pick up the changed default value automatically; verify `internal/monitor/db.go:294` (which has its own inline `CREATE TABLE task_deps`) is consistent or scheduled for E-894 migration.
- **Migration irreversibility** — once Phase 1's `UPDATE`s run, there's no rollback short of a backup restore. `_backup_db()` (db.py:39) auto-snapshots before any migration. Document in CHANGELOG. The `replaces` row swap is the higher-stakes one — if any external tooling reads `task_deps` directly and assumed the old (passive) `replaces` layout, it will break. Grep the repo (Python and Go) for any direct `replaces` SELECT before merging.
- **Old `replace_task` UNIQUE error message** — refactoring through `link_tasks` may produce a generic "already linked" message instead of "already replaced by". Preserve a specialized message in `replace_task`.

## Out of scope

- E-980 `--decision <text>` flag on `task add`/`task update` (auto-creates a decision and links it). Builds on this work but lives in its own task.
- E-977/E-978 `implements` link-type dedup decision — this plan ships `implements` as one of the six canonical types; if E-977/E-978 want a more specialized command on top, that's separate.
- Cross-project relation UX (project-prefixed IDs in flags). Schema already supports it; CLI sugar deferred.
- Graph visualization / ASCII tree of relations.
- Migrating `internal/monitor/db.go:294`'s inline CREATE — tracked under E-894.

## End-to-end verification (post-PR)

```
just test                                                   # all tests pass
endless task add "T1"
endless task add "T2" --blocked-by E-<T1> --relates-to E-<T1>
endless task show E-<T2>                                    # shows blocked_by + relates_to groups
endless task link E-<T1> --to E-<T2> --as implements
endless task list --related-to E-<T1> --rel-type implemented_by
endless task unlink E-<T1> --to E-<T2>                      # errors: ambiguous
endless task unlink E-<T1> --to E-<T2> --as implements
endless task block E-<T1> --by E-<T2>                       # legacy shortcut
endless decision add "D1" --about E-<T1> --decides E-<T2>
endless task next                                           # blockers respected
sqlite3 ~/.endless/endless.db "SELECT DISTINCT dep_type FROM task_deps"
                                                            # only canonical 5: blocks, implements, informs, replaces, relates_to
sqlite3 ~/.endless/endless.db "SELECT source_id, target_id, dep_type FROM task_deps LIMIT 5"
                                                            # spot-check: source is the actor in active voice
```

payload.title: Add session-to-pane sidecar so sibling panes can identify the active Claude session deterministically
payload.description:
Problem: a sibling tmux pane (or a separate terminal/window) needs to know which Claude session is running in another pane to do useful things — cd into its worktree, view its history, show its bound task. Today there is no reliable way:

- lsof on the claude PID does not show the session JSONL (claude appends-and-closes), and lsof is unavailable on Windows.
- "Newest *.jsonl in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/" works heuristically but is ambiguous when two claude processes share a cwd, and depends on Claude's path-encoding scheme staying stable.
- Process-tree walks (tmux pane_pid -> claude child) are portable but only get us as far as the PID; there is still no deterministic PID -> Claude session UUID link without one of the above heuristics.

Solution: deterministic sidecar written by a SessionStart hook and removed by SessionEnd.

- Path: .endless/sessions/active-<key>.json (per project; key TBD — pane_id when running under tmux, otherwise something stable like ppid or terminal id).
- Contents: { claude_session_uuid, endless_session_id, pane_id_or_null, cwd, pid, started_at, worktree_path_or_null }.
- Lifecycle: SessionStart writes it; SessionEnd removes it; stale entries (pid not alive) cleaned lazily on read.
- Read path: any sibling enumerates sidecars in this project's .endless/sessions/, filters to "not me", and either auto-selects (one match) or asks (multiple).

Cross-platform constraints:
- No lsof, no /proc walking. Pure filesystem + whatever the terminal multiplexer exposes via env (TMUX, TMUX_PANE on tmux; nothing required on plain terminals — sidecar still works, just no sibling auto-discovery).
- Must work on Windows eventually; sidecar approach is portable, the only OS-specific bit is liveness check (kill -0 vs OpenProcess).

Initial consumer: endless session cd [<id>] — default behavior in tmux is "cd this pane into the worktree of the *other* Claude pane in this window." Outside tmux, requires explicit id. Same sidecar later powers session show and any session-aware command that needs to default to "the session over there."

Verification:
- Two panes, two sessions, two worktrees: each writes a sidecar; `endless session cd` from a third pane disambiguates and lands in the right cwd.
- Kill claude with SIGKILL (no SessionEnd fires): next read prunes the stale sidecar.
- No tmux: sidecar still written; sibling-discovery falls back to "explicit id required."

Out of scope for this task:
- The endless session cd command itself (file as a follow-up once sidecar lands).
- Session history / show enhancements.
- Windows port (design must not preclude it; implementation can be macOS/Linux first).

payload.title: Add 'endless session cd' to switch the current shell into the worktree of another Claude session
payload.description:
Primary use case (short term): from a tmux pane that is NOT the Claude pane, get into the cwd / worktree of the Claude session running in a sibling pane so you can run tests, inspect state, or just look around without polluting Claude's working directory.

Resolution rules (in order):
1. Explicit: endless session cd <endless-id> — look up sidecar by endless_session_id, cd to its cwd (or worktree_path if set).
2. Tmux sibling: in tmux with a single sibling Claude pane, auto-resolve to that one. Multiple siblings → list and require disambiguation.
3. Otherwise: error with the list of sidecars so the user can retry with an explicit id.

Implementation notes:
- A child process cannot change its parent shell's cwd. The command therefore prints the target path to stdout and the user wraps it in a shell function (e.g. escd() { cd \"\$(endless session cd \"\$@\")\"; }). Document the function; consider shipping an 'endless shell-init' that prints it.
- Alternative for tmux: when invoked under tmux, optionally tmux send-keys a cd into the calling pane. Cleaner UX but more magic; default off behind a flag.
- Reads sidecars from .endless/sessions/active-*.json (E-989). No DB read on the hot path.

Verification:
- Two panes, two sessions, two worktrees: from a third pane, 'endless session cd <id1>' lands in worktree 1; same for id2.
- One Claude pane in tmux: 'endless session cd' (no arg) resolves automatically.
- Two Claude panes, no arg: prints disambiguation list, exits non-zero.
- No tmux, no arg: errors with sidecar list.

Out of scope:
- Auto-cd on session start.
- Cross-window tmux discovery (sibling = same tmux window only, for v1).

payload.title: Add 'endless session show' to display details of the current or specified session
payload.description:
Today there is no concise way to see 'what is this session, what task is it bound to, where is its worktree, when did it start, how many messages.' 'session list' is global and noisy; 'session history' dumps messages. We need a focused per-session detail command — and crucially, one that defaults to the current session without requiring the user to look up a cryptic UUID prefix.

Behavior:
- 'endless session show' (no arg) → resolve current session via the sidecar (E-989). In tmux, 'current' means the Claude pane in this window if there is exactly one, else error and list candidates. Outside tmux, 'current' means the sidecar whose pid matches a claude ancestor of the calling shell — fall back to error if ambiguous.
- 'endless session show <id>' → explicit lookup by endless integer id or by Claude UUID prefix.

Output (text mode):
- Endless id, Claude UUID, project, state, started_at, last_activity, message count
- Bound task (id + title) if one is active
- Worktree path + branch + dirty/clean if a worktree is bound
- Most recent summary, flattened to one line (we already do this for 'session list')

Add --json for scripting.

Verification:
- From inside a Claude pane: 'endless session show' returns its own session.
- From a sibling pane in tmux: 'endless session show' returns the Claude session in this tmux window.
- Two Claude panes in window: errors with disambiguation list.
- Explicit id always works regardless of context.

payload.title: Update 'endless session history' to default to the current session via sidecar
payload.description:
Currently 'endless session history' requires an explicit session id or Claude UUID prefix, both of which are cryptic and forgettable. Once E-989 lands, 'current session' can be resolved deterministically from the sidecar, so the default should be to show history for the current session.

Resolution mirrors 'session show':
- No arg: resolve via sidecar — calling shell's claude ancestor, or sole claude pane in this tmux window. Error with candidates if ambiguous.
- Explicit id/UUID: behaves as today.

Verification:
- Inside a Claude pane: 'endless session history' shows that session's messages.
- From a sibling pane in tmux with one Claude session: same result.
- Ambiguous (two Claude panes, no arg): errors with candidates.
- Explicit arg path: unchanged from current behavior.

Out of scope: redesigning the history output itself. This is purely about default resolution.

payload.title: Decide approach for clearing text/prompt fields without bypassing event log
payload.description:
**STATUS: UNRESOLVED. Needs discussion and explicit decision before implementation.**

## Background

During E-969 verification testing (2026-04-28), I needed to set E-979.text via 'endless task update --text <file>' to verify the per-task plan-file mirror, then restore E-979.text to its original NULL state. The CLI provides no path to clear a task's text:

- 'endless task update --text <path>' requires a file path; no clear flag.
- 'endless task clear' only supports the 'tier' subcommand.

I bypassed by running raw SQL: 'sqlite3 ~/.config/endless/endless.db "UPDATE tasks SET text = NULL WHERE id = 979;"'. This worked but violates the event-sourced architecture (E-808, commit c9425fb).

## The architectural violation

Endless is event-sourced. Every legitimate mutation flows:
  emit_event() -> writes JSONL line to .endless/events/events-*.jsonl  (FIRST, authoritative)
       |
       v
  SQL mutation against ~/.config/endless/endless.db  (SECOND, projection)

The event log is the source of truth; the DB is a derivable projection. The projection engine (E-807, commands 'endless validate-db' and 'endless rebuild-db') can rebuild the DB from the event log.

My raw SQL bypassed the event log. Result:
- DB says: E-979.text = NULL.
- Events log: last event for E-979 was 'task.fields_updated {text: "# Test plan content"}'.
- DISAGREE.

If 'endless rebuild-db' is run, it replays events and restores E-979.text to '# Test plan content'. My SQL change is a ghost write, gone on rebuild.

Severity in THIS instance: low. E-979.text being polluted with test content is harmless. But the precedent is dangerous: "raw SQL is fine when the API doesn't cover something." That erodes the events-authoritative invariant the rebuild engine depends on.

## Options

### A. Emit a synthetic clear event now (one-off fix, no API change)

Run emit_event(kind='task.fields_updated', entity_id=979, payload={'fields': {'text': None}}) directly from a Python repl or one-shot script. DB and log re-sync. Future rebuilds correct.

Pros: closes the immediate loop with no codebase changes.
Cons: doesn't add the missing CLI capability. Next person hitting the same need will be tempted to bypass again. Requires verifying the projection engine handles 'set field to null' correctly.

### B. Add 'endless task clear text' (and 'clear prompt') verb, no E-979 fix

Extend the existing 'task clear' group beyond just 'tier'. Implementation in src/endless/task_cmd.py: new clear_text(item_id) calls emit_event with fields={'text': None}. Same pattern for prompt and any other nullable fields.

Pros: adds the missing capability properly. Future cleanups go through the right API.
Cons: doesn't fix E-979's existing desync. Need to use the new verb (or option A) afterward.

### C. Restore E-979 to a non-null value via existing API

Run 'endless task update E-979 --text <some-file>' with non-empty content. Emits a proper event, DB and log sync.

Pros: closes the loop with existing API only.
Cons: end state has SOMETHING in text, not nothing. E-979.text was always meant to be empty for this task.

### D. Combine B + use B to fix E-979

Ship 'task clear text' (option B), then run 'endless task clear text E-979' to properly clear. Closes the loop AND adds the missing capability via the same mechanism.

Pros: best long-term hygiene. Rule-following: every change goes through events; the new verb is the right way to record the clear.
Cons: more work upfront. Probably one new task to file (this one).

### E. Accept the desync, do nothing

Live with it. If rebuild-db ever runs, the test content reappears, someone clears it then.

Pros: zero work.
Cons: bad precedent. Erodes the events-authoritative invariant.

## Claude's recommendation (Mike has not yet decided)

Option D. Adds the missing capability and fixes the existing desync via the same mechanism. The 'task clear' group is the natural home -- extending it from 'tier' to 'tier|text|prompt|...' is a small, contained change.

## Open questions for the discussion

1. Which option (A, B, C, D, or E)?
2. If B or D: what's the full set of clearable fields? text, prompt, description, parent_id (already clearable via --parent 0)?
3. If B or D: should 'clear' subcommands accept multiple item_ids like other task subcommands?
4. Does the projection engine (E-807) handle null field updates correctly, or does it silently coerce nulls to '' or skip them? (Should be verified before option A or D.)

## File pointers

- src/endless/task_cmd.py:1427 -- update_plan, where --text logic lives.
- src/endless/cli.py:603 -- task_clear group definition.
- src/endless/event_bridge.py:16 -- emit_event signature.
- internal/events/writer.go -- event log writer.
- cmd/endless-event/main.go -- event-sourced mutation entry point.

fields.description:
Problem: a sibling tmux pane (or a separate terminal/window) needs to know which Claude session is running in another pane to do useful things — cd into its worktree, view its history, show its bound task. Today there is no reliable way:

- lsof on the claude PID does not show the session JSONL (claude appends-and-closes), and lsof is unavailable on Windows.

- Process-tree walks (tmux pane_pid -> claude child) are portable but only get us as far as the PID; there is still no deterministic PID -> Claude session UUID link without one of the above heuristics.

Solution: deterministic per-session companion file written by a SessionStart hook and removed by SessionEnd.

- Path: .endless/sessions/claude-<claude_session_uuid>.json — per-agent prefix so future agent harnesses (e.g. codex-<id>.json) coexist in one flat directory without rename churn.

- No lsof, no /proc walking. Pure filesystem + whatever the terminal multiplexer exposes via env (TMUX, TMUX_PANE on tmux; nothing required on plain terminals — companion file still works, just no sibling auto-discovery).
- Must work on Windows eventually; companion-file approach is portable; the only OS-specific bit is liveness check (kill -0 vs OpenProcess).

Open design item (not in scope here, but flag it on the plan): how the file gets written for non-Claude harnesses that may lack SessionStart-equivalent hooks. Not blocking v1.

- Windows port (design must not preclude it; implementation can be macOS/Linux first).

'session list' is global and noisy; 'session history' dumps messages. We need a focused per-session detail command — and crucially, one that defaults to the current session without requiring the user to look up a cryptic UUID prefix.

In tmux, 'current' means the Claude pane in this window if there is exactly one, else error and list candidates. Outside tmux, 'current' means the companion file whose pid matches a claude ancestor of the calling shell — fall back to error if ambiguous.
- 'endless session show <id>' → explicit lookup by endless integer id or by Claude UUID prefix.

Output (text mode):
- Endless id, Claude UUID, project, state, started_at, last_activity, message count
- Bound task (id + title) if one is active
- Worktree path + branch + dirty/clean if a worktree is bound
- Most recent summary, flattened to one line (we already do this for 'session list')

Add --json for scripting.

Verification:
- From inside a Claude pane: 'endless session show' returns its own session.
- From a sibling pane in tmux: 'endless session show' returns the Claude session in this tmux window.
- Two Claude panes in window: errors with disambiguation list.
- Explicit id always works regardless of context.

Currently 'endless session history' requires an explicit session id or Claude UUID prefix, both of which are cryptic and forgettable. Once E-989 lands, 'current session' can be resolved deterministically from the companion file, so the default should be to show history for the current session.

payload.title: Reconcile snapshotPlanFile error handling against fatal-on-error criteria
payload.description:
While building E-989 we articulated when non-fatal-on-error is justified:

1. Opportunistic / best-effort by design (diagnostics, telemetry).
2. Self-healing on a later event (e.g. BackfillProcess retries on UserPromptSubmit).
3. High expected error rate under normal operation — would be noisy without informing the user.

snapshotPlanFile in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:538 currently swallows errors with 'log.Printf' and returns nil to the caller. Audit whether that fits criterion (1) — losing one plan snapshot is genuinely tolerable — or whether it actually matters because downstream features (plan import, plan attach, drift detection) silently break when snapshots are missing.

If (1) holds: leave it, document the rationale in a comment so the next reader doesn't second-guess.
If not: convert to fatal, matching InitSession / SetProcess / WriteCompanion (E-989).

Out of scope: a sweep of every other 'log.Printf("...: %v", err)' site in the hook — file follow-ups if the audit finds more.

payload.title: Design sandbox/fixture pattern for tests that mutate state
payload.description: Tests and verification work that mutate Endless state must run against an isolated sandbox/fixture (separate event log + DB), never against the live system. Today there is no such pattern, which forces verification work to either touch live data and require cleanup (creating the temptation for raw-SQL bypass that produced the E-979 desync — see declined E-993) or to skip mutation testing entirely. This task is BIG and needs its own planning session: scope includes how a sandbox is provisioned (temp dir? in-memory SQLite? cloned event log?), how it is selected (env var? CLI flag? context manager?), how event-sourcing invariants are preserved within the sandbox, and what the developer/agent ergonomics look like. No implementation until plan is approved. A follow-up task to add 'task clear text' / 'task clear prompt' verbs should be filed AFTER this lands — sandboxes must exist before agents get a tool that makes 'mutate live then reset' easier.

fields.description: DECLINED 2026-04-29 (session 249). The premise was wrong. The E-979 desync wasn't caused by a missing 'task clear text' verb — it was caused by running verification mutations against the live DB and then needing to undo them. The right fix is sandboxed test fixtures (see E-995), not adding a CLI verb that makes 'mutate live then reset' easier. Original analysis preserved in event log. A separate task to add 'task clear text/prompt' may be filed later, but ONLY after E-995 lands — giving agents a clear verb before sandboxes exist normalizes the wrong workflow.

payload.title: Fix go.mod replace paths to work inside .endless/worktrees
payload.description:
Worktrees under .endless/worktrees/<id>/ break Go builds because go.mod uses 'replace github.com/mikeschinkel/X => ../go-pkgs/X', which is relative to the go.mod's location. From a worktree the path resolves to .endless/worktrees/go-pkgs/X, which doesn't exist.

Workaround in use today: a one-shot symlink at .endless/worktrees/go-pkgs -> /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/go-pkgs makes 'just build' and 'go test' succeed inside any e-NNN worktree. This was created during E-989 implementation. The symlink is outside any worktree's tree (it sits in .endless/worktrees/ alongside the e-NNN dirs) so no .gitignore change is needed and it is invisible to all worktrees.

Decide a durable answer:

Options:
1. Keep the symlink permanently. Document in worktree setup notes (when E-971 lands, that doc should call it out). Lowest cost; assumes Mike's go-pkgs lives at a stable path.
2. Use Go workspace files (go.work) with absolute paths or an env-driven path. Requires every dev to maintain go.work but doesn't depend on directory layout.
3. Vendor go-pkgs into the endless repo (vendor/ or a submodule). Heaviest but eliminates the path coupling entirely.
4. Replace 'replace ../go-pkgs/X' with module-proxy versions (publish go-pkgs releases). Cleanest for shared codebases; requires release discipline upstream.

Recommendation pending: depends on whether go-pkgs is intended to stay co-developed (favors option 2) or stabilize as published deps (favors option 4).

Verification (whichever option lands): from .endless/worktrees/<id>, 'just build' and 'go test ./...' both succeed without the manual symlink.

Out of scope: the symlink itself is fine for now. This task is about replacing it.

fields.description:
**STATUS: UNRESOLVED. Needs discussion and explicit decision before implementation.**

## Background

During E-969 verification testing (2026-04-28), I needed to set E-979.text via 'endless task update --text <file>' to verify the per-task plan-file mirror, then restore E-979.text to its original NULL state. The CLI provides no path to clear a task's text:

- 'endless task update --text <path>' requires a file path; no clear flag.
- 'endless task clear' only supports the 'tier' subcommand.

I bypassed by running raw SQL: 'sqlite3 ~/.config/endless/endless.db "UPDATE tasks SET text = NULL WHERE id = 979;"'. This worked but violates the event-sourced architecture (E-808, commit c9425fb).

## The architectural violation

Endless is event-sourced. Every legitimate mutation flows:
  emit_event() -> writes JSONL line to .endless/events/events-*.jsonl  (FIRST, authoritative)
       |
       v
  SQL mutation against ~/.config/endless/endless.db  (SECOND, projection)

The event log is the source of truth; the DB is a derivable projection. The projection engine (E-807, commands 'endless validate-db' and 'endless rebuild-db') can rebuild the DB from the event log.

My raw SQL bypassed the event log. Result:
- DB says: E-979.text = NULL.
- Events log: last event for E-979 was 'task.fields_updated {text: "# Test plan content"}'.
- DISAGREE.

If 'endless rebuild-db' is run, it replays events and restores E-979.text to '# Test plan content'. My SQL change is a ghost write, gone on rebuild.

Severity in THIS instance: low. E-979.text being polluted with test content is harmless. But the precedent is dangerous: "raw SQL is fine when the API doesn't cover something." That erodes the events-authoritative invariant the rebuild engine depends on.

## Options

### A. Emit a synthetic clear event now (one-off fix, no API change)

Run emit_event(kind='task.fields_updated', entity_id=979, payload={'fields': {'text': None}}) directly from a Python repl or one-shot script. DB and log re-sync. Future rebuilds correct.

Pros: closes the immediate loop with no codebase changes.
Cons: doesn't add the missing CLI capability. Next person hitting the same need will be tempted to bypass again. Requires verifying the projection engine handles 'set field to null' correctly.

### B. Add 'endless task clear text' (and 'clear prompt') verb, no E-979 fix

Extend the existing 'task clear' group beyond just 'tier'. Implementation in src/endless/task_cmd.py: new clear_text(item_id) calls emit_event with fields={'text': None}. Same pattern for prompt and any other nullable fields.

Pros: adds the missing capability properly. Future cleanups go through the right API.
Cons: doesn't fix E-979's existing desync. Need to use the new verb (or option A) afterward.

### C. Restore E-979 to a non-null value via existing API

Run 'endless task update E-979 --text <some-file>' with non-empty content. Emits a proper event, DB and log sync.

Pros: closes the loop with existing API only.
Cons: end state has SOMETHING in text, not nothing. E-979.text was always meant to be empty for this task.

### D. Combine B + use B to fix E-979

Ship 'task clear text' (option B), then run 'endless task clear text E-979' to properly clear. Closes the loop AND adds the missing capability via the same mechanism.

Pros: best long-term hygiene. Rule-following: every change goes through events; the new verb is the right way to record the clear.
Cons: more work upfront. Probably one new task to file (this one).

### E. Accept the desync, do nothing

Live with it. If rebuild-db ever runs, the test content reappears, someone clears it then.

Pros: zero work.
Cons: bad precedent. Erodes the events-authoritative invariant.

## Claude's recommendation (Mike has not yet decided)

Option D. Adds the missing capability and fixes the existing desync via the same mechanism. The 'task clear' group is the natural home -- extending it from 'tier' to 'tier|text|prompt|...' is a small, contained change.

## Open questions for the discussion

1. Which option (A, B, C, D, or E)?
2. If B or D: what's the full set of clearable fields? text, prompt, description, parent_id (already clearable via --parent 0)?
3. If B or D: should 'clear' subcommands accept multiple item_ids like other task subcommands?
4. Does the projection engine (E-807) handle null field updates correctly, or does it silently coerce nulls to '' or skip them? (Should be verified before option A or D.)

## File pointers

- src/endless/task_cmd.py:1427 -- update_plan, where --text logic lives.
- src/endless/cli.py:603 -- task_clear group definition.
- src/endless/event_bridge.py:16 -- emit_event signature.
- internal/events/writer.go -- event log writer.
- cmd/endless-event/main.go -- event-sourced mutation entry point.
fields.text:
# Resolution: Declined 2026-04-29 (session 249)

The premise of this task was wrong. The E-979 desync I created during E-969 verification was not caused by a missing `task clear text` verb. It was caused by running verification mutations directly against the live event log + DB and then needing to undo them.

Adding a CLI verb that makes "mutate live then reset" easier would normalize the wrong workflow — agents would reach for it whenever a verification run leaves crud behind, instead of reaching for an isolated test environment.

## What was actually wrong

Two unstated assumptions in the original framing, both of which need to be answered before the "missing verb" question is even meaningful:

1. **Should writes ever bypass the event log to avoid having to redo work?** No. The event log is authoritative; the DB is a projection. The moment a "small" bypass is allowed, `rebuild-db`'s correctness guarantee is destroyed because real state lives outside the log. There is no principled line between "OK bypass" and "not OK bypass."

2. **Should tests run against live data and then clean up?** No. Tests and verification work that mutate state belong in an isolated sandbox/fixture (separate event log + separate DB). Cleanup of live state is the wrong shape — it creates exactly the temptation that produced the E-979 desync in the first place.

## Replacement work

E-995 — Design sandbox/fixture pattern for tests that mutate state. Big task, needs its own planning session before implementation.

A `task clear text` / `task clear prompt` verb may still be filed later on its own merits (legitimate user need to null out a field), but it must wait until E-995 lands. Giving agents a clear verb before sandboxes exist normalizes "mutate live then reset," which is the exact anti-pattern that triggered this whole thread.

fields.title: Add session-to-pane companion file so sibling panes can identify the active Claude session deterministically

payload.title: Verify E-989 companion file is written on real Claude SessionStart
payload.description: Spawn target for end-to-end verification of E-989. Reads its own .endless/sessions/claude-*.json and reports.

fields.prompt:
You are spawned to verify the E-989 companion-file mechanism.

Do exactly this and nothing else:

1. Run: `ls -la .endless/sessions/`
2. Find the file matching `claude-*.json` — it should exist for your own session.
3. Run: `cat .endless/sessions/claude-*.json`
4. State whether all expected fields are present: `harness`, `harness_session_id`, `endless_session_id`, `pane_id`, `cwd`, `pid`, `started_at`.
5. Print the literal string: `E989_VERIFICATION_OK` if all fields are present, otherwise `E989_VERIFICATION_FAILED` with reasons.
6. Stop. Do not start any tasks. Do not edit any files. Do not run anything else.

payload.title: Add per-worktree .claude/settings.json so spawned sessions test the worktree's hook
payload.description:
Problem: today, exercising new hook code in a real Claude session requires temporarily repointing the global ~/.local/bin/endless-hook symlink at the worktree's bin/endless-hook. This is risky: every other live Claude session on the machine runs the unverified binary for the duration of the swap. We hit this during E-989 verification.

Claude Code has no CLI flag or env var to point at a custom settings file (confirmed against the official 4-tier precedence model: Managed → Command line → Local → Project → User; none accept a path override). However, project-level .claude/settings.json takes precedence over ~/.claude/settings.json for hook configuration. That gives us a clean lever inside a worktree.

Proposal: when endless creates a worktree (or as part of an explicit 'endless worktree prep-test' command), generate a .claude/settings.json under the worktree root that overrides the hook command path to the worktree's bin/endless-hook. Any Claude session spawned with cwd inside that worktree picks up the override automatically; sessions outside the worktree are unaffected.

Verification:
- From a worktree with the override settings file: spawn Claude there; SessionStart writes via the worktree's binary; main checkout's hook is untouched.
- Concurrent main-checkout Claude session: continues using its own hook unaffected.
- Worktree removed: the override is removed with it.

Open design items:
- Generated vs hand-edited: should the file be git-ignored (per-worktree generated) or committed as a template? Probably ignored — the absolute path to bin/endless-hook is worktree-specific.
- Should the hook binary be auto-rebuilt before the override is wired up, or assumed already built? Probably auto-build on the first override invocation.
- Interaction with E-971's worktree subsystem: this should land as part of that workstream rather than a standalone command, if the timing aligns.

Origin: E-989 verification round-trip. Filed because the symlink-swap workaround is unacceptable as a recurring pattern.

payload.title: Design field separation for plan content vs analysis content on tasks
payload.description:
The tasks.text column is conventionally treated as 'the plan' (see comments in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go referencing --text <plan-file>; E-971's planned plan-bearing detector keys off plan presence on tasks). But there is no field for problem analysis, design briefs, research notes, decision rationale, or postmortems — content that is long-form but is not a plan.

Today the hook does not read tasks.text for any behavioral decision (GetActiveTasks selects only description), so the overloading is not yet causing concrete misbehavior. However, the moment E-971's plan-bearing detection lands, anything stored in text will be interpreted as 'this task is planned' — which would mark mere analysis as planned and bypass actual planning.

Decide: how should non-plan long-form content live on a task?

Options:
1. Rename text -> plan AND add a separate analysis field (or notes / brief). Cleanest semantics; one field, one purpose. Cost: schema migration + CLI churn.
2. Rename text -> plan AND keep description as the home for everything between 'one paragraph' and 'a full plan'. Cost: description becomes unbounded; task show summaries get noisy (the original problem we hit on E-989).
3. Keep text as is but document the convention 'text = plan, full stop' and refuse to write analysis there. Cost: discipline-only; no enforcement.

Recommendation pending: option 1. Tradeoff: schema migration cost up front, paying off as soon as plan-bearing detection lands.

This is the design decision; the rename itself is filed separately so they can land in order. Origin: E-989 finalization revealed the gap.

payload.title: Rename tasks.text column and CLI flag to tasks.plan
payload.description:
Implement the rename decision from the field-separation design task: tasks.text -> tasks.plan throughout the schema, ORM, CLI, hook references, and templates.

Surface area:
- DB column rename via ALTER TABLE (schema.sql + migration logic in internal/monitor/db.go).
- CLI flags on task add / task update / task show: --text -> --plan; --text-only display flag stays consistent.
- task show: '## Text' heading -> '## Plan'.
- Hook comment references in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go.
- Web templates (internal/web/pages/task_detail.templ etc.).
- Plan attachment commands: 'endless task update <id> --text <plan-file>' becomes '--plan <plan-file>' (or --plan-file per the file-flag convention task).

Out of scope:
- The decision to separate analysis from plan (parent design task — file-separation).
- File-flag naming convention (separate task — applies orthogonally).

Verification:
- task show on a task with non-empty plan still renders the plan section.
- Existing scripts using --text either continue working via alias or fail loudly. Decide alias vs hard-break in the field-separation design.
- Web task-detail page renders plan content.

Origin: E-989 finalization revealed text-as-plan overloading.

payload.title: Refactor file-loading CLI flags to use --xxx-file convention consistently
payload.description:
Today some flags carry inline values (--description <text>) and others carry file paths despite the flag name implying inline content (--text <file>, --prompt <file>). This is inconsistent across commands and forces the user to remember which is which.

Proposal: any flag whose value is a file path uses --xxx-file. The bare --xxx flag carries inline content. Apply uniformly across all endless commands.

Examples (post-change):
- endless task update --text 'short inline text'
- endless task update --text-file path/to/long.md
- endless task add --prompt-file prompts/foo.md
- endless plan update --text-file .endless/plans/X.md

Surface area:
- CLI parsing: src/endless/cli.py and the task_cmd / plan_cmd handlers.
- Help text and error messages.
- README, in-repo docs, hook references.

Decisions to make:
- Allow auto-detect (path-exists -> file, else inline)? Recommend NO — brittleness outweighs convenience.
- Migration window with both names accepted, or hard cutover? Endless is pre-1.0 and 'move fast' applies; recommend hard cutover in one shot.

Verification:
- All existing flag usages in src/, cmd/, docs/, README.md updated.
- Help output for affected commands shows the split (--xxx for inline, --xxx-file for path).
- A grep for --text / --prompt across the codebase finds no leftovers.

Origin: E-989 finalization. Independent of the text-vs-plan rename and the field-separation design — applies to whichever flag names survive.

payload.title: Remove 'informs'/'informed_by' dep_type from canonical vocabulary
payload.description: Drop informs/informed_by from CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES in task_cmd.py. Update 'decision add --about' to default to relates_to instead. Update E-958's vocabulary text. Update tests/test_relations.py to remove informs assertions. Reinstall via 'uv tool install --reinstall .'

payload.title: Drop 'informs'/'informed_by' dep_type — too vague to ever reach for
payload.description:
Decision (2026-04-29): the 'informs' dep_type added in E-958's locked vocabulary is being removed because the verb is too non-specific. Mike's reaction: 'I cannot imagine myself ever using the task link command and saying that one informs the other.' Per the agent-reach principle (feedback_cli_naming_agent_friction.md) — vocabulary that goes unused becomes noise; if a human won't reach for it, an agent won't either except by accident.

Remaining canonical dep_types: blocks/blocked_by, implements/implemented_by, replaces/replaced_by, relates_to. The 'soft decision↔task' use case that informs was meant to cover folds into relates_to (the symmetric catch-all) when implements is too strong.

Cascading changes (tracked under E-1002): CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES map entry removal; 'decision add --about' default flips from informs to relates_to; E-958's text updated; tests updated.

fields.text:
Decision: standardize task relation vocabulary so the general-purpose linking CLI (E-957) can land without churn.

## Decided (2026-04-28; updated 2026-04-29)

**Verb:** `link` is the canonical command for creating typed relationships between tasks.

**Syntax:** `endless task link <source> --to <target> --as <type>` (and inverse `endless task unlink ...`).

**`block` is a shortcut, not a separate verb.** `endless task block <id> --by <other>` remains as a thin wrapper that calls `link --as blocked_by` under the hood. Same applies to other inverse-paired shortcuts (e.g. `replace`).

**Canonical dep_types (4 stored + inverse views):**
- `blocks` / `blocked_by`
- `implements` / `implemented_by` — link impl task to its decision
- `replaces` / `replaced_by`
- `relates_to` — symmetric catch-all

**Storage convention:** active-voice. A row stores `source=actor, target=acted_upon, dep_type=<active_verb>`. So `dep_type='blocks'` reads "source blocks target". Inverses (`blocked_by`, `implemented_by`, `replaced_by`) are query-time views — same row, source/target swapped.

**Schema:** `UNIQUE(source_type, source_id, target_type, target_id, dep_type)` — multiple typed relations between the same ordered pair are allowed (e.g. A blocks B AND A relates_to B can coexist).

**Removed from initial vocabulary (2026-04-29, E-1003):**
- `informs` / `informed_by` — verb is too vague to ever reach for. Per agent-reach principle: vocabulary that goes unused becomes noise. The soft "decision affects task" use case folds into `relates_to`. The hard "task acts on decision" case is covered by `implements`. `decision add --about <task>` defaults to `relates_to` going forward.

**Deferred (not adopted):**
- `decides` / `decided_by`: `implements` covers the hard case; nothing left for it.
- `documents` / `documented_by`: deferred; not needed for current use cases.

## Why this matters

CLI flags hard-code these strings. Locking the vocabulary now prevents renaming churn once `task add --blocks <id>`, `task add --implements <id>`, etc. are wired up.

Schema is already permissive (CHECK constraints removed), so this is purely a UX/docs decision — no migration needed.

## Implements / unblocks

- E-957 (general-purpose relation CLI) — implements this decision.
- E-1002/E-1003 (drop `informs` from vocabulary) — refines this decision.
- E-977/E-978 (`implements` link type) — covered by the canonical set.
