Existing `E-NNN` task IDs remain unchanged in this PR. The migration to `ET-NNN` is filed as a follow-up Endless task to be run in a quiet window with no parallel sessions in flight (atomic rename across plan files, event log, schema display fields, and prose cross-references).

**Follow-up to file as an Endless task before this plan ships:**

> Title: Migrate task IDs `E-NNN` → `ET-NNN` for two-letter prefix consistency.
>
> Description: Once Session Focus lands with `EF-NNN`, the codebase has two ID conventions in flight (`E-` for tasks, `EF-` for focus). This task migrates tasks to `ET-NNN` so all entity types share the `E<Type>-NNN` shape. Must run in a quiet window: pause all sessions, run migration script (rename `.endless/plans/E-NNN.md` → `ET-NNN.md`, rewrite event log task references via translation pass, update display fields in `schema.sql`, regenerate any cached cross-references), verify `rebuild-db` round-trips, restart sessions. Acceptance: `endless task list` shows `ET-NNN` IDs; old `E-NNN` references in plan prose are not auto-rewritten (preserved as historical text); new task creation uses `ET-NNN`.

Filing this as a real task — not just a note in chat — is the only way to keep the phased plan from calcifying into permanent inconsistency.

---

## Sequencing notes

This depends on E-968 W3 (the `UserPromptSubmit` hook + matchers config) for the pivot clearance integration. The data model and CLI surface are independent and can land first; the hook integration follows once E-968 W3 ships. If desired, focus could land in two phases:

1. **Phase 1**: schema + files-as-truth + CLI + `SessionStart` nag + Layer 1 breadcrumb injection (no Layer 2 dependency).
2. **Phase 2**: Layer 2 clearance integration once E-968 W3 lands.

Phase 1 alone solves the "where are we now?" problem (the breadcrumb is the high-leverage piece). Phase 2 adds the deterministic-pivot-handling polish.

payload.title: Refresh companion file on every UserPromptSubmit instead of only when missing
payload.description:
Today the UserPromptSubmit handler in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go calls writeClaudeCompanion only when CompanionExists returns false (the E-1011 backfill semantic). That leaves the companion file vulnerable to drift any time the active_task_id changes through a path that doesn't trigger writeClaudeCompanion — for example when:

- A multi-command bash invocation contains 'endless task start E-NNN' but the hook's tool_input.command doesn't include the relevant fragment (observed during E-1027/E-1028 verification: the synthetic stdin test fired StartWorkSession correctly while the same command in a multi-command Bash tool invocation did not).
- A user edits sessions.active_task_id directly via sqlite3.
- Any future code path that mutates session state without going through claude.go's PostToolUse handlers.

Symptom: 'endless session use' cd's to a stale worktree path; 'session show' reports stale active task. Easy for a human to misattribute or shrug off.

Fix: change the UserPromptSubmit handler to always call writeClaudeCompanion (drop the CompanionExists guard). Cost: one atomic JSON write per prompt — sub-millisecond. Benefit: the companion file is at most one prompt out of date with the DB regardless of which intermediate hook events fire or miss.

Implementation:
- Replace the 'if !exists { write }' block with an unconditional write.
- writeClaudeCompanion already overwrites atomically (tempfile + rename) — no concurrency concern.
- Delete CompanionExists if no other caller remains; otherwise keep it.

Verification:
- Manually mutate sessions.active_task_id in DB. Submit any prompt. Companion file's worktree_path updates within that turn.
- Run 'endless task start E-NNN' through whatever bash form previously failed to fire the hook. Within one prompt, companion catches up.
- Existing companion-file tests still pass; the backfill behavior subsumes the new always-write behavior.

Origin: E-1027/E-1028 verification surfaced that the hook can miss task-state events depending on Bash tool_input format. Rather than chase that intermittent miss, kill the entire drift class. Mike's question after I 'flagged' it: 'how will we know when it recurs?' — we won't; better to make recurrence harmless.

payload.title: Rename remaining 'sidecar' references to 'companion file' across codebase and plans
payload.description:
Mike (2026-04-30) confirmed 'sidecar' is obsoleted in favor of 'companion file' (per the existing internal/monitor/companion.go convention). The E-971 foundation commit (e291de0) was authored with the right terminology, but several pre-existing references remain.

Files to update:

**Code:**
- cmd/endless-hook/claude.go lines 690, 713, 726, 732, 740 (snapshot-file metadata uses 'sidecar' in comments and a variable name; rename to 'companion' to match the convention)

**Plans:**
- .endless/plans/E-968.md (lines 140, 142, 146, 148, 157, 184, 213, 227, 228, 230 + 'Sidecar format' heading)
- .endless/plans/E-971.md (lines 39, 66, 82, 109, 139, 153, 154, 156, 176 + 'Sidecar format (JSON)' heading)
- .endless/plans/E-987.md (line 26 'Sidecar metadata for above')
- .endless/plans/E-969.md (lines 26, 47, 72)

Mechanical replacement: 'sidecar' -> 'companion file' or 'companion' depending on context (variable names use 'companion'; prose uses 'companion file'). 'Sidecar' (capitalized headings) -> 'Companion file'.

Naming details for clarity:
- The per-session companion file already exists: <project>/.endless/sessions/<harness>-<session-id>.json (E-989, internal/monitor/companion.go)
- The per-worktree companion file is the new one: <worktree>/.endless/worktree.json (E-971 foundation, planned full impl in subsequent layers)
- Both are 'companion files' for their respective entity (session vs worktree)

Verification: 'grep -rn sidecar' across the repo returns only matches in unrelated documentation that legitimately uses the dictionary word.

fields.text:
# E-787: Add `outcome` field and `task decline` verb

## Context

Endless tasks have terminal statuses (`confirmed`, `assumed`, `declined`, `obsolete`) but no field for capturing **why** a task ended that way. Today the answer lives in commit messages, chat transcripts, or nowhere at all — searchable retrieval is impossible. E-787 adds an `outcome TEXT` column, a `task decline` verb that requires a reason, and `--outcome` flags on the other terminal-status entry points.

The asymmetry — `--reason` is required on `decline` but `--outcome` is optional on `confirm`/`assume`/`replace` — is intentional. Confirming a task usually means "we tested it and it worked" (not informative to log). Declining a task is uncommon and the reason is rarely obvious without explanation; forcing capture exactly there is where the cost/value math lands.

E-787 was previously marked `declined` in error (the `declined` enum value half had shipped, the rest hadn't). Status is now `ready`, work resumes here.

### Decisions already locked with Mike

1. `--reason` on `task decline`, `--outcome` everywhere else. Both write to the same `outcome` column. Pave the cowpath: each verb gets the flag name agents instinctively type for it.
2. Outcome is REQUIRED whenever the resulting status is `declined`, regardless of entry point (`task decline`, `task update --status declined`, `task replace --status declined`). No back doors.
3. `task replace` gains optional `--status <s>` (default `obsolete`) and `--outcome <text>`.
4. `task update --outcome` works standalone or alongside `--status`. Emits an event like every other field update — that's the whole point of the architecture (this was the E-993 lesson).
5. Outcome is mutable via `task update --outcome` (event-sourced, not a frozen field).

### Decisions made by me, flagged here for review

6. **Event shape: extend `task.status_changed` with optional `Outcome`.** *(Revised during Phase 2 implementation. Original Decision #6 had this backwards — see below.)* All dedicated terminal-status verbs (`task decline`, `task confirm`, `task assume`) emit `task.status_changed` with an optional `outcome` field on the payload. The cascade semantics already built into `task.status_changed` are preserved; outcome applies to the root task only (per Decision #8). `update_plan` continues to use `task.fields_updated` for multi-field updates (which can include status); `replace_task` routes its status+outcome write through `update_plan`, so its output is `task.fields_updated`. **Original framing was wrong:** I'd planned to switch `complete_item`/`assume_item` from `task.status_changed` to `task.fields_updated`, but `task.fields_updated` has no cascade support — switching would have silently broken cascade. Extending `task.status_changed` keeps that purpose-built event focused on its purpose and adds outcome with minimal surface area (one struct field, two ~5-line write paths in executor.go and projector.go).

7. **`replace_task` event-emission fix is in scope.** Currently `replace_task` (task_cmd.py:2146-2179) does raw SQL — both the `task_deps` insert and the `UPDATE tasks SET status='obsolete'`. This violates the events-authoritative invariant we just reaffirmed in E-993. Adding `--outcome`/`--status` to it is impossible without going through events, so the status-write half MUST be fixed here. The `task_deps` insert (also raw SQL) is the same bug shape but lives in E-957's typed-link territory; I'm leaving it alone in this plan and will note it as a follow-up.

8. **Cascade + outcome:** when `task confirm --cascade --outcome "x"` is used, the outcome applies to the root task only. Descendants get cascaded confirm with no outcome. Matches existing cascade UX (only the root gets explicit attention).

9. **`task show --outcome` flag honors spec literally.** In default human mode, outcome renders only when `--outcome` is passed. In `--llm` and `--json` modes, outcome always renders when set (those modes already include all fields). Worth revisiting later — a one-line outcome on a terminal task is short enough to always display by default — but defer that to a follow-up.

10. **Validation enforced at the task_cmd impl layer**, not the CLI. `decline_item`, `update_plan`, `replace_task` each call a shared `_require_outcome_for_declined(status, outcome)` helper. Centralizes the rule so all entry points behave identically.

---

## Prerequisites

**E-1020 (precursor, blocking): Add `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` support to Python CLI.**

Currently `src/endless/config.py:6` hardcodes `Path.home() / ".config" / "endless"`. The Go side (`internal/monitor/db.go:21-28`) honors `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`; Python doesn't. This parity gap blocks isolated binary-level testing of Python CLI commands — without it, Phase 5 of E-787 cannot run against a copied DB and would silently mutate `~/.config/endless/endless.db`.

Scope of precursor:
- `src/endless/config.py`: read `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` env var first, fall back to `Path.home() / ".config"`. Mirror the Go-side logic in `internal/monitor/db.go:ConfigDir()`.
- Audit other `Path.home()` references in `src/endless/` to ensure all config-relative paths derive from `CONFIG_DIR` rather than re-deriving from `Path.home()`.
- pytest should still pass (the `isolated_env` fixture overrides `CONFIG_DIR` directly via monkeypatch, bypassing the env-var read path entirely).
- Manual verification: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/cfg-test ./bin/endless task list` should not touch `~/.config/endless/endless.db`.

Estimated size: ~30 lines. E-787 is now formally `blocked_by E-1020`. Also serves as concrete input to E-995's design — it's exactly the kind of isolation primitive E-995 will need to formalize.

### Decisions recorded (linked to E-787)

- E-1021 — Use `--reason` on `task decline` and `--outcome` elsewhere
- E-1022 — Require outcome at every entry point that lands a task in declined
- E-1023 — Use `task.fields_updated` for terminal-status writes; deprecate `task.status_changed` for new code
- E-1024 — Include `replace_task` event-emission fix in E-787 scope
- E-1025 — Apply cascade outcome to root task only, not descendants
- E-1026 — Enforce outcome validation at task_cmd impl layer, not CLI

---

## Implementation

### Phase 1 — Schema + Go projection/executor (must precede any event emission)

**`internal/schema/schema.sql`** (canonical, `//go:embed`-ed)
- Tasks table (lines 74-93): add `outcome TEXT` column. Place after `tier INTEGER` for diff readability.

**`src/endless/db.py`** (note: `_migrate_v5` was taken by E-957's task_deps active-voice migration since planning; using `_migrate_v6`)
- After `_migrate_v5` (the task_deps active-voice migration), add `_migrate_v6(conn)`:
  ```python
  def _migrate_v6(conn: sqlite3.Connection):
      """Schema v6: add outcome column to tasks (E-787)."""
      if _has_table(conn, "tasks") and not _has_column(conn, "tasks", "outcome"):
          conn.execute("ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN outcome TEXT")
          conn.commit()
  ```
- Wire into `_migrate()` orchestrator: add `_migrate_v6(conn)` after `_migrate_v5(conn)`.

**`internal/events/executor.go:258-262`** — `allowedFields` map: add `"outcome": "outcome"`.

**`internal/events/projector.go:225-229`** — `allowedFields` map: add `"outcome": "outcome"`.

**Critical:** executor REJECTS unknown fields (line 267), projector silently SKIPS them (line 234). Both maps must be updated *before* any event with `outcome` is emitted — otherwise live emit fails.

**`internal/web/data/types.go:68-84`** — add `Outcome string` to `TaskView` struct so the web UI can display it (web rendering itself is not in this plan; just the field availability).

**`internal/events/payload.go`** — `TaskFieldsUpdatedPayload` already supports arbitrary fields via `map[string]any`, no change needed. `TaskStatusChangedPayload` is unchanged (we're not extending it; new code uses `task.fields_updated`).

### Phase 2 — Python task_cmd implementations

**`src/endless/task_cmd.py`**

- New helper near top of the terminal-status section:
  ```python
  def _require_outcome_for_declined(status: str | None, outcome: str | None):
      if status == "declined" and not (outcome and outcome.strip()):
          raise click.ClickException(
              "An outcome is required when declining a task. "
              "Use --reason (on `task decline`) or --outcome to explain why."
          )
  ```

- New `decline_item(item_id: int, reason: str)`:
  - Validates reason is non-empty (handled at click layer too via `required=True`).
  - Emits ONE `task.fields_updated` with `{"status": "declined", "outcome": reason}`.
  - Mirrors structure of `complete_item` for output (cyan bullet, task title display).

- `complete_item` (lines 1314-1365):
  - Add `outcome: str | None = None` param.
  - Switch from `task.status_changed` to `task.fields_updated` with `{"status": "confirmed", **({"outcome": outcome} if outcome else {})}`.
  - Cascade: outcome applies only to root; descendants are cascaded via existing logic without outcome.

- `assume_item` (lines 1368-1419): same shape as `complete_item` change.

- `update_plan` (lines 1452-1551):
  - Add `outcome: str | None = None` param.
  - Add to fields-building block (around line 1538):
    ```python
    if outcome is not None:
        fields["outcome"] = outcome
        changed_names.append("outcome")
    ```
  - After all fields built, BEFORE emit_event, call `_require_outcome_for_declined(fields.get("status"), fields.get("outcome"))`. Note: `fields.get("outcome")` may be the existing DB value's stand-in if the user didn't pass `--outcome`; the rule only fires when user is *setting* status to declined without supplying outcome in this call.
  - Tier-1 auto-advance logic (line 1534) is unrelated; preserve.

- `replace_task` (lines 2146-2179) — fix to be event-sourced:
  - Add `status: str = "obsolete"` and `outcome: str | None = None` params.
  - Validate via `_require_outcome_for_declined(status, outcome)`.
  - Replace the `db.execute("UPDATE tasks SET status = 'obsolete', tier = 0 ...")` (lines 2171-2174) with a call to `update_plan(old_id, status=status, outcome=outcome)`. This routes the status/outcome write through `task.fields_updated` and the existing terminal-status tier-zero logic in the projector handles the `tier=0` automatically (projector.go:246-250 already does this).
  - The `task_deps` INSERT (lines 2158-2162) stays as raw SQL for now. It's a separate event-sourcing gap living in E-957's typed-link territory; flagging as a follow-up rather than expanding scope here.
  - Output message stays.

- `detail_item` (`task show` rendering, around lines 1570-1720):
  - Add `show_outcome: bool = False` param.
  - Modify the SELECT in this function (around line 1580-1584) to include `outcome` column.
  - JSON mode (lines 1593-1623): always include `outcome` when set.
  - LLM mode (lines 1626-1663): always echo `outcome=<value>` when set, after `status` line.
  - Human-readable (lines 1665-1720+): only show outcome if `show_outcome` is True OR if it's set and the task is in a declined status (we owe the user the "why" for declines specifically). Worth confirming with Mike but I'm planning the latter; if pushback, drop to `show_outcome only`.

### Phase 3 — CLI wiring

**`src/endless/cli.py`**

- Status enum at lines 28-29 and 544-545: no change (`declined` and `obsolete` already present).

- `task update` (lines 560-589): add `@click.option("--outcome", default=None, help="Outcome / reason for status (required if status=declined)")`. Pass through to `update_plan`.

- `task confirm` (lines 618-626): add `@click.option("--outcome", default=None, help="Outcome — what was confirmed")`. Pass to `complete_item`.

- `task assume` (lines 629-637): add `@click.option("--outcome", default=None, help="Outcome — what was assumed")`. Pass to `assume_item`.

- New `task decline` command, after `task assume`:
  ```python
  @task_cmd.command("decline")
  @click.argument("item_ids", type=TASK_ID, nargs=-1, required=True)
  @click.option("--reason", required=True, help="Why this task is being declined")
  def task_decline(item_ids, reason):
      """Decline one or more tasks (sets status=declined; reason required)."""
      from endless.task_cmd import decline_item
      for item_id in item_ids:
          decline_item(item_id, reason=reason)
  ```

- `task replace` (lines ~706-713): add `--status` (default `"obsolete"`, choices match terminal statuses) and `--outcome`. Pass both to `replace_task`.

- `task show` (lines 397-418): add `@click.option("--outcome", "show_outcome", is_flag=True, help="Show outcome field")`. Pass to `detail_item`.

### Phase 4 — Tests

**`tests/test_cli.py`** — new test functions following the existing `CliRunner` + `isolated_env` fixture pattern (conftest.py:13-76):

1. `test_task_decline_writes_outcome` — decline E-X with `--reason "x"`, assert status=declined and outcome="x" in DB.
2. `test_task_decline_requires_reason` — invoke without `--reason`, assert click error.
3. `test_task_confirm_with_outcome` — confirm + `--outcome`, assert outcome stored.
4. `test_task_confirm_without_outcome_still_works` — outcome remains NULL, status=confirmed.
5. `test_task_assume_with_outcome` — same shape as #3.
6. `test_task_replace_default_obsolete` — `task replace E-X --by E-Y`, assert E-X status=obsolete.
7. `test_task_replace_with_status_declined_requires_outcome` — `task replace E-X --by E-Y --status declined` (no outcome) — error.
8. `test_task_replace_with_status_declined_and_outcome` — same with `--outcome` — succeeds, status=declined, outcome set.
9. `test_task_update_status_declined_requires_outcome` — `task update E-X --status declined` (no outcome) — error.
10. `test_task_update_outcome_standalone` — `task update E-X --outcome "x"` — succeeds, outcome updated, status untouched.
11. `test_task_show_outcome_flag` — `task show E-X --outcome` displays outcome.
12. `test_task_show_llm_mode_includes_outcome_when_set` — `task show E-X --llm` always includes outcome line when set.
13. `test_event_log_records_outcome` — after decline, read the JSONL event log and assert the `task.fields_updated` event contains both `status: "declined"` and `outcome: "<reason>"`.
14. `test_rebuild_db_preserves_outcome` — set outcome, run `endless rebuild-db`, assert outcome survives.

   **Note:** test #14 invokes a Go subprocess (`endless rebuild-db`), and the in-process `monkeypatch.setattr` does NOT propagate to subprocesses. The test must explicitly set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (pointing to the pytest tmp_path's parent) in the `subprocess.run()` env before invoking the binary. This is contingent on Phase 4.5 confirming the Go side honors XDG; do not write this test until then.

### Phase 4.5 — Verify Go binaries respect isolation (spike, BEFORE writing test #14)

Read `cmd/endless-event/main.go:280-354` (`runRebuildDB`) and any other Go entry points that touch the DB. Confirm:

1. DB path comes from `monitor.DBPath()` (which reads `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`), not a hardcoded `~/.config/endless/`.
2. The temp/scratch DB used by `ProjectToTempDB` is derived from the configured root, not from `/tmp` with a fixed name that could collide between concurrent test runs.
3. No fallthrough hardcoded paths anywhere in the rebuild/validate/projection code paths.

If any of these fail, fix them BEFORE writing test #14 — without this verification, a test that subprocess-invokes `endless rebuild-db` could mutate `~/.config/endless/endless.db` despite the pytest fixture's monkeypatching.

This spike also serves as final confirmation that the precursor task (Python XDG support) was complete: invoke `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/spike ./bin/endless task list` against an empty config dir and confirm it does NOT touch the production DB.

### Phase 5 — Manual sanity verification against an isolated DB copy

**Prerequisites:**
- Precursor task complete (XDG support in Python CLI).
- Phase 4.5 spike passed.
- `just build` has produced a fresh `./bin/endless`.
- **Do NOT run `just install` yet.** Phase 5 uses `./bin/endless` directly to keep the production binary at `/usr/local/bin/endless` untouched until after verification.

Setup (run from inside the e-787 worktree):
```bash
cd /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-787
mkdir -p .test-isolated/endless
cp ~/.config/endless/endless.db .test-isolated/endless/endless.db
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$(pwd)/.test-isolated"
```

Verify isolation FIRST (before doing anything destructive):
```bash
./bin/endless task list | head -3   # should show tasks from the COPY, mtime of prod DB unchanged
stat -f "%Sm" ~/.config/endless/endless.db  # record mtime; check at end that it's unchanged
```

Then run sanity checks (all using `./bin/endless`, never installed `endless`):
- `./bin/endless task add "E-787 sanity test 1"` → returns ID (call it E-A).
- `./bin/endless task decline E-A --reason "outcome verification"` → succeeds.
- `./bin/endless task show E-A --outcome` → shows the reason.
- `./bin/endless task add "E-787 sanity test 2"` → returns E-B.
- `./bin/endless task update E-B --status declined` (no outcome) → fails with `_require_outcome_for_declined` error.
- `./bin/endless task update E-B --status declined --outcome "back-door test"` → succeeds.
- `./bin/endless task add "E-787 sanity test 3"` → returns E-C.
- `./bin/endless task add "E-787 replacement target"` → returns E-D.
- `./bin/endless task replace E-C --by E-D` → status=obsolete (default).
- `./bin/endless task add "E-787 sanity test 4"` → returns E-E.
- `./bin/endless task add "E-787 replacement target 2"` → returns E-F.
- `./bin/endless task replace E-E --by E-F --status declined` (no outcome) → fails.
- `./bin/endless task replace E-E --by E-F --status declined --outcome "replaced because X"` → status=declined.
- `./bin/endless task update E-A --outcome "amended reason"` → succeeds.
- `./bin/endless task show E-A --outcome` → shows the amended reason.
- `./bin/endless rebuild-db` → no errors; re-run `task show --outcome` on E-A, E-B, E-E to confirm outcomes survived.

Verify nothing leaked to prod:
```bash
stat -f "%Sm" ~/.config/endless/endless.db  # compare against recorded mtime — must be identical
```

Cleanup (from inside the e-787 worktree):
```bash
unset XDG_CONFIG_HOME
rm -rf .test-isolated
```

**Only after all checks pass against the copy, run `just install`** to deploy to `/usr/local/bin/`. The first invocation against the production DB will run `_migrate_v6` (the safe, idempotent `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN outcome TEXT`).

---

## Critical files (full list)

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — schema column add
- `src/endless/db.py:160-163, 349+` — migration registration + new `_migrate_v6`
- `internal/events/executor.go:258-262` — allowedFields
- `internal/events/projector.go:225-229` — allowedFields
- `internal/web/data/types.go:68-84` — TaskView struct
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py:1314, 1368, 1452, 2146, 1570` — terminal-status impls + detail rendering + new `decline_item` and `_require_outcome_for_declined`
- `src/endless/cli.py:397, 560, 618, 629, 706` — `task show`, `update`, `confirm`, `assume`, `replace` flag wiring + new `task decline`
- `tests/test_cli.py` — new tests

## Reusable existing helpers

- `_has_table` / `_has_column` (db.py:166, 174) — migration idempotency
- `emit_event` (event_bridge.py:16-26) — event emission signature
- `_resolve_project` (used throughout task_cmd.py) — project name resolution
- `validate_title` (used in `update_plan`) — unrelated, just preserving
- `_write_task_plan_file` (used in `update_plan`) — unrelated, preserving
- `task_id_display` — formatted ID rendering for output

## Out of scope / follow-ups

- Fix `task_deps` INSERT in `replace_task` to be event-sourced. Same bug shape as the status write but belongs in E-957's typed-link work.
- Web UI rendering of `outcome`. Field availability is in scope (TaskView struct); template work isn't.
- Reconsider whether `task show` should always display outcome by default in human mode (current plan: opt-in via `--outcome` per spec, default-on for declined tasks). If Mike wants always-on, simplify.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. Precursor task (Python XDG_CONFIG_HOME support) shipped first; confirmed working manually.
2. `just build` — confirm no compile errors (Go) and no Python import errors. **Do NOT `just install` yet.**
3. Phase 4.5 spike completed — Go binaries confirmed to honor isolation.
4. `just test` — confirm all new + existing tests pass (including the new tests #1-14, with test #14 honoring XDG via subprocess env).
5. Phase 5 manual sanity checks all pass against the copied DB; prod DB mtime unchanged.
6. Only NOW run `just install` to deploy. The first prod invocation runs `_migrate_v6` against `~/.config/endless/endless.db` — one idempotent `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`.
7. Post-deploy smoke: one `endless task show <some-existing-task>` against prod to confirm the deployed binary works.

## Notes for E-995 (sandbox/fixture pattern design input)

Keep this section updated during implementation. Friction points captured here become concrete design input when E-995 is planned.

Initial observations from PLANNING E-787 (will accumulate more during execution):

1. **Python/Go isolation parity gap.** Go honors `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (`internal/monitor/db.go:21-28`); Python `config.py:6` hardcodes `Path.home()`. Required filing a precursor task. E-995 should mandate a single source-of-truth for "where is config" that both languages consume — probably a small Go binary command like `endless config-paths` that Python shells out to, or a shared file-based config pointer.

2. **In-process monkeypatch vs subprocess isolation are incompatible.** pytest's `isolated_env` fixture monkeypatches Python module attributes — fine for in-process tests, useless for tests that invoke Go binaries via subprocess. E-995 should provide a unified isolation primitive both Python in-process AND subprocess-launched Go binaries opt into (likely env-var based, since that's the only thing that crosses process boundaries cheaply).

3. **No `endless sandbox` (or `just test-isolated`) for binary-level smoke.** Phase 5 setup/cleanup is hand-rolled bash. A reusable harness (`endless sandbox start <name> [--copy-from prod]`, `endless sandbox stop <name>`) would have made this trivial. Worth scoping into E-995.

4. **Manual cleanup is error-prone.** Phase 5 ends with `unset XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and `rm -rf .endless/worktrees/e-787/.test-isolated`. Easy to forget the unset and leave the shell pointing at a deleted path, leading to confusing errors on the next `endless` command. Auto-cleanup (e.g., trap-on-EXIT in a wrapper script, or a context-manager-style `endless sandbox` command) is safer.

5. **Verification of "no prod leak" is purely manual.** Phase 5 uses `stat` mtime check, which is brittle. E-995 should consider an explicit "this command CANNOT touch prod paths" assertion at sandbox boundaries — e.g., a wrapper that bind-mounts `~/.config/endless` read-only during sandbox runs.

6. **Migration safety against real prod schema vs fresh schema.** Phase 5 catches "does the new migration apply cleanly to a copy of prod?". The pytest fixture only catches "does it apply to a fresh schema?". These are different. E-995 should consider whether the standard test workflow includes a prod-copy migration spike (and a fixture for that) or relies on case-by-case manual setup.

7. **Installed binary is a uv-tool snapshot, not a symlink to src/.** `endless` is installed via `uv tool install` to `/Users/mikeschinkel/.local/share/uv/tools/endless/lib/python3.14/site-packages/endless/` — a snapshotted copy. Source changes in `src/endless/` don't reach the running binary until `uv tool reinstall`. E-995 should either (a) document this deploy-hop explicitly in test workflow guidance or (b) provide a `just dev-install` recipe that swaps the snapshot for a symlink to `src/` for development, restoring the snapshot on cleanup. Hit during E-1020 — the verification path required PYTHONPATH manipulation to test the source code without disturbing the installed binary.

payload.title: Fix focus event projection failing on missing session FK
payload.description: rebuild-db projects task events into a temp DB but skips session events, so focus events fail the session_id FK constraint when replayed. Add ensureSession() to projector.go (mirroring ensureProject) that creates a stub session row when a focus event references one not yet present. Surfaced during E-1031 minimal-slice testing: 'events: insert focus: constraint failed: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed'.

payload.title: Fix just install to refuse running from a git worktree
payload.description: just install has two globally-observable side effects (ln -sfn into /usr/local/bin/, uv tool install -e . --force) that hijack tooling state for every concurrent session when run from a git worktree. Detect a worktree via 'git rev-parse --git-dir' (worktree paths contain /worktrees/, main returns .git) and refuse with a helpful message that suggests local-invocation patterns (PATH/PYTHONPATH overrides). E-986 is the long-term per-worktree install fix; this is a stop-gap guard.

payload.title: Validate companion worktree_path exists before using it; fall back to cwd if not
payload.description:
The companion file's worktree_path can go stale between hook events: it's written from active_task_id at hook time, but the underlying worktree directory can be deleted (e.g. 'git worktree remove') with no hook signal. Readers (session use, session cd) then emit a 'cd <gone>' that fails.

Hit during E-1015 verification: ran 'endless task start E-1015' which set companion worktree_path = .endless/worktrees/e-1015. Then landed E-1015 (FF-merged, removed the worktree). Companion still pointed at e-1015. From a side pane, 'esu' eval'd 'cd /.../e-1015' and got 'no such file or directory'. Next UserPromptSubmit refreshed the companion correctly, but the failure had already happened.

E-1033 (refresh-every-UserPromptSubmit) doesn't cover this — side panes read between Claude's turns. Adding more writer refresh points scales poorly (would need a hook on every git worktree operation, every rmdir, etc.).

Fix on the reader side: in _target_path (src/endless/session_cmd.py), validate worktree_path with os.path.isdir(). If it doesn't exist, ignore it and fall back to cwd. One stat per read. Companion file stays as 'intent'; reader treats it as a hint, not a guarantee.

Affects:
- session_cmd._target_path (the single place where worktree-vs-cwd is chosen for cd)
- ENDLESS_WORKTREE_PATH emission in session_use_resolve (currently emits stale path; should emit '' when stale)

Verification:
- Companion has worktree_path pointing at a directory that exists → that path is used.
- Companion has worktree_path pointing at a deleted directory → reader falls back to cwd silently. No error, no warning (the fallback is the right answer).
- Companion has no worktree_path → unchanged behavior.

Origin: E-1015 verification revealed the gap. Manual workaround: send any prompt to current Claude to force a refresh. This task removes the foot-gun entirely.

payload.title: Reduce 'session use' activation to ENDLESS_SESSION_ID + cd; lookup-on-demand for the rest
payload.description:
Today 'endless session use' emits 5 env vars (ENDLESS_SESSION_ID, ENDLESS_HARNESS_SESSION_ID, ENDLESS_HARNESS, ENDLESS_PROJECT_ROOT, ENDLESS_WORKTREE_PATH) plus a cd. Only the integer id is canonical; the rest are derivable from it via DB. Snapshotting them into env saves an 'endless' lookup per consumer, but at the cost of staleness — exactly the class of bug E-1015 verification surfaced when the worktree was removed mid-turn.

Mike's call: minimal is right. A static value can never be stale if it's never written.

Activation block becomes:

  cd <target>
  export ENDLESS_SESSION_ID=<id>

Where <target> is the worktree path if its directory exists at lookup time, else cwd. Validation is at the point the cd line is emitted — narrow window, no value persisted.

Consumers that need other fields call 'endless session show <id> --json' (already exists, returns the full picture). One process spawn per lookup; trade for guaranteed freshness.

Extension contract changes:
- .endless/extensions/use.sh now receives only ENDLESS_SESSION_ID in its env.
- Extensions that need worktree path / project root / etc. call 'endless session show \$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID --json' and parse with jq.
- Documented as the contract; existing extensions need updating (none in the wild today).

Verification:
- 'endless session use 247' emits exactly two lines: cd and ENDLESS_SESSION_ID export.
- worktree_path at lookup time exists -> cd to it. Doesn't exist -> cd to cwd. Same logic E-1037 wanted.
- Extension's env contains ENDLESS_SESSION_ID, nothing else ENDLESS_* prefixed.
- 'endless session show \$ENDLESS_SESSION_ID --json' returns same fields as before.

Supersedes E-1037 (worktree_path validation alone is too narrow; the larger fix is the env minimization).

Origin: E-1015 verification + Mike's design call: 'It will never fail for a stale value.'

payload.title: Wire up remaining CLI verbs (child/pivot/update/close/reopen/goto)
payload.description: Build out the rest of Phase 1's CLI surface in the worktree, test via local PATH/PYTHONPATH invocation, then handle EF-1 cleanup as the close verb test case.

payload.title: Add worktree-local binary preference to endless CLI
payload.description: /usr/local/bin/endless-event is a single global symlink. When 'just install' runs from worktree A, all endless commands (run from anywhere) hit A's binary. If worktree B builds binaries with different Go code, B's bin/ is ignored — even commands run from inside B. This caused E-787 test failures (the conftest PATH override is a workaround). Possible approaches: (a) prepend ./bin/ to PATH in shell-init when in a worktree, (b) endless CLI auto-detects worktree-local bin/ and prefers it via shutil.which fallback, (c) just install per-worktree to alternative paths. Surfaced during E-787 worktree migration.

payload.title: Implement safeguard against uncommitted-work loss during cross-session worktree migration
payload.description: During E-787, another session moved their uncommitted work in main to a worktree and accidentally wiped my uncommitted Go-side files (schema.sql, executor.go, payload.go, projector.go, cli.py changes) at the same time. Recovery required restoring from conversation history. Possible solutions: (a) workflow rule via CLAUDE.md mandating 'start every non-trivial task in a worktree' so main never accumulates concurrent uncommitted state; (b) pre-flight backup hook before destructive cross-session ops on main (git stash with timestamp); (c) tooling that detects when main has uncommitted work belonging to other sessions and warns before destructive operations. Surfaced during E-787.

payload.title: Fix rebuild-db FK failures from pre-E-808 legacy tasks
payload.description: Tasks created BEFORE E-808 (events-authoritative architecture) have no task.created event in the log — they were inserted via raw SQL during the pre-event-sourcing era. When endless-event rebuild-db replays events into a clean projection DB, those legacy tasks never get inserted, so subsequent tasks with parent_id pointing at them fail FOREIGN KEY constraints (saw multiple warnings during E-787 Phase 5 manual sanity, e.g. 'insert task 910: constraint failed: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed (787)'). Fix options: (a) backfill synthetic task.created events for pre-E-808 tasks via a one-shot script (proper fix), (b) disable PRAGMA foreign_keys during projection (lossy — loses safety net), (c) skip insertion of tasks whose parent doesn't exist in projection (lossy). Discovered during E-787; out of scope of E-787 itself.

payload.title: Document ENDLESS_AUTO_MIGRATE env var in CLAUDE.md and dev guide
payload.description: E-787 added ENDLESS_AUTO_MIGRATE env var gate in src/endless/db.py. Default is '1' (auto-migrate, preserves prior behavior). Setting to '0' in shell defers migrations — useful for editable-install dev workflows where you don't want source edits to silently migrate the prod DB the moment you run an endless command. Tests force '1' via conftest. This needs to be documented somewhere developers will find it: CLAUDE.md, the dev guide at docs/guide-2026-04-15-using-endless-in-sessions.md, or a new docs/dev-environment.md. Could also add an explicit 'endless db migrate' command for explicit deployment-time migration. Surfaced during E-787 Phase 5.

payload.title: Design unified subprocess isolation primitive for tests
payload.description: pytest's isolated_env fixture uses monkeypatch.setattr to override Python module attributes — works for in-process tests, useless for tests that subprocess to Go binaries. E-787 hand-rolled a fix in conftest.py: monkeypatch.setenv('XDG_CONFIG_HOME', ...) plus PATH override so subprocess invocations see the isolated env. This pattern needs to be a first-class isolation primitive that ALL tests opt into — likely a fixture (or context manager) that sets up an isolated config dir and ensures both Python in-process AND subprocess-launched binaries see the same isolated paths. Surfaced as E-995 design input during E-787.

payload.title: Refactor test isolation cleanup to robust pattern
payload.description: E-787 Phase 5 manual sanity uses bash 'unset XDG_CONFIG_HOME' and 'rm -rf .test-isolated' for cleanup. Easy to forget the unset and leave the shell pointing at a deleted path, leading to confusing errors on the next endless command. Pytest fixtures have no such issue (monkeypatch auto-cleans), but binary-level/manual sanity flows are exposed. E-995 should consider: (a) trap-on-EXIT in a wrapper script, (b) a context-manager-style 'endless sandbox' command that auto-cleans, (c) per-session sandbox that lives until the session ends. Surfaced as E-995 design input during E-787.

payload.title: Add programmatic prod-leak detection for tests
payload.description: E-787 Phase 5 verified 'no prod leak' via stat -f mtime check on ~/.config/endless/endless.db before/after the test sequence. Brittle: prod can change for unrelated reasons (background tasks, other sessions) during a test window, producing false positives. Need stronger guarantee. Options: (a) wrap sandbox runs with bind-mount or read-only overlay on prod paths so any write to them errors immediately, (b) explicit assertion at sandbox boundaries that prod paths weren't touched, (c) stat inode + checksum (not just mtime) to distinguish 'modified' from 'replaced'. Surfaced as E-995 design input during E-787.

payload.title: Add migration spike against prod-schema copy to test workflow
payload.description: E-787 Phase 4 pytest tests catch 'does the new migration apply cleanly to a fresh schema'. E-787 Phase 5 catches 'does the new migration apply cleanly to a copy of prod schema'. These are different — fresh schemas don't carry the historical column ordering, dropped tables, or quirks accumulated over real prod migrations. E-995 should consider whether the standard test workflow includes a prod-copy migration spike (and a fixture that snapshots prod into the test dir) or relies on case-by-case manual setup. Without it, migrations can silently break against real prod schemas after passing all pytest tests. Surfaced as E-995 design input during E-787.

payload.title: Add status messages to 'session use' and 'session cd' so users see what happened
payload.description:
'esu' / 'endless session use' currently produces no visible feedback when activating a session — the user can't tell if anything happened. Same for 'escd' / 'endless session cd'. Silent success is wrong here: these commands are not run frequently enough to justify silence, and the user has nothing to confirm the effect.

Mike: 'Don't be noisy' is only right when (a) the expectation succeeded AND (b) the process runs so often that constant reminders would be annoying. session use / session cd hit (a) but not (b).

Design:
- Status messages go to stderr so they don't pollute stdout (which is eval'd by the shell wrapper).
- Format: '• Session <id> -> <path>' on success (concise; shows the cd target).
- When companion's worktree_path was set but the directory is missing (E-1038's silent fallback): emit an additional warning to stderr first, then the status. Format: '! worktree <path> no longer exists; falling back to cwd'. Still falls back silently in the no-worktree case (expected, common).
- Applies to both session_use_resolve and session_cd_resolve.

Verification:
- 'endless session use 247' prints the eval block on stdout AND a 'Session 247 -> ...' line on stderr.
- 'esu' / 'eval $(...)': the shell evaluates stdout (cd + export), the user sees the stderr line as confirmation.
- Companion worktree_path stale: stderr shows '! worktree ... no longer exists' before the status line.
- 'endless session cd 247' prints the path on stdout AND a status on stderr.
- Tests cover: success status, stale-worktree warning + status, no-worktree-set silent fallback (no warning).

Origin: E-1038 verification — Mike ran 'esu' in his shell and saw no visible effect. The cd-to-same-cwd happened silently. Filing the rule: when an expectation succeeded but produced no visible change, tell the user.

payload.title: Fix failing tests in test_outcome.py from E-787 landing
payload.description:
After commit c32f068 ('Add outcome field and task decline verb (E-787)') landed on main, 11 tests in tests/test_outcome.py fail. Spotted while running the full pytest suite during E-1047 verification.

Failing tests (sample):
- test_task_decline_writes_outcome
- test_task_confirm_with_outcome
- test_task_assume_with_outcome
- test_task_replace_with_status_declined_and_outcome
- test_task_update_outcome_standalone
- test_task_update_outcome_amends
- test_task_show_outcome_flag_renders_outcome
- test_task_show_declined_always_shows_outcome
- test_task_show_llm_includes_outcome
- test_task_show_json_includes_outcome
- test_rebuild_db_preserves_outcome

Many are 'click.exceptions' — looks like a CLI flag or command path that didn't land cleanly. Possibly Mike's parallel session has a fix in flight; this ticket is a marker so it doesn't get lost.

Verification: 'uv run pytest tests/test_outcome.py' passes.

Origin: noticed during E-1047 development. Not my work to fix (the parallel session's), but filing to ensure visibility.

payload.title: Shorten home directory to ~ in session status/warning messages
payload.description:
'esu' / 'endless session use' status messages on stderr include full paths, which are noisy when most of the path is the user's home directory:

  ! worktree /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1047 no longer exists; falling back to cwd
  • Session 247 → /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless

Replace the home-directory prefix with ~ for display:

  ! worktree ~/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1047 no longer exists; falling back to cwd
  • Session 247 → ~/Projects/endless

Scope: stderr-only (status + warning). The stdout 'cd <path>' line keeps the full path because shlex.quote turns ~ into a literal that the shell will not expand. Eval-safety wins over brevity for the consumed line.

Implementation: small helper _short_path(p) in session_cmd.py that swaps the home prefix; called from _emit_resolution_status only.

Verification:
- Status line uses ~/... when path is under home.
- Status line uses absolute path otherwise (e.g. /tmp/foo).
- 'cd' on stdout stays absolute (no ~ substitution).

Origin: Mike's note after E-1047 landed: 'replace home dir with ~ so output is not so noisy.'

payload.title: Add --target flag to 'session cd'; replace escd wrapper with esp
payload.description:
Mike's call: 'esp works, cd --target works, remove escd; more than one way to do things with no obvious benefit just leads to confusion. Legacy is not a concern.'

Add --target option to 'endless session cd' with choices:
  - auto (default; current behavior — worktree if exists, else cwd)
  - worktree (companion's worktree_path; error if not set or missing)
  - project (project root from DB via session.project_id)
  - cwd (companion's cwd field as-is)

Shell snippet from 'endless shell-init' becomes:

  esu()  { eval "$(endless session use "$@")"; }                       # activate
  esp()  { cd "$(endless session cd --target project "$@")"; }         # project root

Removing 'escd' entirely. Anyone wanting cd-only-to-worktree can run
'endless session cd --target worktree' directly; not common enough to
warrant a wrapper.

Verification:
- 'endless session cd --target worktree' on a session with no worktree -> stderr error, exit 1.
- 'endless session cd --target worktree' on a stale worktree -> stderr error, exit 1.
- 'endless session cd --target project' returns the project root (via DB lookup of session.project_id -> projects.path).
- 'endless session cd --target cwd' returns the cwd field as-is, no fall-back.
- 'endless session cd' (no --target) unchanged from today.
- 'esp' wrapper cds to project root.
- 'esu' unchanged.
- 'escd' no longer present in 'endless shell-init' output.

Origin: E-1049 conversation; Mike preferred a single primitive with --target over multiple verbs.

payload.title: Add focus handoff verb to spawn a new session from a child focus
payload.description: Implements the deferred Phase 1 verb for E-1031. Closes a focus as 'handoff', creates a new session bound to a fresh worktree (when E-968 W3 worktree machinery is ready, or via manual git worktree before that), copies the focus's Description into the new session's primary focus file, sets handoff_to_session_id/handoff_to_focus_id on the source and spawned_from_focus_id on the new primary. Reopen path already exists for adding info forgotten during handoff.

payload.title: Decide that .endless/focus/ prose files are user-curated, not auto-committed
payload.description: Decision (E-1031): .endless/focus/EF-NNN.md files mirror .endless/plans/<task-id>.md semantics — user/AI-curated content that ships with focus lifecycle commits, not auto-recorded history. They are NOT in E-987's auto-commit list. Same reasoning as plan files: edits are intentional curated prose, evolving as snapshots get appended; auto-committing would create commit-log noise without adding value. Implementation: see follow-up task to add .endless/focus/ to E-987's explicit-exclude documentation.

payload.title: Define focus_cmd.py + query_bridge.py as the established pilot for the E-894 Go-query refactor pattern
payload.description: Decision (E-1031): focus_cmd.py is the first Python CLI module to comply with E-894's 'Python has zero SQLite knowledge' direction. Pattern: writes go through event_bridge → endless-event emit → Go executor; reads go through query_bridge → endless-event get-* / focus-* JSON subcommands. internal/events/query.go holds the read-side helpers. Future Python CLI work should follow this shape; future task_cmd.py / session_cmd.py / suggestions_cmd.py migrations under E-894 should mirror it.

payload.title: Decide that focus prose files use E-1004 worktree-aware path resolution
payload.description: Decision (E-1031): per-focus prose files (.endless/focus/EF-NNN.md) follow the same worktree-aware resolution as task plan files (E-1004 fix). Implemented as focus_cmd._focus_file_root(): if cwd is inside a git worktree of a registered project, return the worktree's checkout root; otherwise the registered project path. Files land in the worktree's branch when running from one (so they ride along with that branch's commits). Distinct from focus_cmd._project_root_for_cwd() which is naive (returns main) and used for session-companion lookup, since companion files always live in main's .endless/sessions/ regardless of which worktree the session process is in.

payload.title: Add .endless/focus/ to E-987's explicit-exclude (NOT auto-commit) documentation
payload.description: Implements decision E-1052. Edit .endless/plans/E-987.md: add a row to the 'Explicitly NOT in the auto-commit list' table for '.endless/focus/<focus-id>.md' with rationale 'Per-focus prose files; user/AI-curated content shipping with focus lifecycle commits.' One-line documentation update; no code change to E-987's auto-commit machinery (which already excludes anything not in the auto-commit list).

payload.title: Auto-commit .endless/config.json with endless historical files
payload.description:
Decision (Mike, 2026-05-01).

**Rule**: '.endless/config.json' joins '.endless/events/*.jsonl' and '.endless/plans/snapshots/*' in E-987's auto-commit list. Land treats it as endless-managed and auto-commits any pending changes as part of the land flow.

**Why**: 'endless phrase add' is called frequently by AI sessions (and by Mike adding new title verbs as he hits friction). Each call dirties main's '.endless/config.json'. Without auto-commit, every land would refuse on dirty main, defeating the purpose.

**Original E-987 design** explicitly excluded config.json on the rationale 'user edits matter'. That rationale undervalued how often config.json is touched by automation. Revising.

**Trade-offs accepted**:
- Manual user edits to config.json (e.g. changing project description) get bundled with the next auto-commit if not separately committed first. Rare scenario; recoverable by making manual edits in a worktree if intentional attribution matters.
- Whole-file reserialization means JSON writer formatting changes look sweeping in diffs. Mitigated by stable deterministic JSON output (already the case in src/endless/matchers.py).

**Implementation**: covered by separate task to update E-987's plan and the eventual implementation in 'endless worktree land'.

payload.title: Add .endless/config.json to E-987 auto-commit list per E-1056
payload.description:
Implements decision E-1056: '.endless/config.json' joins the auto-commit list.

Edit needed in .endless/plans/E-987.md:
- Auto-commit list table: add a row for '.endless/config.json' with note 'AI-driven phrase add and similar mutations write here frequently per E-1056'
- 'Explicitly NOT in the auto-commit list' table: remove the '.endless/config.json' row
- Configurability section can stay as-is

When E-987's actual implementation lands ('endless worktree land' verb), the auto_commit_paths default in code/config should include '.endless/config.json'.

Verification: after both this plan update and E-987's implementation land, running 'endless phrase add verb foo' followed by 'endless worktree land <task-id>' from a clean main should auto-commit the config.json change as part of the land flow without refusing on 'main has uncommitted user changes'.

payload.title: Description field is a brief blurb, not analysis or plan content
payload.description: Policy: the description column on tasks and decisions holds a 2-3 sentence pitch that gives a reader a quick understanding of the item. Hard limits: length <= 1024, no embedded newlines. Long-form context (analysis, design, verification, decision rationale, plans) belongs in tasks.analysis or an attached plan file (.endless/plans/E-NNN.md), never in description. Mirrors E-962's pattern of enforcing content rules at the CLI write boundary rather than via schema. Drives a validation task (reject violating writes) and a backfill task (rewrite the 41 descriptions where the prior practice dumped analysis-style prose).

payload.title: Validate description on write: cap at 1024 chars and reject embedded newlines
payload.description: Add CLI-side validation for the description field on endless task add, endless task update, and endless decision add. Reject inputs longer than 1024 characters or containing any embedded newline (\n or \r). Mirrors E-962's existing pattern of enforcing content rules at the CLI write boundary rather than via schema migration. Implements the policy from E-1058.

payload.title: Rewrite oversized descriptions on tasks where analysis field carried the load
payload.description: 41 tasks have non-null analysis fields and descriptions that violate the new policy (length > 1024 or embedded newlines), reflecting the prior practice of using description as a long-form analysis dump. Rewrite each description as a concise blurb (<=1024, single line) and decide per-task whether the analysis field also needs adjustment. Backfill task; complements E-1059 (validation) and follows the policy in E-1058.

payload.title: Add post-edit commit prompt for CLI commands that mutate auto-committed files
payload.description:
Per discussion (Mike, 2026-05-01) on E-1056: when a user manually edits .endless/config.json AND an AI session runs 'endless phrase add', both changes get bundled in the next 'endless worktree land' auto-commit, losing the user's manual-edit attribution.

Mitigation: after CLI commands that mutate auto-committed files (e.g. 'endless phrase add', 'endless task add' if it touches config.json), prompt: 'Commit this change now as a separate commit? [y/N]'. Default no. If yes, runs the equivalent of 'git add <file> && git commit -m "<descriptive message>"' in the current worktree, attributing the change to the calling session.

Decide later: which commands trigger the prompt, default behavior, suppression flag (--no-prompt), and whether to auto-commit silently for headless/scripted use.

Revisit when: the manual-edit bundling has actually surfaced as a real complaint.

payload.title: Add periodic monitor that detects manual auto-tracked file changes and offers to commit
payload.description:
Companion to E-1061. Catches the case where a user edits .endless/config.json (or another auto-committed file) outside an endless CLI command and would otherwise have those edits bundled into the next auto-commit by 'endless worktree land'.

Approach options to decide later:
- File-watching daemon (fsnotify on .endless/config.json, plans/snapshots/, etc.); reports detected changes in 'endless session show' or surfaces via the channel
- Periodic check on hook events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit) compares mtimes against last-seen; if changed, surface to Claude as context
- 'endless status' command that shows pending auto-commits (events log, config edits, snapshot count) and offers 'endless commit-pending'

When to revisit: if accidental bundling becomes a real complaint, or if users start losing manual-edit attribution often enough to notice.

fields.description: Implement Vigil-style shadow git commits as Endless's own version history of project files: auto-commit changes with debouncing to give Endless change detection and staleness tracking without depending on the project's own git state. Subsumes the dropped file_changes table and the documents-table hash tracking, and absorbs the Vigil concept (debounced file-watching + auto-commit) into Endless rather than building it as a separate tool. Open question on shadow location (~/.config/endless/repos/<project>/ vs .git/info/endless/) and full background are in the analysis field.

Sibling to E-852 (which shipped narrow blocks/blocked-by support). Broader scope: make all task_deps relations first-class via at-creation flags on task add and decision add, a general task relate/unrelate primitive, full-relations display in task show, and reverse-lookup filters on task list and decision list. Schema accepts any dep_type so no migration is needed; vocabulary lock is in E-958 and blocks implementation. Punch list and ordering rationale are in the analysis field.

Pending decision (placeholder until resolved): lock the canonical, frozen set of task_deps.dep_type values and their inverses. In use today: blocks, needs, replaces. Proposed additions: informs/informed_by, decides/decided_by, relates_to (symmetric), documents/documented_by. Pure UX/docs decision (schema is type-permissive), but blocks the sibling CLI implementation because flags hard-code these strings. Specific calls and rationale are in the analysis field.

Add 'endless session summary [<session-id>]' that lists tasks touched in a session, grouped by parent task ID. Defaults to the current session if no ID is given. v1 grouping is parent_id only; AI-based topical clustering is a future follow-up if parent grouping proves too sparse. Decision (2026-04-28) deferred implementation until E-799's event-sourced architecture lands so attribution can query the event log by session_id rather than time-window or a join table.

Add a new task relationship type 'implements' (with inverse 'implemented-by') so an implementation task can link back to the decision it acts on. Mirrors the existing replaces/replaced-by pattern from E-890 (commit 68b7986). Use case that motivated this: link E-976 (gitignore worktree artifacts) to its decision E-975 so future readers can navigate either direction. Acceptance criteria and the test wire-up are in the analysis field.

E-972 fixed event_bridge.emit_event so events now correctly land in main's .endless/events/. But before the fix, events written from inside worktrees went to <worktree>/.endless/events/events-*.jsonl. Those stray events are not yet reflected in main's authoritative event log. Scope: identify worktrees with stray event files, determine duplicates vs unique writes, and either replay them into main's log or document them as accepted loss. The e-967 worktree-specific evidence is in the analysis field.

Decision (2026-04-28): replace the 3-step 'make change, create decision task, link them' ritual with a single --decision <text> flag on 'endless task add' and 'endless task update' that performs all three steps atomically. Friction in capturing rationale at the moment of decision was causing it to leak into commit messages or be lost entirely. Implements via E-980; depends on the dep_type vocabulary settled in E-958. Alternatives considered are in the analysis field.

Once E-969 ships eager plan snapshots to .endless/plans/snapshots/, this task adds the prune mechanism: 'endless plan snapshots prune [--days N] [--max-size MB] [--dry-run]', triggered opportunistically rather than via daemon. Defaults: 30-day retention for unattached snapshots, 100MB hard cap with oldest-first eviction. Snapshots whose content was attached to a task are redundant after attach (provenance lives at the task's plan file). Idempotent; surfaces count and bytes freed.

go.mod has 'replace github.com/mikeschinkel/go-cfgstore => ../go-pkgs/go-cfgstore' (and similar for go-doterr, go-dt, go-cliutil, go-logutil). The relative path resolves correctly from main but not from inside a worktree (~/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/<id> resolves to .endless/worktrees/go-pkgs/, which doesn't exist). Reproducer: cd into a worktree and run go build. Likely fix: a worktree-bootstrap symlink baked into E-971's worktree-creation hook. Options analysis is in the analysis field.

E-985 surfaced a Go-specific worktree friction (go.mod replace paths). Generalize: every language/project will have its own worktree-bootstrap concerns (npm install, venv recreation, Rust target/ cleanup, etc.) that Endless cannot bake in. Make worktree-bootstrap pluggable via a project-owned hook at <project>/.endless/hooks/post-worktree-create.sh, run inside the freshly-created worktree with worktree path as argument. Endless ships no default; each project writes its own. Acceptance criteria in the analysis field.

Architectural conflict: E-808's event log files are committed and authoritative (every CLI mutation appends), while E-971's 'worktree land' refuses dirty main. Together they make main continuously dirty and unlandable. Surfaced during E-969 work; we already had to commit a stray events JSONL just to keep main landable. Options range from auto-commit on every append, to gitignoring the log, to a narrower 'landable cleanliness' definition, to moving the log out of the working tree, to batched commits via a daemon. Full options analysis is in the analysis field.

From a tmux pane that is NOT the Claude pane, get into the cwd / worktree of a sibling Claude session so you can run tests or inspect state without polluting Claude's working directory. Resolution rules: explicit endless-id, single tmux sibling auto-resolve, or error with companion-file list. A child process cannot change its parent's cwd, so the command prints the target path and the user wraps it in a shell function (escd). Reads .endless/sessions/claude-*.json (E-989), no DB hot path. Verification matrix is in the analysis field.

Today there is no concise way to see what a session is, what task it's bound to, where its worktree lives, when it started, and how many messages it has. session list is global and noisy; session history dumps messages. Add a focused per-session detail command that defaults to the current session via the companion file (E-989) and accepts an explicit endless id or Claude UUID prefix. Output: id, project, state, started_at, last_activity, message count, bound task, worktree path/branch/cleanliness, last summary. --json for scripting.

Currently 'endless session history' requires an explicit session id or Claude UUID prefix, both of which are cryptic and forgettable. Once E-989 lands, current session can be resolved deterministically from the companion file, so the default should be to show history for the current session. Resolution mirrors session show (E-991): companion-file lookup with disambiguation in tmux. Out of scope: redesigning the history output itself; this task is purely about default resolution. Verification matrix in the analysis field.

UNRESOLVED: needs explicit decision before implementation. During E-969 verification I cleared a task's text field via raw SQL because the CLI provides no path to clear it (only --text <file>; no clear flag); the bypass violated the events-authoritative architecture (E-808) and produced a DB-vs-log desync that 'rebuild-db' would silently reverse. Options A-E (synthetic clear event, add 'task clear text' verb, restore via API, combine, accept desync) and Claude's recommendation are in the analysis field.

While building E-989 we articulated the criteria for non-fatal-on-error: opportunistic by design, self-healing on a later event, or high expected error rate. Audit snapshotPlanFile (cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:538), which currently swallows errors with log.Printf and returns nil, against those criteria. If it qualifies, document the rationale in a comment; otherwise convert to fatal, matching InitSession / SetProcess / WriteCompanion. Out of scope: a sweep of every other log.Printf-on-err site in the hook.

Worktrees under .endless/worktrees/<id>/ break Go builds because go.mod uses 'replace github.com/mikeschinkel/X => ../go-pkgs/X', which resolves from the worktree's location rather than main. Workaround in use today is a one-shot symlink at .endless/worktrees/go-pkgs -> ~/Projects/go-pkgs. Decide a durable answer: keep the symlink, switch to go.work, vendor go-pkgs, or publish module versions. Recommendation depends on whether go-pkgs stays co-developed or stabilizes as published deps. Options analysis is in the analysis field.

Today, exercising new hook code in a real Claude session requires temporarily repointing the global ~/.local/bin/endless-hook symlink at the worktree's binary, which puts every other live Claude session on the machine on the unverified build for the duration of the swap. Claude Code's project-level .claude/settings.json takes precedence, so generate one under each worktree that overrides the hook command path to the worktree's bin/endless-hook. Sessions outside the worktree are unaffected. Open design items and verification matrix are in the analysis field.

The tasks.text column is conventionally treated as 'the plan' (planned plan-bearing detector keys off its presence), but there is no field for problem analysis, design briefs, research notes, decision rationale, or postmortems — content that is long-form but not a plan. Today the hook does not read text for behavioral decisions, but the moment plan-bearing detection lands, anything stored in text gets interpreted as 'planned'. Options: rename text->plan and add a separate analysis field, overload description, or convention-only. Recommendation: rename + add field. Full analysis in the analysis field.

Implement the rename decision from the field-separation design task: tasks.text -> tasks.plan throughout the schema, ORM, CLI, hook references, and templates. Surface area: schema migration, CLI flags on task add/update/show (--text -> --plan), the '## Text' heading, hook comments, and web templates. Decide alias-vs-hard-break in the parent design task. Verification: task show still renders the plan section, web task-detail renders, and existing scripts either continue via alias or fail loudly. Out-of-scope items in the analysis field.

Today some flags carry inline values (--description <text>) and others carry file paths despite the flag name implying inline content (--text <file>, --prompt <file>). Standardize: any flag whose value is a file path uses --xxx-file; the bare --xxx flag carries inline content. Apply uniformly across all endless commands. Endless is pre-1.0 and 'move fast' applies; recommend hard cutover in one shot rather than a migration window. Verification, surface area, and decisions on auto-detect are in the analysis field.

Decision (2026-04-29): the 'informs' dep_type added in E-958's locked vocabulary is being removed because the verb is too non-specific. Mike: 'I cannot imagine myself ever using the task link command and saying that one informs the other.' Per the agent-reach principle, vocabulary that goes unused becomes noise. Remaining canonical dep_types: blocks/blocked_by, implements/implemented_by, replaces/replaced_by, relates_to. The soft decision-task case folds into relates_to. Cascading changes are tracked under E-1002.

Bug surfaced during E-957 implementation. When 'endless task update --text' runs from inside a git worktree of the project, the per-task plan-file mirror lands in main's checkout, not the worktree's. Today _project_root_for_task() reads the project's path from the projects table, which is fixed at registration time and points at main. Resolution rule: detect cwd-in-worktree via 'git rev-parse --git-common-dir', return the worktree's root in that case. Critical file: src/endless/task_cmd.py. Verification matrix in the analysis field.

Observed during E-970 implementation. With multiple Claude Code sessions running endless work concurrently, the editable uv tool install at ~/.local/share/uv/tools/endless/ keeps reverting to a snapshot install (marker: missing _endless.pth). Symptom: 'endless phrase add foo' succeeds, then 'endless task add' immediately fails because validate_title runs against a stale snapshot. Workaround: 'uv tool install -e . --force'. Suspected cause: another session running 'uv tool install' without -e. Investigation steps and resolution options are in the analysis field.

Decision (2026-04-29): the documents/documented_by dep_type was deferred in E-958 for lack of concrete use case. With E-980 (--decision flag) and E-957's 'decision add --about' both shipping, both wire decision-task pairs via relates_to — which is wrong (the relation is directional 'decision documents task' and more specific than the symmetric catch-all). Active-voice storage: 'documents' (source=decision, target=task). Display order between replaces and relates_to. Cascading changes tracked in E-1006.

The Python endless CLI is installed via 'uv tool install' but not in editable mode — it copies src/endless/ into the uv tool's site-packages, so after every merge to main the installed copy is stale until 'uv tool install --reinstall .' runs. We hit this immediately after landing E-990: 'endless session cd' was missing on PATH despite being merged. Two routes: (1) editable install via 'uv tool install --editable .' or 'uv pip install -e .' — cleanest if uv supports it, (2) add --reinstall to 'just install'. Recommend #1; fall back to #2.

E-989's companion file is written only at SessionStart. Sessions that were already running when E-989 landed never got one, so 'endless session cd' (E-990) cannot see them until restart. We hit this immediately after landing E-990. Same pattern as the existing BackfillProcess: in the UserPromptSubmit hook handler, after BackfillProcess, stat the companion file; if missing, build the same record writeClaudeCompanion would build at SessionStart. Idempotent (only write when missing). Verification: delete a live session's companion and see it reappear on next prompt.

Mechanism enforcement for the per-session-worktree discipline: standalone follow-up to E-971, smaller scope, immediately shippable. Claude keeps making 'tidy-up' commits directly on main instead of in a worktree; stated discipline doesn't hold. Layer: extend the existing Claude Code hook (cmd/endless-hook/claude.go) with a PreToolUse Bash rule that denies 'git commit' when cwd is main's working tree (allow worktrees and active merges). Returns deny via Claude Code hook response so the rejection appears in Claude's context. Acceptance criteria in the analysis field.

Companion to E-1012 (Claude Code hook for blocking commits-on-main). E-1012 catches Claude's bash 'git commit' calls; this task captures the deferred broader-scope option of git hooks for catching the user's direct git commit on main, IDE git integrations, scripts, and sibling AI agents not using Claude Code. Deferred because none of those are current pain points. Challenges to design through: distribution, composability with existing hooks, cross-platform Windows hostility, --no-verify bypass, worktree handling. Likely-approach sketch and revisit triggers in the analysis field.

Open question, not a committed deprecation. Once session use (E-1014) and shell-init (E-1015) have been in real use long enough for confidence, decide whether session cd remains as a thin lookup verb (just print the path, no env-vars) or gets retired in favor of eval-style activation via session use. Mike pushed back on 'will deprecate' framing — keep cd until evidence shows it's friction, not before. Arguments for and against keeping both are in the analysis field.

Decision (Mike, 2026-04-30 during E-987 design discussion). Rule: files endless writes as historical records are write-once-by-endless; hand-editing is forbidden. Members: '.endless/events/*.jsonl' (append-only event log per E-808) and '.endless/plans/snapshots/*.{md,json}' (write-once-per-file per E-969). Why: hand-edits break invariants the projection engine (E-807 rebuild-db) depends on, producing silent corruption. Implications for 'worktree land', the Claude Code hook layer, and CLAUDE.md docs are in the analysis field.

Surfaced during E-987 design discussion. Tomorrow's problem; not blocking E-987 or E-971. Two patterns for the projection engine when the events log grows: (A) always rebuild from full event log, scaling linearly — fine for solo dev for years; (B) periodic data.sql checkpoint plus event-log truncation, bounded but more complex. Current state: data.sql exists but is a stale manual dump, so we're loosely in B without truncation. Decisions to make and revisit triggers are in the analysis field.

Companion to E-987 (auto-commit endless-managed files during land). E-987's land flow rebases the worktree onto main after auto-committing endless-managed files; in normal operation rebase is conflict-free, but corner cases (pre-E-972 worktrees with stale auto-file commits, hand-edits, future code paths writing auto-files from a worktree) will hit eventually. v1: document the recovery procedure (rebase --abort, checkout main -- <files>, retry land) and surface the hint in 'worktree land's rebase-failure error message. v2: 'land --resolve-auto' flag, gated on real recurrence.

Currently src/endless/config.py:6 hardcodes Path.home() / '.config' / 'endless'. The Go side (internal/monitor/db.go:21-28) honors XDG_CONFIG_HOME via os.Getenv; Python doesn't. This parity gap blocks isolated binary-level testing of Python CLI commands — the pytest isolated_env fixture works in-process via monkeypatch but cannot isolate subprocess-launched binaries. Read XDG_CONFIG_HOME first, fall back to Path.home() / '.config'. Audit other Path.home() references. Estimated size: ~30 lines. Blocks E-787 Phase 5; serves as input to E-995's design.

The companion file (E-989) has a worktree_path field that is currently never set — writeClaudeCompanion always leaves it empty. This makes session use (E-1014) and session cd (E-990) fall back to cwd, which for sessions started outside a worktree (like the bootstrap session running in main) does not point at the worktree the user actually wants to test. Recommendation: writer-side hook update on task-start (option A) — single source of truth, all readers benefit, small hook change. Worktree naming convention: '.endless/worktrees/e-<task_id>*'. Verification matrix in the analysis field.

The CLI's TaskIDType (src/endless/cli.py:10) accepts both 'E-1027' and '1027', but the hook's regex matchers in DEFAULT_MATCHERS (src/endless/matchers.py:75,79) only match bare digits. So 'endless task start E-1027' typed in a Bash tool is silently a no-op for the session-state side effect: StartWorkSession never fires, sessions.active_task_id stays NULL. Fix: relax both regexes to accept an optional [Ee]- prefix; capture group still extracts only digits. Two places: DEFAULT_MATCHERS and existing machine configs (decide migration vs manual edit).

Mike (2026-04-30, during E-971 foundation work) directed extracting the gitutils package from gomion into a standalone go-pkgs module, adding worktree CRUD (Add/Remove/List --porcelain), and using it from endless via the existing 'replace' pattern. Source: ~/Projects/gomion/gommod/gitutils/. External deps already at ~/Projects/go-pkgs/. After extraction, add the replace directive to endless's go.mod. Mike will later update gomion to import from go-pkgs/go-gitutil; until then two copies coexist. Existing functionality and new APIs are in the analysis field.

Today the UserPromptSubmit handler in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go calls writeClaudeCompanion only when CompanionExists returns false (the E-1011 backfill semantic). That leaves the companion file vulnerable to drift any time active_task_id changes through a path that doesn't trigger writeClaudeCompanion. Fix: drop the CompanionExists guard and always write. Cost: one atomic JSON write per prompt, sub-millisecond. Benefit: companion at most one prompt out of date with the DB. Drift scenarios and verification matrix are in the analysis field.

Mike (2026-04-30) confirmed 'sidecar' is obsoleted in favor of 'companion file' (per the existing internal/monitor/companion.go convention). The E-971 foundation commit (e291de0) was authored with the right terminology, but several pre-existing references remain in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go and in plan files (.endless/plans/E-968.md, E-971.md, E-987.md, E-969.md). Mechanical replacement: 'sidecar' to 'companion file' or 'companion' depending on context. Verification: 'grep -rn sidecar' returns only legitimate dictionary usage. File list and naming details in the analysis field.

The companion file's worktree_path can go stale between hook events: written from active_task_id at hook time, but the underlying worktree directory can be deleted ('git worktree remove') with no hook signal. Readers (session use, session cd) then emit 'cd <gone>' that fails. Hit during E-1015 verification. E-1033 (refresh-every-UserPromptSubmit) doesn't cover side panes that read between Claude's turns. Fix on the reader side: validate worktree_path with os.path.isdir() in _target_path; fall back to cwd silently if missing. One stat per read; companion stays as 'intent', reader treats it as a hint.

Today 'endless session use' emits 5 env vars (ENDLESS_SESSION_ID, ENDLESS_HARNESS_SESSION_ID, ENDLESS_HARNESS, ENDLESS_PROJECT_ROOT, ENDLESS_WORKTREE_PATH) plus a cd. Only the integer id is canonical; the rest are derivable and snapshotting them invites the staleness E-1015 verification surfaced. Mike's call: minimal is right. Activation reduces to 'cd <target>' + 'export ENDLESS_SESSION_ID=<id>'. Other consumers call 'endless session show <id> --json' for fresh data. Supersedes E-1037. Extension contract change: extensions parse JSON instead of reading env vars.

payload.title: Migrate Python CLI surface entirely to Go (umbrella)
payload.description:
Long-horizon goal: endless's CLI surface (currently Python via Click) ports entirely to Go. The Go binaries (endless-hook, endless-event, endless-channel, endless-serve) already handle hot-path operations; this task is the umbrella for moving the human-facing Python CLI (task, worktree, phrase, etc.) too.

Existing partial-migration tasks:
- E-799 (parent): Migrate to event-sourced architecture
- E-893: Consolidate Go binaries into unified endless-go CLI
- E-894: Move task display reads from Python to Go
- E-954: Migrate Python config readers to honor Go config merging

This task captures cross-cutting constraints that apply to ALL Python-to-Go ports, not just one binary or feature.

## Critical constraint: ordered maps for JSON serialization

Go's map iteration order is randomized by design (intentional anti-feature to discourage relying on iteration order). This breaks deterministic JSON output: re-serializing a map produces keys in a different order each time, generating spurious diffs in tracked files like .endless/config.json.

Required: use ordered map types (insertion-order-preserving) for any JSON we serialize to disk. Mike has ordered-map code in one of his go-pkgs that should be used.

Failure mode if ignored: every 'endless phrase add' from Go would scramble the matchers list order in config.json, producing a sweeping diff for what semantically should be a single-line append. Auto-commit of config.json (E-1056) would then be unusable.
