Find Mike's ordered-map package: probably ~/Projects/go-pkgs/go-dt/dtx (OrderedMap was visible in gogit's repo.go: dtx.NewOrderedMap[K, V]).

## Other constraints to gather

(To populate as the migration proceeds and edge cases surface.)

## When to start

Not now. Python CLI is mature, stable, and active sessions are productive. Move only when there's a concrete forcing function (perf, dependency reduction, distribution simplification, etc.).

payload.title: Audit analysis fields on tasks for accuracy and staleness
payload.description: Pass over tasks where the analysis field is populated and assess each: is the analysis still accurate given current code/decisions, or has the work moved on? Flag stale items (e.g. analysis references options that are now decided, or open questions that have since been resolved). Out of scope: rewriting analysis content. This is a survey pass that produces follow-up tasks for any items that need refresh, deletion, or promotion. Complements E-1058 (description policy) by checking the destination field's hygiene.

payload.title: Rewrite oversized or multi-line descriptions on tasks without populated analysis
payload.description: Eight tasks violate the new description policy (length > 1024 or embedded newlines) but were outside E-1060's scope because their analysis field is empty. E-924 and E-963 exceed 1024 chars; E-1047, E-1048, E-1049, E-1050, E-1056, E-1057 contain embedded newlines. Rewrite each description as a concise blurb (<=1024, single line). For each, decide whether the trimmed long-form content should be moved into the analysis field or simply dropped, since there is no existing analysis to merge with. Companion to E-1060 and follows the policy in E-1058.

fields.description: Eleven tasks violate the new description policy (length > 1024 or embedded newlines) but were outside E-1060's scope because their analysis field is empty. E-924 and E-963 exceed 1024 chars; E-1047, E-1048, E-1049, E-1050, E-1056, E-1057, E-1061, E-1062, E-1063 contain embedded newlines (the last three were added after the original count). Rewrite each description as a concise blurb (<=1024, single line). For each, decide whether the trimmed long-form content should move into the analysis field or be dropped. Companion to E-1060; follows the policy in E-1058.

AI-agent feedback Claude has accumulated about working with Endless (record all actions as tasks, STOP on CLI errors, self-explanatory titles, decision-vs-task distinction, no legacy without asking, etc.) currently lives only in per-user ~/.claude/projects/<proj>/memory/. New developers adopting Endless start without these and repeat the same failure modes. Need a delivery mechanism that travels with the project. Triage: separate Endless-universal rules (should ship) from Mike-personal preferences (should NOT ship). Candidate mechanisms: docs file in repo, SessionStart hook injection, 'endless rules' CLI command, prompt file bundled with the project, or a combination. Probably docs+hook at minimum. Discuss before implementing.

Enforce the description-required policy at CLI write time. (1) endless task add: reject when neither --description nor --text is provided; if --text is given and --description is not, auto-derive description from the first non-blank, non-heading line of the text file. (2) endless decision add: same rule, but decisions usually have no --text, so practically --description becomes required. (3) endless task update: do not touch description when updating other fields; require explicit confirmation when clearing. (4) Display fallback in task show / decision show: when description is NULL, render an excerpt of text or '(no description, see text)' rather than blank. No schema change. Pairs with the backfill task; ship together so the CLI rule does not start failing against not-yet-backfilled rows.

'esu' / 'endless session use' currently produces no visible feedback when activating a session — the user can't tell if anything happened. Same for 'escd' / 'endless session cd'. Silent success is wrong here: these commands aren't run frequently enough to justify silence. Design: status line to stderr (so it doesn't pollute stdout-eval'd output) — '• Session <id> -> <path>' on success; when companion's worktree_path is set but the directory is missing (E-1038's silent fallback), prepend '! worktree <path> no longer exists; falling back to cwd' first. Applies to both session_use_resolve and session_cd_resolve. Origin: E-1038 verification surfaced silent activation as confusing.

After commit c32f068 ('Add outcome field and task decline verb (E-787)') landed on main, 11 tests in tests/test_outcome.py fail (sample: test_task_decline_writes_outcome, test_task_confirm_with_outcome, test_task_show_outcome_flag_renders_outcome, test_rebuild_db_preserves_outcome). Many are 'click.exceptions' — looks like a CLI flag or command path that didn't land cleanly, possibly mid-flight in a parallel session. Filing as a marker so it doesn't get lost. Verification: 'uv run pytest tests/test_outcome.py' passes. Origin: noticed during E-1047 development.

'esu' / 'endless session use' status messages on stderr include full paths, which are noisy when most of the path is the user's home directory (e.g. '! worktree /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1047 no longer exists'). Replace the home prefix with ~ for display. Scope: stderr-only (status + warning lines). The stdout 'cd <path>' line keeps the full path because shlex.quote turns ~ into a literal that the shell will not expand — eval-safety wins over brevity for the consumed line. Implementation: small _short_path(p) helper in session_cmd.py, called from _emit_resolution_status only. Origin: Mike's note after E-1047 landed.

Mike's call: 'esp works, cd --target works, remove escd; more than one way to do things with no obvious benefit just leads to confusion. Legacy is not a concern.' Add --target option to 'endless session cd' with choices: auto (default; current behavior), worktree (companion's worktree_path; error if not set or missing), project (project root from DB via session.project_id), cwd (companion's cwd field as-is). Shell snippet from 'endless shell-init' becomes esu (eval session use) and esp (cd to project root); 'escd' is removed. Anyone wanting cd-only-to-worktree runs 'endless session cd --target worktree' directly. Origin: E-1049 conversation; Mike preferred a single primitive with --target over multiple verbs.

Decision (Mike, 2026-05-01). Rule: '.endless/config.json' joins '.endless/events/*.jsonl' and '.endless/plans/snapshots/*' in E-987's auto-commit list. Land treats it as endless-managed and auto-commits any pending changes as part of the land flow. Why: 'endless phrase add' is called frequently by AI sessions (and by Mike adding new title verbs as he hits friction); each call dirties main's config.json, and without auto-commit every land would refuse on dirty main. The original E-987 design excluded config.json on the rationale 'user edits matter'; that undervalued how often it is touched by automation. Trade-offs: manual user edits get bundled with the next auto-commit unless committed first (rare; recoverable via worktree). Implementation tracked separately.

Implements decision E-1056: '.endless/config.json' joins the auto-commit list. Edit needed in .endless/plans/E-987.md — auto-commit list table gains a row for '.endless/config.json' with note 'AI-driven phrase add and similar mutations write here frequently per E-1056', and the 'Explicitly NOT in the auto-commit list' table loses its config.json row. When E-987's actual implementation lands, the auto_commit_paths default in code/config should include '.endless/config.json'. Verification: 'endless phrase add verb foo' followed by 'endless worktree land' from clean main auto-commits the config change as part of land.

Per discussion (Mike, 2026-05-01) on E-1056: when a user manually edits .endless/config.json AND an AI session runs 'endless phrase add', both changes get bundled in the next 'endless worktree land' auto-commit, losing the user's manual-edit attribution. Mitigation: after CLI commands that mutate auto-committed files, prompt 'Commit this change now as a separate commit? [y/N]' (default no); if yes, runs 'git add <file> && git commit -m "<message>"' in the current worktree. Decide later: which commands trigger the prompt, default behavior, suppression flag (--no-prompt), and headless/scripted behavior. Revisit when manual-edit bundling has actually surfaced as a real complaint.

Companion to E-1061. Catches the case where a user edits .endless/config.json (or another auto-committed file) outside an endless CLI command and would otherwise have those edits bundled into the next auto-commit by 'endless worktree land'. Approach options to decide later: file-watching daemon (fsnotify on .endless/config.json, plans/snapshots/, etc.) reporting via 'endless session show' or the channel; periodic check on hook events comparing mtimes; an 'endless status' command that surfaces pending auto-commits with an 'endless commit-pending' helper. Revisit when accidental bundling becomes a real complaint or attribution loss starts to bite.

Long-horizon goal: endless's CLI surface (currently Python via Click) ports entirely to Go. The Go binaries (endless-hook, endless-event, endless-channel, endless-serve) already handle hot-path operations; this task is the umbrella for moving the human-facing Python CLI (task, worktree, phrase, etc.) too. Existing partial-migration siblings: E-799 (event-sourced architecture parent), E-893 (consolidate Go binaries into unified endless-go CLI), E-894 (move task display reads from Python to Go), E-954 (migrate Python config readers to honor Go config merging). Critical cross-cutting constraint: use ordered map types (e.g. dtx.OrderedMap from go-pkgs/go-dt) for any JSON we serialize to disk — Go's randomized map iteration would otherwise scramble config.json key order on every write, defeating E-1056's auto-commit. Not now: Python CLI is mature, stable, and active sessions are productive. Move only on a concrete forcing function.

fields.text:
# E-1064: Audit analysis fields on tasks for accuracy and staleness

Survey of the 41 tasks where `tasks.analysis IS NOT NULL` after E-1060 finalised. Goal: flag entries whose analysis content has drifted from current code/decision state, and recommend refresh-or-leave-as-is per item. No analysis content rewritten in this pass.

## Method

1. Pulled `(id, status, analysis)` for all 41 records.
2. Cross-referenced status with git log (`--grep "E-NNN"`) and existing plan files in `.endless/plans/`.
3. Bucketed each into one of:
   - **A. Shipped — analysis is historical record.** Status verify/confirmed/assumed/declined; analysis describes options or punch lists that the implementation has resolved. Low urgency. Trim only if it becomes a navigation hazard.
   - **B. Shipped/locked — analysis still presents a live decision.** Active mismatch between analysis content and current state of the world. Worth refreshing before someone reads the analysis and acts on stale options.
   - **C. Time-frozen data.** Analysis cites byte counts, file paths, or counts that were a snapshot of one moment. Refresh before acting on the task.
   - **D. Currently relevant.** Status needs_plan or in_progress; analysis informs the upcoming work.
   - **E. Obsolete/declined.** Analysis is archival; no action.

## Findings

### B. Shipped/locked but analysis still presents a live decision (refresh recommended)

| ID | Status | Why analysis is stale | Recommendation |
|----|--------|----------------------|----------------|
| E-958 | confirmed | "Pending decision (placeholder until resolved)" with open vocabulary questions, but `informs` was dropped (E-1003) and `documents` promoted (E-1007), and the relations CLI shipped via commit `e372759`. The frozen vocabulary differs from what the analysis enumerates. | Replace analysis with a one-line "vocabulary frozen — see E-1003 / E-1007" pointer, OR delete analysis entirely (description already conveys what the task was). |
| E-987 | ready | Analysis lists Options A-E and tags the task `--type research`, but the design was locked to Option F (auto-commit endless-managed files independently) per commit `1825a35` and `.endless/plans/E-987.md`. Anyone reading analysis would think the decision is still open. | Refresh analysis to "design locked to Option F (see E-987 plan file)", OR drop analysis since the plan file is canonical. |
| E-993 | obsolete | Status flipped to obsolete; analysis still reads "STATUS: UNRESOLVED. Needs discussion and explicit decision before implementation." Direct contradiction. | Drop analysis or prepend "Resolved: marked obsolete YYYY-MM-DD". |
| E-1037 | obsolete | Analysis ends with "Supersedes E-1037" — but that's the wrong direction; E-1038's analysis says it supersedes E-1037, and the task itself is now obsolete. Reader sees "supersedes itself", which is nonsense as written. | Drop analysis or replace with one-line "obsoleted by E-1038". |

### C. Time-frozen data (refresh before acting)

| ID | Status | Stale data | Recommendation |
|----|--------|-----------|----------------|
| E-979 | needs_plan | Byte counts for `e-967` worktree's events file (150651 vs 153226) snapshotted 2026-04-29 or so. May have moved on. | Re-measure when the task is picked up; don't trust cited bytes. |
| E-1034 | needs_plan | Specific line numbers in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` (lines 690, 713, 726, 732, 740) and plan files. Other commits may have shifted those line numbers. | Re-grep `sidecar` before editing. |
| E-994 | needs_plan | Cites `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:538`. Drift likely after subsequent hook work (E-1011, E-1012, E-1027, E-1028, E-1033). | Re-locate `snapshotPlanFile` before editing. |

### A. Shipped — analysis is historical record (low urgency, leave unless aggregating)

These shipped or terminalised since the analysis was written. Their analysis still reads as "design exploration" or "punch list", which is now historical. No active misdirection — the status communicates done — but the field has stopped earning its keep. Consider trimming if/when the analysis field gains a CLI surface that displays it (today nothing reads it).

| ID | Status | Implemented by |
|----|--------|----------------|
| E-957 | assumed | `e372759` (general-purpose task link/unlink CLI) |
| E-977 | obsolete | folded into E-1006/E-1007 work |
| E-981 | confirmed | `acc7291` (--decision flag) |
| E-990 | verify | `d5f94f6` (session cd) |
| E-991 | verify | `0b0f1d0` (session show) |
| E-992 | verify | `0b0f1d0` (session history default) |
| E-1003 | confirmed | E-1002 cascading changes |
| E-1004 | verify | `a9ba29f` (cwd-aware project root) |
| E-1007 | confirmed | `3061a4d` (documents promoted) |
| E-1011 | verify | `5e12ac7` (companion backfill) |
| E-1012 | verify | `4f69a05` (block git commits) |
| E-1017 | confirmed | decision-record only; no implementation needed |
| E-1020 | confirmed | `e180710` (XDG support) |
| E-1027 | verify | `7219eb9` (companion worktree_path) |
| E-1028 | verify | `6e048ea` (hook regex E-prefix) |
| E-1033 | verify | `58ac6c8` (refresh on every UserPromptSubmit) |
| E-1038 | verify | `9ea22b3` (reduce session use activation) |

### D. Currently relevant (no change recommended)

Analysis informs ongoing or upcoming work. Leave as-is.

| ID | Status |
|----|--------|
| E-966 | needs_plan |
| E-984 | needs_plan |
| E-985 | needs_plan |
| E-986 | needs_plan |
| E-996 | needs_plan |
| E-998 | needs_plan |
| E-999 | needs_plan |
| E-1000 | needs_plan |
| E-1001 | needs_plan |
| E-1005 | needs_plan |
| E-1008 | needs_plan |
| E-1013 | needs_plan |
| E-1016 | needs_plan |
| E-1018 | needs_plan |
| E-1019 | needs_plan |
| E-1029 | in_progress |

### E. Obsolete/declined — archival, no action

| ID | Status |
|----|--------|
| E-767 | declined |

## Cross-cutting observation: analysis has no CLI surface

`tasks.analysis` was added to the schema and populated for these 41 records by some out-of-band mechanism (raw SQL or one-time migration; no `--analysis` flag exists on `endless task add` / `update`, and no Python or Go code in `src/` or `cmd/` reads it). Until the column is wired into the CLI, audit findings cannot be acted on through the normal event-sourced path without re-tripping the architectural concern raised in E-993 (raw-SQL writes bypass the event log).

Two directions worth a follow-up task:

1. **Add `--analysis` / `--analysis-file` flag** to `endless task add` and `endless task update`, with display in `endless task show`. Without this, the field is write-only-via-SQL and not actionable.
2. **Decide the field's role.** Is `analysis` long-form rationale (then it overlaps with `text`/plan files), or is it scratch space for design exploration before promotion to a plan file? E-999 is already the design task for "plan vs analysis content separation" and should absorb this question.

## Recommended follow-ups

- File one task to refresh-or-drop analysis on **E-958, E-987, E-993, E-1037** (bucket B). Concrete, contained, ~20 minutes of edits.
- File one task to wire `analysis` into the CLI (or fold this into E-999's design pass). Without it, the field cannot be acted on safely.
- Buckets A and C are not actionable on their own — flag them in the relevant tickets if/when those tickets are picked up.

No task additions made by this audit pass; the user requested a survey, not implementation.

# E-995: Sandbox/Fixture Pattern for Tests That Mutate State

## Context

Tests and verification work that mutate Endless state must run against an isolated sandbox, never against the live system. Today there is no first-class sandbox primitive. E-787 proved this matters: I bypassed event-sourcing with raw SQL during E-979 cleanup (declined as E-993), then hand-rolled a partial sandbox in `tests/conftest.py` (`XDG_CONFIG_HOME` env, `PATH` prepend, `_seed_project_at_cwd` helper). E-787's Phase 5 manual sanity used yet another bash-only `.test-isolated/` setup. None of these are reusable; each session reinvents.

This plan establishes a first-class `endless sandbox` mechanism with three layered guarantees: env-var isolation as the foundation (cheap, default), opt-in deep clone of live state (for migration / sanity work), and OS-level enforcement via Go 1.24's `os.Root` (paranoid mode, kernel-level write barrier on prod paths). A single Python `Sandbox` class wraps the same machinery as the CLI for use in pytest fixtures.

This plan is general-purpose — sandboxes isolate "whatever the current user's endless install is." It's not specialized for the endless-developing-endless workflow, even though that's the immediate driver.

## Decisions locked during planning conversation

These get filed as Endless decisions on plan exit (via `endless decision add --decides E-995 ...`):

1. **Layered isolation model.** Foundation (#1) is `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`-based env-var redirection — Go honors it natively, Python gained support in E-1020. Opt-in (#2) deep-clone copies the user's DB plus per-project state with paths rewritten. Opt-in (#3) OS-level guard uses Go 1.24's `os.Root` to make writes outside the sandbox dir fail at the syscall level. Each layer composes; none replaces another.

2. **`--clone` is deep-clone.** Copies the DB *and* per-project `.endless/events/`, `.endless/plans/`, etc., and rewrites `projects.path` rows so events written in the sandbox land in sandboxed locations rather than the real project dirs. Cost: ~200-500MB and ~500ms-2s typical; safety justifies it. `--clone` is the only provisioning flag — no `--clone-prod`, no other variants. Without `--clone`, sandbox starts empty.

3. **`endless sandbox` command suite.** `run`, `enter`, `list`, `prune`, `destroy`. No `create` — sandboxes are created implicitly by `run`/`enter`.

4. **Default = ephemeral.** No flags → sandbox lives only for the duration of `run`'s command. Auto-destroyed at exit (via `defer`/`trap`). `--keep` makes it persistent; `--name N` gives it a stable name. Ephemerals get random names internally.

5. **`enter` opens a subshell.** Not env-var modification of the current shell. Cleaner exit semantics; `exit` always works. Speed cost is negligible.

6. **Storage: `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/<name>/`** (XDG_CACHE_HOME-aware). Out of project dirs (sandbox is endless-config-scoped, not project-scoped).

7. **Crashed sandboxes survive for inspection.** Detected via PID liveness check on `creator_pid` in `.sandbox-meta.json`. `endless sandbox enter <name>` works on orphans for debugging.

8. **`prune` semantics.** Default: orphaned ephemerals only. `--older-than DURATION` filter, minimum 24h enforced (any value below errors with `"minimum --older-than is 24h"`). No `--all` flag — if you want everything gone, `rm -rf ~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/`. Outputs scope being addressed before acting (e.g., `"Pruning 3 orphaned ephemeral sandboxes older than 24h: ..."`).

9. **Always-on noisy startup warning.** When N>0 orphans exist, `endless` prints a one-line warning at startup with explicit disable instructions. Default ON. Disabled via `endless config set sandbox.startup_warning false`. Visibility > temporary annoyance — silent options are invisible options.

10. **pytest fixture API: object + methods.** `sb.run("task add ...")`, `sb.db` (sandboxed connection for assertions), `sb.env` (env vars dict for further subprocesses), `sb.path` (sandbox dir). Wrapped in a context manager; pytest fixture yields it.

11. **Binary-selection helper shared with E-1039.** `_resolve_endless_bin_dir()` (or equivalent) returns the right `bin/` for subprocess calls, detecting worktree-local builds and falling back to PATH. E-995 uses it to set PATH inside `sandbox run`. E-1039 later refactors `event_bridge.emit_event` and other call sites to use the same helper. Shared logic, two consumers — no conflict, clean delegation.

12. **Enforcement layering: `os.Root` + Python app-level guard.** Go binaries open everything via `os.OpenRoot(sandbox_dir)`; out-of-sandbox writes fail at the kernel. Python (which still opens SQLite directly) checks `ENDLESS_SANDBOX` env var and refuses to open paths outside. Once E-894 moves all SQL to Go, the Python guard becomes vestigial.

## Implementation

### Phase 1 — Go core: `endless-sandbox` binary

New binary `cmd/endless-sandbox/main.go`. Subcommands: `create`, `destroy`, `list`, `prune`, `run`, `enter`. (`endless sandbox <subcommand>` in Python dispatches to this.)

Core operations:
- `create` (internal, called by `run`/`enter`): provisions `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/<name>/`, writes `.sandbox-meta.json` (`created_at`, `mode`, `creator_pid`, `name`).
- `--clone`: deep-copies the user's DB to `<sandbox>/endless/endless.db` and walks the `projects` table. For each project, copies `<project_root>/.endless/` to `<sandbox>/projects/<project_name>/.endless/`; updates the sandbox DB's `projects.path` to point at the sandboxed location.
- Uses `os.OpenRoot(sandbox_dir)` for all file operations. Out-of-root writes return EACCES.

Cleanup:
- `run` registers a `defer` that destroys the sandbox if `--keep` not set.
- Crash-resistant cleanup: trap on EXIT/INT/TERM via signal handler.
- Crashed runs leave the sandbox; orphan detection in `list`/`prune` finds them via dead `creator_pid`.

### Phase 2 — Python CLI integration

New `src/endless/sandbox_cmd.py` with `endless sandbox` Click group. Subcommands shell out to `endless-sandbox` binary.

App-level guard in `src/endless/db.py`:
```python
def _enforce_sandbox_boundary(path: Path):
    sandbox = os.environ.get("ENDLESS_SANDBOX")
    if sandbox and not str(path).startswith(sandbox):
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"sandbox boundary violation: refusing to open {path} outside {sandbox}"
        )
```
Called from `get_db()` and any other path-opening site in Python.

### Phase 3 — Binary-selection helper (delegates with E-1039)

New `src/endless/binary_resolution.py` (or a module under existing structure):
```python
def resolve_endless_bin_dir() -> Path:
    """Return the bin/ containing endless-* binaries to use for subprocess calls.
    Detects worktree-local bin/, else falls back to first PATH match."""
```
Used by `endless sandbox run` to set PATH for the launched command. E-1039 (separate task) will refactor `event_bridge.emit_event` and any other `shutil.which("endless-event")` site to use this same helper.

### Phase 4 — pytest fixture and `Sandbox` class

New `src/endless/testing/sandbox.py`:
```python
class Sandbox:
    def __init__(self, *, clone: bool = False, name: str | None = None): ...
    def __enter__(self) -> "Sandbox": ...   # creates sandbox dir, sets env
    def __exit__(self, *exc): ...            # destroys (unless name set)
    def run(self, cmd: str, *, check: bool = True) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess: ...
    @property
    def db(self) -> sqlite3.Connection: ...  # connection to sandbox DB
    @property
    def env(self) -> dict[str, str]: ...     # env vars to pass to subprocesses
    @property
    def path(self) -> Path: ...              # sandbox dir
```

Update `tests/conftest.py`:
```python
@pytest.fixture
def sandbox(tmp_path):
    with Sandbox() as sb:
        yield sb
```

Migrate existing tests opportunistically (out of scope for this plan; file as separate cleanup task).

### Phase 5 — Startup orphan warning

Hook in `endless` CLI entry point (`src/endless/cli.py:main`). Reads sandbox dir, counts orphans (ephemerals whose `creator_pid` is dead). If >0, prints to stderr:
```
warning: 3 orphaned sandboxes (run 'endless sandbox prune' to clean,
or 'endless config set sandbox.startup_warning false' to silence).
```
Disabled via `endless config set sandbox.startup_warning false`.

### Phase 6 — Documentation

- `docs/sandbox.md`: usage examples (devs, agents, pytest).
- Update `CLAUDE.md` to direct sessions toward `endless sandbox` for any test/sanity work that mutates state.

## Critical files

**New:**
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/main.go` — Go binary (Phase 1)
- `src/endless/sandbox_cmd.py` — Python CLI integration (Phase 2)
- `src/endless/binary_resolution.py` — shared bin-dir helper (Phase 3)
- `src/endless/testing/sandbox.py` — Python `Sandbox` class for tests (Phase 4)
- `docs/sandbox.md` — user-facing docs (Phase 6)

**Modified:**
- `src/endless/db.py` — add `_enforce_sandbox_boundary` guard
- `src/endless/cli.py` — register sandbox group; add startup orphan warning
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — (later, via E-1039) use `resolve_endless_bin_dir` helper
- `tests/conftest.py` — add `sandbox` fixture
- `CLAUDE.md` — direct sessions to use `endless sandbox`

## Reusable existing helpers

- `monitor.ConfigDir()` / `monitor.DBPath()` (`internal/monitor/db.go`) — already XDG-aware; sandbox sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME to redirect.
- `config._config_root()` (`src/endless/config.py:14`, from E-1020) — Python equivalent.
- pytest `monkeypatch` fixture — used in conftest to set env without leaking across tests.
- `tests/conftest.py:isolated_env` — current partial isolation; will be deprecated once `sandbox` fixture lands.

## Decisions to record on plan exit

File via `endless decision add --decides E-995 "..."`:
- D1: Layered isolation model (env-var foundation + opt-in deep clone + opt-in OS-level guard).
- D2: Binary-selection helper shared between E-995 and E-1039 (delegation pattern, no duplication).
- D3: Always-on noisy startup warning when orphans exist (visibility > annoyance).
- D4: `prune` semantics — orphans only by default, no `--all`, 24h minimum on `--older-than`.
- D5: pytest fixture API shape — `Sandbox` object with `run`/`db`/`env`/`path` members.
- D6: `--clone` is deep (DB + per-project state + path rewrites); no `--clone-prod`, no other variants.
- D7: `enter` opens a subshell (not current-shell env modification).

## Tasks to file/update on plan exit

**New:**
- "Implement endless-sandbox Go binary (E-995 Phase 1)" — child of E-995
- "Wire endless sandbox CLI group in Python (E-995 Phase 2)" — child of E-995
- "Add binary-selection helper (E-995 Phase 3, shared with E-1039)" — child of E-995
- "Implement pytest Sandbox class and fixture (E-995 Phase 4)" — child of E-995
- "Add startup orphan warning (E-995 Phase 5)" — child of E-995
- "Document endless sandbox usage (E-995 Phase 6)" — child of E-995
- "Migrate existing pytest tests to sandbox fixture" — child of E-995, phase=later

**Update existing:**
- E-1039 (`Add worktree-local binary preference to endless CLI`) — note delegation to E-995 Phase 3's helper; E-1039 becomes "extend the helper's caller set to include event_bridge and other subprocess sites."
- E-1043, E-1044, E-1045, E-1046 — already children of E-995; map them to phases above and either close as covered-by-Phase-N, or keep as discoverability aids and link to the implementing phase task.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. **Unit isolation:** `endless sandbox run -- task add "Foo"` writes to sandbox DB; assert `~/.config/endless/endless.db` mtime unchanged via stat (still brittle — better via Phase 1's `os.Root` write-failure path).
2. **Deep-clone correctness:** `endless sandbox run --clone -- task list` shows the same tasks as `endless task list` (without sandbox) but all writes during the sandbox session land in sandbox locations.
3. **Os.Root enforcement:** test that any code path attempting to open a prod path during a sandbox run fails with EACCES at syscall level (not just app-level rejection).
4. **Crash inspection:** kill `endless sandbox run` mid-flight; `endless sandbox list` shows orphan; `endless sandbox enter <orphan>` re-attaches; `endless sandbox prune --older-than 0d` errors with `"minimum --older-than is 24h"`; default `prune` after 24h cleans it.
5. **Startup warning:** with N orphans, `endless task list` prints one-line warning; with `sandbox.startup_warning=false`, silent.
6. **pytest:** existing tests migrated to use `sandbox` fixture pass with no functional changes; `seeded_project_at_cwd` workaround can be removed.
7. **No prod leak:** wrap E-787's Phase 5 sanity script in `endless sandbox run --clone` and verify no writes to `~/.config/endless/endless.db` (via os.Root EACCES, not just mtime).

## Out of scope (file separately if needed)

- **Migrating all existing pytest tests** to the new fixture (gradual, opportunistic).
- **Web UI integration** — sandboxes are CLI/test-time concern for v1.
- **Multi-user shared sandboxes** — every sandbox is single-user.
- **In-memory SQLite provisioning mode** — disk-backed sandbox is fast enough; inspection of crashed ones requires disk anyway.
- **Network/process isolation** — only filesystem isolation in scope.
- **E-894** ("Move task display reads from Python to Go") — independent but synergistic; once it lands, the Python app-level guard in Phase 2 becomes vestigial.

## Notes for future planning

- E-1041 (rebuild-db FK failures from pre-E-808 legacy tasks) is independent of E-995 but commonly hit when sandboxes run rebuild-db against `--clone`d state. Worth scheduling near the same time so Phase 5/integration testing isn't blocked by FK noise.
- E-1042 (document `ENDLESS_AUTO_MIGRATE`) overlaps with Phase 6 documentation — fold into the same docs PR.
- E-1040 (block cross-session worktree migration from wiping uncommitted main) is unrelated but came from the same E-787 thread; mention in CLAUDE.md update if scope allows.

payload.title: Use layered isolation model for sandboxes
payload.description: Sandbox feature is composed of three optional layers: (#1) env-var foundation — XDG_CONFIG_HOME redirection — Go honors natively, Python gained support via E-1020. Always present. (#2) Opt-in deep clone (--clone flag) — copies user's DB plus per-project state with paths rewritten. For migration, integration, and sanity testing. (#3) Opt-in OS-level guard via Go 1.24's os.Root — kernel-level write barrier on prod paths. For paranoid runs. Layers compose; none replaces another. Rationale: each layer addresses a distinct concern (isolation mechanism, starting state, leak protection); collapsing them would either over-spec the cheap default or make the robust mode mandatory.

payload.title: Implement shared binary-selection helper for E-995 and E-1039
payload.description: Both E-995 (sandbox needs to invoke worktree-local Go binaries from a launched process) and E-1039 (general endless CLI needs to prefer worktree-local binaries) require the same binary-resolution logic: detect worktree-local bin/, fall back to PATH. Solution: implement once as src/endless/binary_resolution.py:resolve_endless_bin_dir(). E-995 uses it inside 'sandbox run' to set PATH. E-1039 later refactors event_bridge.emit_event and other shutil.which sites to use the same helper. Delegation pattern, not duplication. Confirmed during E-995 planning conversation: alternative orderings (E-1039 first, E-995 first, both at once) all yield the same end state with no conflict.

payload.title: Add always-on startup warning for orphaned sandboxes
payload.description: When orphaned sandboxes exist (ephemerals whose creator_pid is dead), 'endless' prints a one-line warning at startup with explicit disable instructions: 'warning: 3 orphaned sandboxes (run "endless sandbox prune" to clean, or "endless config set sandbox.startup_warning false" to silence).' Default ON. Rationale: silent options are invisible options — users who'd benefit from knowing about orphans never discover the feature exists. Visibility (and instructions to disable) > temporary annoyance. Pattern generalizable to other actionable warnings.

payload.title: Implement prune as orphans-only with 24h minimum on --older-than
payload.description: endless sandbox prune scope is orphaned ephemerals only. No --all flag — if user wants everything gone, 'rm -rf ~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/' is one command, not a CLI we should encourage. --older-than DURATION accepted, with 24h minimum enforced: any value below errors with 'minimum --older-than is 24h'. Rationale: a sandbox <24h old might still be 'in use' mentally even if process died (debugging, partial test runs). YAGNI on --all — the destructive sledgehammer is already available via shell. prune always outputs scope being addressed before acting, e.g.: 'Pruning 3 orphaned ephemeral sandboxes older than 24h: ...'.

payload.title: Use object-with-methods API for pytest sandbox fixture
payload.description: pytest 'sandbox' fixture yields a Sandbox object exposing four members: sb.run(cmd) — invoke endless commands; sb.db — sqlite3 connection to sandboxed DB for direct SELECT in test assertions; sb.env — dict of env vars (XDG_CONFIG_HOME, ENDLESS_SANDBOX, PATH) to pass to further subprocesses; sb.path — sandbox dir for inspecting files. Wrapped as a context manager so cleanup is automatic via pytest's tmp_path lifecycle. Rationale: object+methods is the Pythonic choice; far cleaner than a collection of disjointed helpers like _seed_project_at_cwd that E-787 had to invent ad-hoc. Mike confirmed preference for objects over inconsistently-named functions in Python.

payload.title: Implement --clone as deep clone of DB plus per-project state
payload.description: endless sandbox run --clone (and equivalent on enter/create) deep-copies the user's DB to <sandbox>/endless/endless.db AND walks the projects table, copying each project's <project_root>/.endless/ tree (events, plans, sessions) to <sandbox>/projects/<project_name>/.endless/, then rewrites projects.path rows in the sandboxed DB to point at the sandboxed locations. Cost: ~200-500MB and ~500ms-2s depending on event log sizes. No --clone-prod or other variants — single flag, clear semantics. Without --clone, sandbox starts empty (fresh schema, no projects). Rationale: per-project event logs are referenced from DB rows by absolute path; without rewriting them, events written during sandbox testing flow to the real project's event log — a leak. Safety justifies the cost.

payload.title: Use subshell for sandbox enter command
payload.description: endless sandbox enter <name> forks a subshell with the sandbox's env vars set (XDG_CONFIG_HOME, ENDLESS_SANDBOX, PATH). User runs commands in the subshell; 'exit' returns to the parent shell with original env restored. Alternative — modifying current shell's env via eval/source — was rejected: subshell is more robust (exit always works cleanly), avoids the trap of leaving stale env vars when something goes wrong. Speed cost (a few ms to spawn subshell) is negligible. Confirmed during E-995 planning conversation: 'Robustness > a few milliseconds faster.'

payload.title: Analysis field content rules and lifecycle
payload.description: Three rules govern the analysis field on tasks (extends E-1058's description policy and grounds E-1064's audit). (1) Analysis is pre-plan/pre-implementation design content only; once a task ships, the analysis is historical record. Post-shipping staleness is not a concern because the design is realised in code and plan files; auditing post-implementation analysis for currency is a category error. (2) Analysis must not include time-frozen specifics — exact byte counts, file sizes, line numbers, or sha hashes of moving artifacts. Such data has near-zero historical value and creates false-precision drift. Cite file or function names instead and let the reader re-locate at pickup. (3) When a task's plan file (--text/plan) fully documents the chosen design, analysis may be NULLed; redundant historical record is not the field's job.

fields.description: The tasks.text column is conventionally treated as 'the plan' (planned plan-bearing detector keys off its presence), but there is no field for problem analysis, design briefs, research notes, or decision rationale — content that is long-form but not a plan. The moment plan-bearing detection lands, anything stored in text gets interpreted as 'planned'. Options: rename text->plan and add a separate analysis field, overload description, or convention-only. Recommendation: rename + add field. Constraints from E-1064 audit and E-1073 decision: analysis is pre-plan design content only, no time-frozen specifics (bytes/line numbers), can be NULLed when the plan file fully documents the design. The new analysis column also needs a CLI surface (--analysis / --analysis-file on add/update; render in task show); the column added ahead of E-999 has no surface today.

payload.title: Implement endless-sandbox Go binary (E-995 Phase 1)
payload.description: New binary at cmd/endless-sandbox/main.go. Subcommands: create (internal), destroy, list, prune, run, enter. Provisions ~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/<name>/ with .sandbox-meta.json (created_at, mode, creator_pid, name). Uses Go 1.24's os.OpenRoot(sandbox_dir) for kernel-level write barrier — out-of-root operations return EACCES. Crash-resistant cleanup: defer + signal handler on EXIT/INT/TERM. Implements decisions E-1066, E-1068, E-1069, E-1072. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Apply E-1073 analysis content rules: NULL redundant entries and strip time-frozen specifics
payload.description: Apply the analysis content rules from E-1073 to items E-1064 flagged. (1) NULL the analysis field on E-958, E-987, and E-993 — each has a plan file (.endless/plans/E-NNN.md) that fully documents the resolved design, so the analysis is redundant historical record. (2) Strip time-frozen specifics from E-979 (event-file byte counts), E-994 (cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:538 line number), and E-1034 (line numbers in claude.go and several plan files) — keep file/function names, drop the numbers. Use raw SQL because the analysis column has no CLI surface yet (E-999 will fix that); same path Mike used to populate the column. Once landed, zero analysis fields remain in the buckets E-1064 flagged for refresh.

payload.title: Add endless sandbox CLI group and Python boundary guard (E-995 Phase 2)
payload.description: New src/endless/sandbox_cmd.py with Click group registered as 'endless sandbox'. Subcommands shell out to endless-sandbox Go binary. Also adds app-level guard in src/endless/db.py: _enforce_sandbox_boundary(path) checks ENDLESS_SANDBOX env var and refuses to open paths outside sandbox dir. Called from get_db() and any other path-opening site in Python. Becomes vestigial once E-894 moves all SQL to Go. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Add binary-selection helper (E-995 Phase 3, shared with E-1039)
payload.description: New src/endless/binary_resolution.py:resolve_endless_bin_dir() returns the bin/ containing endless-* binaries to use for subprocess calls. Detects worktree-local bin/, falls back to PATH first match. E-995 Phase 1 uses it inside 'sandbox run' to set PATH for the launched command. E-1039 will refactor event_bridge.emit_event and other shutil.which sites to use the same helper. Implements decision E-1067. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Implement pytest Sandbox class and fixture (E-995 Phase 4)
payload.description: New src/endless/testing/sandbox.py with Sandbox class (context manager): __init__(clone, name), __enter__/__exit__, run(cmd), and properties db/env/path. Wraps the same machinery as the CLI but callable from Python tests. Update tests/conftest.py with new 'sandbox' fixture using the class. Implements decision E-1070. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Add startup orphan warning (E-995 Phase 5)
payload.description: Hook in src/endless/cli.py:main entry point. Reads ~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/, counts orphans (ephemerals whose creator_pid is dead). If >0, prints to stderr: warning with disable instructions. Default ON. Disabled via 'endless config set sandbox.startup_warning false'. Implements decision E-1068. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Document endless sandbox usage (E-995 Phase 6)
payload.description: New docs/sandbox.md with usage examples for three audiences: developers (interactive sanity via 'sandbox enter'), agents (one-shot via 'sandbox run'), pytest (fixture pattern). Update CLAUDE.md to direct sessions toward 'endless sandbox' for any test/sanity work that mutates state. Fold E-1042 (document ENDLESS_AUTO_MIGRATE) into the same docs PR. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Migrate existing pytest tests to sandbox fixture
payload.description: Once E-995 Phase 4 lands, opportunistically migrate existing tests in tests/ to use the new 'sandbox' fixture instead of the older 'isolated_env' / 'seeded_project_at_cwd' pattern. Gradual — does not need to be done in one sweep. Once complete, the legacy fixtures can be removed from conftest.py. See E-995 plan.

payload.title: Fix typed-link display direction in task show output
payload.description: task show (and task show --llm) renders typed relations from the wrong perspective — the displayed label is the active-voice dep_type from STORAGE rather than the directional view from the displayed task's perspective. Concrete examples observed: 'task show E-787' renders 'blocks=E-1020' but actual relation is E-787 blocked_by E-1020 (storage: source=E-1020, target=E-787, dep_type=blocks). 'task show E-995' renders 'replaced_by=E-993' but actual relation is E-995 replaces E-993 (E-993 is the declined task; E-995 supersedes it). Both are inverted. Affected file: src/endless/task_cmd.py:detail_item — the relations group iteration uses the stored dep_type label, not the inverse-name lookup. E-957 Phase 4 plan addressed this with get_all_relations(item_id) and CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES bidirectional lookup; appears the fix landed for the relations grouping in some modes but not all. Verify against E-957 plan + check both --llm and human-readable modes.

fields.prompt:
# Task E-1074 — Implement endless-sandbox Go binary (E-995 Phase 1)

## Why you're here

You are starting Phase 1 of E-995, "Design sandbox/fixture pattern for tests that mutate state." The full plan and 7 locked decisions were written in a prior session. You are implementing the foundational Go binary that every other phase depends on.

**Read the plan first:** `endless task show E-995`

**Read the locked decisions:** E-1066 (layered isolation model), E-1068 (always-on startup warning — informs the orphan detection you build here), E-1069 (prune semantics — orphans only, 24h minimum on `--older-than`), E-1072 (subshell for `enter`). Run `endless decision show E-1066 E-1068 E-1069 E-1072` to read them all at once.

## Set up your worktree (manual)

This is a Go-side task; Mike wants close involvement on Go work. Run from the main checkout:

```bash
git -C /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless worktree add -b task/1074-sandbox-go-binary /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1074
cd /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1074
endless task start E-1074
endless task update E-1074 --status in_progress
```

Per `CLAUDE.md`: do NOT run `just install` from inside the worktree (would point the global Python tool at this worktree). Use `just build` for binaries; they land in `./bin/`.

## What you're building

A new Go binary at `cmd/endless-sandbox/main.go` exposing subcommands:

- `endless-sandbox run [--clone] [--name N] [--keep] -- <cmd>` — provision sandbox, exec command, auto-destroy unless `--keep`
- `endless-sandbox enter [--clone] <name>` — provision sandbox, fork a subshell with sandbox env active (`exit` returns to parent)
- `endless-sandbox list` — show all sandboxes, marking each Live / In-use / Orphaned
- `endless-sandbox prune [--older-than DURATION]` — destroy orphaned ephemerals; minimum `--older-than 24h`, error below
- `endless-sandbox destroy <name>` — explicit destroy of a named sandbox

**Storage:** `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/<name>/` (XDG_CACHE_HOME-aware; default to `$HOME/.cache/endless/sandboxes/` if unset).

**Metadata file** at `<sandbox>/.sandbox-meta.json` written at creation:
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-05-01T12:00:00Z",
  "mode": "ephemeral" | "keep",
  "creator_pid": 12345,
  "name": "abc123"
}
```

**OS-level guard via Go 1.24's `os.Root`:**
- After provisioning, open the sandbox dir with `os.OpenRoot(sandboxDir)`.
- All file operations the sandbox performs (writing meta, copying clone data, etc.) MUST go through the Root — methods like `root.Create`, `root.Open`. This makes any attempt to escape the sandbox dir (`..`, out-of-tree symlinks) fail at the syscall level with EACCES.

**Crash-resistant cleanup:**
- `run` registers a `defer` to destroy the sandbox unless `--keep`.
- Install signal handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP that triggers the same cleanup before exit.
- If the process is SIGKILL'd or crashes hard, the sandbox stays on disk — that's by design (orphan inspection per E-1068/E-1069).

**Orphan detection** (used by `list` and `prune`):
- Walk `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/`, read each `.sandbox-meta.json`.
- For ephemerals, check if `creator_pid` is still alive (`syscall.Kill(pid, 0)` returns nil if alive, ESRCH if dead).
- Named/`--keep` sandboxes are never marked orphaned regardless of pid state.

**Output expectations:**
- `prune` outputs scope before acting: `"Pruning 3 orphaned ephemeral sandboxes older than 24h: foo bar baz"` then `"Pruned: foo bar baz"`.
- `prune --older-than 0d` (or any value <24h) errors: `"minimum --older-than is 24h"`.
- `list` shows a table: `NAME  MODE  STATE  AGE  SIZE  CREATOR_PID`.

## What this binary does NOT do

- It does NOT do `--clone`'s deep-copy logic — that's a separate sub-task within this phase. Implement the binary's CLI surface and provisioning machinery; have `--clone` accept the flag and either no-op or call into a stub for now. The deep clone is described in decision E-1071 and may be lifted into a separate task during your work — use your judgment.
- It does NOT add the startup orphan warning (Phase 5, E-1079). Just expose `list` so that warning has something to call.
- It does NOT add the binary-selection helper (Phase 3, E-1077). The `run` subcommand will need to know which `endless-event` to subprocess to eventually, but for now use `os.Getenv("PATH")` lookup like everything else.

## Files to read for context

- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — example of subcommand-based Go CLI in this project (your structure should mirror it)
- `internal/monitor/db.go` — has `ConfigDir()` honoring XDG_CONFIG_HOME (you'll write a parallel `CacheDir()` honoring XDG_CACHE_HOME)
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — the canonical schema (so you know what to copy when `--clone` lands later)
- `Justfile` — how `just build` adds new binaries (you'll add a `go build -o bin/endless-sandbox ./cmd/endless-sandbox` line)
- `CLAUDE.md` — project rules

## Verification

1. `just build` — your new binary lands in `./bin/endless-sandbox`
2. `just test` — existing tests still pass (you haven't touched any tested code paths)
3. Manual smoke (run from worktree, NOT after `just install`):
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox run -- echo hi` → creates sandbox, runs, destroys; `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/` is empty after
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox run --keep --name test1 -- echo hi` → sandbox persists; `endless-sandbox list` shows it
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox prune` → does nothing (no orphans)
   - Kill a long-running `run`: `./bin/endless-sandbox run -- sleep 60 &` then `kill -9 $!` → orphan appears in `list`
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox prune --older-than 0s` → errors with "minimum --older-than is 24h"
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox destroy test1` → removes named sandbox
4. **`os.Root` enforcement test:** in a quick Go test or inline, attempt to write outside the sandbox dir via the Root — assert it fails with EACCES.

## When done

- `just build && just test` must both pass cleanly
- Set status to `verify` (per CLAUDE.md, NEVER `confirmed`): `endless task update E-1074 --status verify`
- Commit on your worktree branch (`task/1074-sandbox-go-binary`); do not merge to main yourself
- Report back what was built and any deviations from the plan

## Things Mike emphasized in the planning conversation

- He'll be hands-on for Go work — don't make architecture-level decisions silently; surface them
- Robustness > a few ms of speed
- Visibility > temporary annoyance (relevant when the orphan-detection feeds Phase 5's warning)
- File don't flag — if you discover a bug or out-of-scope concern, `endless task add` it, don't just mention it

- `endless-sandbox prune [--older-than DURATION]` — destroy orphaned ephemerals; minimum `--older-than 24h`, error below
- `endless-sandbox destroy <name>` — explicit destroy of a named sandbox

**Storage:** `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/<name>/` (XDG_CACHE_HOME-aware; default to `$HOME/.cache/endless/sandboxes/` if unset).

**Metadata file** at `<sandbox>/.sandbox-meta.json` written at creation:
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-05-01T12:00:00Z",
  "mode": "ephemeral" | "keep",
  "creator_pid": 12345,
  "name": "abc123"
}
```

**OS-level guard via Go 1.24's `os.Root`:**
- After provisioning, open the sandbox dir with `os.OpenRoot(sandboxDir)`.
- All file operations the sandbox performs (writing meta, copying clone data, etc.) MUST go through the Root — methods like `root.Create`, `root.Open`. This makes any attempt to escape the sandbox dir (`..`, out-of-tree symlinks) fail at the syscall level with EACCES.

**Crash-resistant cleanup:**
- `run` registers a `defer` to destroy the sandbox unless `--keep`.
- Install signal handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP that triggers the same cleanup before exit.
- If the process is SIGKILL'd or crashes hard, the sandbox stays on disk — that's by design (orphan inspection per E-1068/E-1069).

**Orphan detection** (used by `list` and `prune`):
- Walk `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/`, read each `.sandbox-meta.json`.
- For ephemerals, check if `creator_pid` is still alive (`syscall.Kill(pid, 0)` returns nil if alive, ESRCH if dead).
- Named/`--keep` sandboxes are never marked orphaned regardless of pid state.

**Output expectations:**
- `prune` outputs scope before acting: `"Pruning 3 orphaned ephemeral sandboxes older than 24h: foo bar baz"` then `"Pruned: foo bar baz"`.
- `prune --older-than 0d` (or any value <24h) errors: `"minimum --older-than is 24h"`.
- `list` shows a table: `NAME  MODE  STATE  AGE  SIZE  CREATOR_PID`.

## What this binary does NOT do

- It does NOT do `--clone`'s deep-copy logic — that's a separate sub-task within this phase. Implement the binary's CLI surface and provisioning machinery; have `--clone` accept the flag and either no-op or call into a stub for now. The deep clone is described in decision E-1071 and may be lifted into a separate task during your work — use your judgment.
- It does NOT add the startup orphan warning (Phase 5, E-1079). Just expose `list` so that warning has something to call.
- It does NOT add the binary-selection helper (Phase 3, E-1077). The `run` subcommand will need to know which `endless-event` to subprocess to eventually, but for now use `os.Getenv("PATH")` lookup like everything else.

## Files to read for context

- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — example of subcommand-based Go CLI in this project (your structure should mirror it)
- `internal/monitor/db.go` — has `ConfigDir()` honoring XDG_CONFIG_HOME (you'll write a parallel `CacheDir()` honoring XDG_CACHE_HOME)
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — the canonical schema (so you know what to copy when `--clone` lands later)
- `Justfile` — how `just build` adds new binaries (you'll add a `go build -o bin/endless-sandbox ./cmd/endless-sandbox` line)
- `CLAUDE.md` — project rules

## Verification

1. `just build` — your new binary lands in `./bin/endless-sandbox`
2. `just test` — existing tests still pass (you haven't touched any tested code paths)
3. Manual smoke (run from worktree, NOT after `just install`):
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox run -- echo hi` → creates sandbox, runs, destroys; `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/` is empty after
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox run --keep --name test1 -- echo hi` → sandbox persists; `endless-sandbox list` shows it
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox prune` → does nothing (no orphans)
   - Kill a long-running `run`: `./bin/endless-sandbox run -- sleep 60 &` then `kill -9 $!` → orphan appears in `list`
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox prune --older-than 0s` → errors with "minimum --older-than is 24h"
   - `./bin/endless-sandbox destroy test1` → removes named sandbox
4. **`os.Root` enforcement test:** in a quick Go test or inline, attempt to write outside the sandbox dir via the Root — assert it fails with EACCES.

## When done

- `just build && just test` must both pass cleanly
- Set status to `verify` (per CLAUDE.md, NEVER `confirmed`): `endless task update E-1074 --status verify`
- Commit on your worktree branch (`task/1074-sandbox-go-binary`); do not merge to main yourself
- Report back what was built and any deviations from the plan

## Things Mike emphasized in the planning conversation

- Robustness > a few ms of speed
- Visibility > temporary annoyance (relevant when the orphan-detection feeds Phase 5's warning)
- File don't flag — if you discover a bug or out-of-scope concern, `endless task add` it, don't just mention it

payload.title: Design a strategy to propagate session-learned rules into the endless repo in parallel with memory
payload.description:
Per-session AI memory (~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md and friends) only helps the same Claude instance with that memory directory. It does not reach: other Claude sessions on other machines, other AI harnesses (Codex, etc.), human users of endless-the-product, or future fresh sessions that haven't accumulated those rules.

Yet many learned rules are project-level — they describe how endless's workflow is meant to work, not personal user preferences. Examples surfaced in 2026-05-01 conversation:
- 'Long-form task content goes in .endless/plans/E-NNN.md attached via --text; description stays a 2-3 sentence pitch.'
- 'The session that creates a plan file owns committing it.'

Both are about endless's plan workflow. They should reach every user, not just one Claude instance.

Open question: project-specific feature, or general 'lessons' infrastructure all endless-managed projects could use? (User's global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md already has a pattern: 'After ANY correction from the user: update ~/.claude/LESSONS.md with the pattern.' That's user-scoped. Project-scoped equivalent would be e.g. .endless/lessons/ or project-LESSONS.md.)

Considerations / things to weigh:
- Where do learnings live? .endless/learnings/<topic>.md? .endless/conventions.md? Inline in quick-start guide? Repo-level CLAUDE.md? Multiple?
- Mike's pushback (2026-05-01): 'CLAUDE.md does not work because everyone's CLAUDE.md may be different.' Repo-rooted CLAUDE.md is shared, but users may override or omit it; can't rely on it as authoritative.
- Auto-propagation vs. manual: should there be tooling (e.g. 'endless lesson add') that captures a rule and writes it to the repo? Or just a convention agents follow?
- How does an agent/user discover existing lessons? Surface in 'endless quick-start'? A new 'endless lessons' command?
- Distinguish project-level rules (apply to all who work on this project) from personal rules (apply to this user's preferences). The latter still belongs in personal memory only.
- Migration path: rules already in personal memory that should also be in the repo — how do they get there?

Origin: 2026-05-01 conversation. Mike: 'create a task for another session to work on that come up with a strategy to write learnings to the endless repo in parallel with memory. This may be unique to the endless project itself, or maybe it is a general lessons feature that all projects could use.'

Out of scope for this design task:
- Implementing the chosen strategy (file follow-up once design lands).
- Migrating existing personal-memory rules into the repo (separate task).

payload.title: Add SessionStart/PreToolUse/SessionEnd hooks for worktree adoption
payload.description: Layer D of E-971: worktree lifecycle hooks. Three handlers in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go (SessionStart claim/adopt/create, PreToolUse worktree-task match enforcement, SessionEnd lock release) plus lock-file primitives in internal/monitor/. See attached prompt for full scope and acceptance criteria; full design in .endless/plans/E-971.md.

fields.prompt:
# E-971 Layer D — Worktree adoption hooks

You are picking up a focused slice of E-971 (parallel-session support via git worktrees in endless). Read the parent design at `.endless/plans/E-971.md` for full context. This prompt is the short brief; the plan file is the source of truth.

## Context: what is already shipped

- **Foundation slice (commit `3954f9a`)**: `endless worktree {list, current, show, for-task}` — read-only inspection.
- **Mutation slice (most recent commit on main)**: `endless worktree {land, drop}`. Land implements E-987's auto-commit + rebase + ff-merge + retry algorithm. Drop refuses dirty/foreign without `--force`.
- **E-969**: plan snapshots + per-task plan-file mirror.
- **E-970**: matchers in config files (verbs, pivots, action regexes) at `.endless/config.json` and `~/.config/endless/config.json`.
- **E-972**: `event_bridge.emit_event` correctly routes events to the registered project root from any worktree cwd.
- **E-1004**: `_project_root_for_task` is cwd-aware so per-task plan files land in the worktree.
- **E-1012**: PreToolUse hook blocks Claude bash `git commit` on main checkout.
- **E-1017 (decision, confirmed)**: endless historical files (events log, plan snapshots) are write-once-by-endless; hand-editing forbidden.

## Your scope: Layer D — worktree lifecycle hooks

Three hook handlers in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` plus the lock-file primitives. **Strictly this slice** — do NOT also do Layer E (UserPromptSubmit gate) or Layer F (task-command integration). Those will be spawned separately.

### 1. SessionStart hook: claim, adopt, or create

When a Claude session starts, the hook fires. Currently it calls `monitor.InitSession` and `monitor.SetProcess`. Add the worktree adoption logic:

- Walk up from `payload.CWD` looking for `.endless/worktree.json` (a worktree's companion file).
- **If found and lock missing or stale**: atomically claim via `O_EXCL` create on `.endless/worktree.lock`. Auto-adopt. The cd-into-worktree IS the deliberate act; no separate verb needed.
- **If found and lock owned by a live session** (PID still running per `kill -0 <pid>` style check): refuse to register the new session. Return an error message via the hook response telling the user to use a different worktree or wait until it releases.
- **If not found** (cwd is main): create a per-session worktree.
  - Compute branch name `session/<short-id>` where short-id is e.g. first 8 chars of session_id.
  - `git worktree add -b session/<short-id> .endless/worktrees/session-<short-id> main`.
  - Write companion file `.endless/worktree.json` inside the new worktree with `{flavor: "session", session_id, base_branch: "main", branch: "session/<short-id>", created_at}`.
  - Claim the lock.
  - Return a hook response telling the user to `cd` into the new worktree before any edits.

### 2. PreToolUse hook: enforce worktree↔task match

Extend the existing handler. Currently E-1012 blocks `git commit` on main. Add:

- For Write/Edit tools (and any other file-writing tool already in `writeTools`):
  - Read cwd's worktree (via the same walk-up logic).
  - If no worktree found AND cwd is main: refuse with "Edits in main are not allowed; cd into a worktree first" message.
  - If worktree found AND its companion has a `task_id` AND the session's active task differs: refuse with "this worktree is for E-XXX, not your active task; switch sessions or change active task" message.
  - Otherwise allow.

### 3. SessionEnd hook: release lock

Currently SessionEnd parses transcript and removes the session companion file. Add:

- If the session held a worktree lock, delete the lock file (`.endless/worktree.lock`).
- Do NOT delete the worktree itself; only the lock. The worktree persists until landed/dropped.

### 4. Lock-file primitives in `internal/monitor/`

New file: `internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go` (or fold into existing companion.go if appropriate). Implement:

- `ClaimWorktreeLock(worktreePath, ownerSessionID, ownerPID, ownerTmuxPane string) error` — atomic `O_EXCL` create. Returns error if already exists.
- `ReadWorktreeLock(worktreePath string) (*WorktreeLock, error)` — read JSON, return parsed struct.
- `IsWorktreeLockStale(lock *WorktreeLock) bool` — `kill -0 <pid>` check.
- `ReleaseWorktreeLock(worktreePath string) error` — delete file. Idempotent.
- Lock file path: `<worktree-root>/.endless/worktree.lock`.
- Lock content: JSON `{session_id, pid, tmux_pane, claimed_at}`.

Companion file format (already documented in plan, recapping):
```json
{
  "task_id": "E-808",
  "base_branch": "main",
  "branch": "task/808-event-logs-authoritative",
  "created_at": "2026-04-28T15:30:00",
  "flavor": "task"
}
```

For session-flavor: `{flavor: "session", session_id: "...", base_branch: "main", branch: "session/<short-id>", created_at: "..."}`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Start a session in main → SessionStart creates `.endless/worktrees/session-<short-id>` with branch + companion + lock; hook response tells user to cd there.
- Start a session in an unowned worktree → SessionStart claims the lock; can edit normally.
- Start a session in a worktree owned by another live session → refused with clear message.
- Start a session in a worktree whose lock has a stale PID → claims it (treats stale as unowned).
- From main, attempt a Write to any source file → blocked with "edit from a worktree" message.
- From a worktree owned by this session, Write succeeds.
- SessionEnd → lock file deleted; worktree persists.
- Verify: existing tests still pass; existing hook behaviors (drift detection, task-active enforcement, snapshot capture) still work.

## Constraints and conventions

- **Companion file** terminology, not "sidecar" (per the established convention in `internal/monitor/companion.go` for sessions). The worktree companion file is at `<worktree>/.endless/worktree.json`.
- **Naming**: snake_case for code/DB identifiers, kebab-case for user-facing strings, Go uses PascalCase/camelCase.
- **Discipline**: do this work in a worktree (you'll be in one — your spawn session should be in one). When done, ship via `endless worktree land <task-id>` instead of manual git ops. That's the dogfood test.
- **Use just install** (NOT `uv tool install -e . --force` directly) to refresh the toolchain after landing — only run from main checkout, never from a worktree.
- **Don't run `just install` from your worktree**; it would hijack the global install for other sessions. If you need to test your in-progress changes, use `PYTHONPATH=<worktree>/src endless ...`.

## Plan file pointer

Read `.endless/plans/E-971.md` for the full E-971 design, particularly the "Hook integration summary" and "Ownership model" sections.

## When done

Mark the task `verify` (not `confirmed`; Mike confirms). Use `endless worktree land <task-id>` to ship.

payload.title: Design endless's strategy for co-developed third-party dependencies across worktrees
payload.description: How does an endless-managed project handle dependencies being co-developed alongside it, across worktrees, language-agnostic, without per-machine fragility? E-996 is the narrow Go-specific fix (go.work); this task designs the general pattern. See plan for option analysis and cross-cutting concerns.

fields.text:
# E-1085 — Strategy for co-developed third-party dependencies across worktrees

## Context

The `replace github.com/mikeschinkel/X => ../go-pkgs/X` pattern in endless's own `go.mod` is a specific instance of a general problem: how does an endless-managed project handle dependencies that are being co-developed alongside it, across worktrees, in a way that is language-agnostic and not per-developer fragile?

E-996 is the narrow Go-specific fix (`go.work`). It works today but doesn't generalize. Other languages have analogous mechanisms with the same pain points.

## Examples this needs to cover

- **Go**: `replace` directives, `go.work` overrides.
- **Python**: `pip install -e ../my-package`, `uv pip install -e`.
- **JS / TS**: `npm link`, `workspaces:` with `file:` deps, pnpm workspaces.
- **Rust**: `[patch.crates-io]`, `path` deps in `Cargo.toml`.

Today the workaround is a manual symlink at `.endless/worktrees/go-pkgs -> ~/Projects/go-pkgs` re-created per machine.

## Mike's design ideas (2026-05-01)

### Option 1 — Restructure repo root

Move the project into a subdir (`repo/`, `root/`, or similar) with a sibling `vendor/` (general-purpose, not Go-vendor) at the same level. Worktrees of the project would inherit the same shape; vendor is shared across worktrees.

- **Pro**: clean separation, single vendor location.
- **Con**: highly disruptive — every existing checkout moves, every path changes.

### Option 2 — Per-worktree gitignored deps dir

Place each dep at `<worktree>/.endless/deps/<dep>/`, materialized as a clone, symlink, or git worktree. Per-worktree, language-agnostic, gitignored so it doesn't enter commits.

(Mike's original phrasing was `.git/info/endless/worktrees/<dep>` for the same intent. That doesn't quite work as a literal path — `.git` is a file in worktrees, not a directory; `info/` resolves through git tooling. The corrected version uses a real filesystem path inside the worktree.)

- **Pro**: real filesystem path; language-agnostic; per-worktree isolation; auto-cleaned when worktree drops.
- **Con**: each worktree needs its own materialization step; storage multiplied.

### Option 3 — Per-worktree git worktrees of the deps

Combine (2) with `git worktree add` on the dep itself. Each parent worktree gets a corresponding dep worktree, allowing parallel dev on the dep too.

- **Pro**: parallel-dev capability on deps.
- **Con**: heaviest; most moving parts; cleanup correctness matters.

### Option 4 — Stay with per-machine symlink

Keep the current pattern; document carefully.

- **Pro**: zero design work.
- **Con**: per-machine fragility; new contributors hit it; doesn't generalize.

## Cross-cutting concerns

- **Registration**: endless needs to know which deps the project tracks. Probably extends `.endless/config.json` with a `deps` section.
- **Materialization mode**: per-dep choice of clone / symlink / git-worktree. User picks.
- **Cleanup**: dep dirs go with their parent worktree (gitignored); dep removal prunes everywhere.
- **Language-specific glue**: each dep needs "how to wire into the language's build system" — `go.work` entry, `npm link`, `pip install -e`, etc. This should be pluggable, like `.endless/extensions/use.sh` (E-1014).
- **Discovery / CLI surface**: `endless dep list`, `endless dep add <repo> --as <name>`, `endless dep materialize`, etc.

## Connections

- **E-996** is the narrow Go-only stopgap. Whichever strategy lands here either subsumes or coexists with it.
- **E-971** (worktrees + behavioral gate): worktree creation is the natural moment to materialize per-worktree deps. This task informs E-971's worktree-create flow.
- **E-1083** (memory-to-repo strategy): both are about endless-as-a-product needing to communicate conventions to users. Dep-handling IS one of those conventions.

## Out of scope

- Implementing the chosen approach. File a follow-up once design lands.
- Migrating endless's own `go-pkgs` setup beyond E-996.

## Origin

2026-05-01 conversation. Mike: *"I think that you have identified something we should standardize — how we handle 3rd party code that we are developing alongside what is in the current worktree."*

payload.title: Add per-project worktree-creation extension hook so users wire up their own build/dep glue
payload.description: E-996 fixed endless's own go-pkgs problem with a 'just go-work-init' recipe — dev-only, dogfooding. Users of endless-the-product on other projects hit analogous needs (go.work, npm link, nvm, pip install -e, cargo patch) but have no equivalent. Add an extension hook (e.g. .endless/extensions/worktree-init.sh) that endless invokes after creating a worktree, mirroring the .endless/extensions/use.sh pattern from E-1014. Each user's project supplies whatever shell glue their stack requires. Coordinates with E-1085 (broader dep-handling strategy) and E-971 (worktree creation flow).

fields.prompt:
You are a spawned Claude session working on **E-994** in the `endless` project.

Your goal: audit `snapshotPlanFile` in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` against the fatal-vs-non-fatal error-handling criteria we established, and either (a) document why non-fatal is justified or (b) convert it to fatal-on-error. One commit, small surface, well-scoped.

## Context

`snapshotPlanFile` currently swallows errors with `log.Printf` and returns `nil`. It's called from the `PostToolUse / Write` branch of the Claude hook to snapshot plan-file content into `.endless/plans/snapshots/`.

The fatal-vs-non-fatal criteria, established during E-989/E-1011 work:

**Non-fatal is justified ONLY when:**
1. The operation is opportunistic / best-effort by design (e.g., diagnostics, telemetry, snapshots that aren't required for correctness).
2. Failure is self-healing on a later event (e.g., `BackfillProcess` retries on the next `UserPromptSubmit`).
3. The error rate is high under normal operation and would be noisy if surfaced.

For comparison: `InitSession`, `SetProcess`, `WriteCompanion` are all fatal — silent failure means downstream features mysteriously break.

## What to do

1. `endless task start E-994` to register the session.
2. Read `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — find `snapshotPlanFile` (around line 538) and study its callers, what it produces, and what depends on the snapshots.
3. Apply the criteria. Decide: keep non-fatal (document why) or convert to fatal.
4. Implement the decision. If keeping non-fatal: add a `// Why non-fatal: ...` comment citing the criterion. If converting: change `log.Printf` to a returned wrapped error and propagate it up through the hook.
5. If you discover other `log.Printf("...: %v", err)` sites in the hook that fail the criteria, do NOT sweep them — file follow-up tasks. The audit for this task is scoped to `snapshotPlanFile` only.

## Constraints / conventions

- Read the project's `CLAUDE.md` first (it has worktree, install, and worktree-build setup notes — including E-996's `just go-work-init` for Go builds inside a worktree).
- Work in a git worktree under `.endless/worktrees/e-994/` on branch `e-994-snapshot-error-policy`. Set up with `git worktree add -b e-994-snapshot-error-policy .endless/worktrees/e-994 main` and `cd` there. Run `just go-work-init` if Go builds error on `replace` paths.
- Tests: `go test ./internal/monitor/... && go test ./cmd/endless-hook/...` (whichever apply).
- Commit message ends with the standard `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` line.
- When done: set status to `verify` (NOT completed). Mike marks `assumed` after he's seen it.
- Plan file: this task is small enough that a plan file is not required. If your audit produces design content beyond ~3 paragraphs, write `.endless/plans/E-994.md` and attach via `endless task update E-994 --text <path>` AND commit the plan file in the same commit (per project rule: the session that creates a plan file owns committing it).

## Status flow when finished

1. Commit on the worktree branch with a message like `"Audit snapshotPlanFile error policy (E-994)"`.
2. Land via `git -C <main> merge --ff-only e-994-snapshot-error-policy && git -C <main> worktree remove .endless/worktrees/e-994 && git -C <main> branch -d e-994-snapshot-error-policy` (rebase first if main has diverged).
3. From main, run `just install` to refresh the live binary.
4. `endless task update E-994 --status verify`.
5. Report back what you decided and why.

## Out of scope

- Sweeping other `log.Printf("...: %v", err)` sites — file follow-ups, don't fix them here.
- Refactoring `snapshotPlanFile`'s functionality. This is an error-policy audit, not a rewrite.
- Removing the function or changing what it produces.

fields.text:
# E-1074 — Implement `endless-sandbox` Go binary (E-995 Phase 1)

## Context

E-995 establishes a first-class sandbox/fixture mechanism so that tests and verification work that mutate Endless state run against an isolated DB + event log, never the live system. Today there is no such primitive — every session reinvents one (E-787 Phase 5 used `.test-isolated/`, conftest.py uses ad-hoc XDG redirection, and the lack of a sandbox tempted raw-SQL bypass during E-979 cleanup, declined as E-993). E-995's plan is locked, with 7 confirmed decisions (E-1066, E-1068, E-1069, E-1070, E-1071, E-1072, plus storage layout in the plan body).

This task is Phase 1 of E-995 — the foundational Go binary that every other phase depends on. It exposes the sandbox CLI surface, owns provisioning + cleanup + orphan detection, and uses Go 1.26's `os.Root` for kernel-level write barrier on the sandbox dir. Deep clone (decision E-1071) is **deferred to a follow-up child task**: the binary accepts `--clone` and warns "not yet implemented", proceeding with an empty sandbox. This keeps Phase 1 focused on the CLI + lifecycle plumbing every later phase calls into.

## Worktree setup (auto-mode will run these on plan approval)

Work happens on a dedicated worktree per E-1074:

```bash
git -C /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless worktree add -b task/1074-sandbox-go-binary \
    /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1074
cd /Users/mikeschinkel/Projects/endless/.endless/worktrees/e-1074
just go-work-init
```

`go.mod` has `replace ../go-pkgs/X` directives that only resolve from the main checkout. `just go-work-init` writes a per-checkout `go.work` (gitignored) with absolute paths so Go builds work inside the worktree. Without this, `just build` fails immediately. Broader strategy is open as E-1085.

`endless task start E-1074` and the `in_progress` status flip already ran on the main checkout per the user prompt — re-run inside the worktree if session registration is needed.

**Do NOT run `just install` from the worktree.** Editable `uv tool install -e .` would point the global Python tool at this worktree's source, which disappears when the worktree is dropped. Use `just build` only; binaries land in `./bin/` and are invoked via the explicit `./bin/endless-sandbox` path.

## Files to create

All paths relative to the worktree root:

- `cmd/endless-sandbox/main.go` — dispatcher: parse `os.Args[1]`, route to subcommand handlers, exit codes
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/sandbox.go` — `Sandbox` type, meta JSON read/write, provisioning, destroy, name generation, `os.Root` open
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/run.go` — `run` subcommand: provision → exec via `os/exec` → defer destroy → SIGINT/TERM/HUP handler triggers same cleanup
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/enter.go` — `enter` subcommand: provision-or-load named sandbox, fork `$SHELL` subshell with sandbox env (XDG_CONFIG_HOME, ENDLESS_SANDBOX, PATH preserved)
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/list.go` — walk cache dir, parse each meta, classify Live / In-use / Orphaned, render table
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/prune.go` — orphans-only filter, `--older-than DURATION` with 24h minimum (error below), print scope before acting
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/destroy.go` — explicit destroy by name (lookup meta, RemoveAll dir)
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/orphan.go` — `isAlive(pid)` via `syscall.Kill(pid, 0)` (ESRCH → dead, EPERM → alive); orphan classification helper shared by `list` and `prune`
- `cmd/endless-sandbox/sandbox_test.go` — table test proving `os.Root` blocks out-of-sandbox writes with EACCES

## Files to modify

- `internal/monitor/db.go` — add `CacheDir() string` parallel to existing `ConfigDir()`. Returns `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/endless` if set, else `$HOME/.cache/endless`. Used by `endless-sandbox` to compute `<CacheDir>/sandboxes/<name>/`. Reusing `ConfigDir`'s pattern keeps the helper next to its sibling.
- `Justfile` — add `go build -o bin/endless-sandbox ./cmd/endless-sandbox` to `build:`, `go:`, and the symlink line in `install:`. Three places, matching existing binaries.

## Reusable helpers (already exist — do not reinvent)

- `monitor.ConfigDir()` (`internal/monitor/db.go:21`) — XDG-aware config dir; pattern to mirror for the new `CacheDir()`.
- Signal handling pattern in `cmd/endless-channel/main.go:142-144` — `signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGINT)` is the in-repo precedent; extend to add SIGHUP.
- `flag.NewFlagSet(name, flag.ExitOnError)` subcommand pattern from `cmd/endless-event/main.go:37-50` — copy this dispatch shape.

## CLI surface (final)

```
endless-sandbox run    [--clone] [--name N] [--keep] -- <cmd> [args...]
endless-sandbox enter  [--clone] <name>
endless-sandbox list
endless-sandbox prune  [--older-than DURATION]
endless-sandbox destroy <name>
```

Storage: `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/<name>/` (XDG_CACHE_HOME-aware).

Meta file at `<sandbox>/.sandbox-meta.json`:
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-05-01T12:00:00Z",
  "mode": "ephemeral" | "keep",
  "creator_pid": 12345,
  "name": "abc123ef"
}
```

### Behavioral decisions (judgment calls inside the prompt's latitude)

- **`--clone` is deferred.** Flag is accepted by `run` and `enter` but logs `"warning: --clone deep-copy not yet implemented (see E-NEW); sandbox starts empty"` and proceeds. A child task `Implement --clone deep-copy for endless-sandbox` will be filed via `endless task add` on plan approval (parent E-995, references E-1071). Phase 1 does not block on the deep-copy implementation.
- **`enter` always provisions as `mode=keep`.** Entering a sandbox implies a session you might exit and return to; ephemeral semantics don't fit. To dispose, use `destroy <name>` explicitly.
- **`run --name foo` collisions error.** If `foo` already exists, error with `"sandbox 'foo' already exists; use 'endless-sandbox destroy foo' first"`. Force-overwrite is not in scope.
